× We can start as long as, if everyone is here. 错因：不要把as long as和if杂糅成一个结构。改：We can start as long as everyone is here.

contrasts.title: vs if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整从句，但if只是一般“如果”，as long as更强调“只要这个条件满足就行”。语气上as long as更像规则或许可。对比：If it rains, we’ll stay home. 只是可能条件；As long as it doesn’t rain, we’ll go. 强调满足条件即可。

contrasts.title: vs provided that
contrasts.note: 结构相近，都可引条件从句。provided that更正式、更书面，常见于合同、通知；as long as更自然、更口语。对比：You may leave early as long as you finish this. / You may leave early provided that you finish this.

contrasts.title: vs unless
contrasts.note: unless表示“除非不……否则……”，通常带否定逻辑；as long as表示“只要……就……”，是正向条件。场景上unless常用于警告，as long as常用于许可。对比：Unless you hurry, you’ll miss it. / As long as you hurry, you’ll catch it.

contrasts.title: vs time meaning of as long as
contrasts.note: 同样的词组还可表示时间“长达……；和……一样长”。结构上时间义常接时间比较，不是条件。场景上别把条件义和时间义混用。对比：As long as you call me, I’m happy. 条件；The rope is as long as the table. 长度比较。

contrasts.title: vs only if
contrasts.note: 两者都能表达限制，但only if更硬、更排他，强调“只有在这一种情况下”；as long as较宽松，意思像“满足这个条件就可以”。对比：You can go only if Dad says yes. 更严格；You can go as long as you’re back by ten. 像家规。

examples.sentence: As long as you study every day, you will improve.
examples.translation: 只要你每天学习，你就会进步。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：条件从句用一般现在时，主句用will。

examples.sentence: You can use my laptop as long as you’re careful.
examples.translation: 只要你小心一点，你就可以用我的笔记本电脑。
examples.note: 后置时更像补充限制条件，口语很自然。

examples.sentence: As long as all documents are submitted by Friday, your application will be processed.
examples.translation: 只要所有文件在周五前提交，你的申请就会被处理。
examples.note: 较偏书面，常见于说明、通知、流程。

examples.sentence: I’ll go if she invites me, but I’ll stay longer as long as everyone is having fun.
examples.translation: 如果她邀请我，我会去；但只要大家都玩得开心，我就会多待一会儿。
examples.note: 同句对比if与as long as：前者是是否发生，后者是持续条件。

examples.sentence: As long as you’re home by ten, you can go to the party.
examples.translation: 只要你十点前回家，你就可以去参加聚会。
examples.note: 很典型的许可/家规场景。

examples.sentence: We can keep the meeting short as long as everyone stays focused.
examples.translation: 只要大家保持专注，我们就能把会议开得很简短。
examples.note: 主句用can表示可行性，也很常见。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: [condition]放完整从句，优先用一般现在时，如 you finish your homework
[main clause]放结果或允许，优先用 will/can + 动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: As long as you finish your homework, you can watch TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要你完成作业，你就可以看电视。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
slotHints: [Main clause]先说决定、允许、计划，如 We can start now
[condition]后补限制条件，如 everyone is ready
templates.exampleSentence: We can start now as long as everyone is ready.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要大家都准备好了，我们现在就可以开始。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
slotHints: [condition]可用较正式内容，如 all payments are received
[formal main clause]可用被动或正式表达，如 the order will be shipped
templates.exampleSentence: As long as payment is received today, the order will be shipped tomorrow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要今天收到付款，订单就会在明天发出。

items.title: in case + clause
items.coreExplanation: in case + 句子表示“以防……；免得……”，强调主句的动作是为了提前准备或预防一种可能发生的情况。后面接完整句子，常用一般现在时表示将来可能发生的事。常见位置是主句后：do A in case B，也可前置：In case B, do A。
items.usageNote: 这个结构语气中性，最自然于准备、安全、提醒、日常安排等场景，意思是“先这样做，省得万一发生”。不适合单纯谈真实条件结果时替代if。in case从句前常不用will；放句首后通常加逗号，放句末前面一般不用逗号。初学者先稳用“主句 + in case + 一般现在时从句”最安全，如 Take an umbrella in case it rains.

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 填“先做的预防动作”，常用祈使句或一般现在时。常见错误：写成结果而不是准备，如 I stay home in case it rains 不一定像“预防”。可用：Take a map

slotSchema.label: 可能发生的情况
slotSchema.hint: 填完整句子，表示未来可能发生但不确定的事，通常用一般现在时，不要乱用will。可用：it rains；常见错误：in case it will rain。

slotSchema.label: 位置变体
slotSchema.hint: 可放句末最常见，也可句首前置。句首后通常加逗号。例：In case you need me, call this number. 常见错误：前置后漏逗号。

commonErrors: in case it will rain：错在in case从句通常不用will表示将来可能；改为in case it rains。
If it rains, take an umbrella：若你想表达“先带伞以防下雨”，这里if更像真实条件，不是预防准备；改为Take an umbrella in case it rains。
Take an umbrella in case of it rains：错在in case of后面接名词短语，不接完整句子；改为in case it rains或in case of rain。
In case of you need help, call me：错在把完整句子放到in case of后；改为In case you need help, call me。
I brought my passport, in case I will need it：错在从句时态和逗号都不自然；改为I brought my passport in case I need it。
We stayed quiet in case we don't wake the baby：错在从句应表示可能情况，不用否定来表达目的；改为We stayed quiet so that we wouldn't wake the baby，或We stayed quiet in case the baby woke up。

contrasts.title: vs if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，if表示“如果条件成立，就发生结果”；in case表示“先做准备，以防万一”。场景上，if偏条件逻辑；in case偏预防。对比：If it rains, we'll stay home.（真下雨才待在家）/ Take an umbrella in case it rains.（先带伞）

contrasts.title: vs unless + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，unless = if...not，表示“除非”；in case表示“以防”。语气上，unless是限制条件，in case是防备。对比：Don't call unless it's urgent.（除非紧急）/ Take my number in case it's urgent.（以防紧急）

contrasts.title: vs in case of + 名词
contrasts.note: 结构上，in case后接完整句子；in case of后接名词/名词短语。使用上，两者都可谈预防，但词性不同。对比：Take water in case you get thirsty. / Take water in case of hot weather.

contrasts.title: vs so that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，so that常表达目的，后面常见can/could/won't等；in case表达“防止坏情况”。语气上，so that更直接讲目的，in case更保守。对比：Speak loudly so that everyone can hear. / Bring a microphone in case the room is noisy.

contrasts.title: vs for fear that
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者后都可接句子；for fear that更书面、较正式，带“生怕”的担心感。in case更日常中性。对比：Take notes in case you forget. / He spoke softly for fear that he might wake the baby.

examples.sentence: Take an umbrella in case it rains.
examples.translation: 带把伞吧，以防下雨。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：主句 + in case + 一般现在时。

examples.sentence: In case you need help, call this number.
examples.translation: 如果你需要帮助，就打这个号码，以防万一。
examples.note: 前置时也自然；句首后加逗号。

examples.sentence: Please save your work often in case the computer crashes.
examples.translation: 请经常保存你的工作内容，以防电脑死机。
examples.note: 日常工作中的预防动作。

examples.sentence: Take some cash in case the card machine doesn't work.
examples.translation: 带一些现金，以防刷卡机不能用。
examples.note: 这个句子能看出它和if不同：不是等机器坏了才带现金。

examples.sentence: I packed some medicine in case the kids get sick.
examples.translation: 我带了一些药，以防孩子们生病。
examples.note: 主句可用过去时；从句仍常用一般现在时表示当时对未来的可能。

examples.sentence: Students should keep a copy of the form in case the original is lost.
examples.translation: 学生应保留一份表格副本，以防原件遗失。
examples.note: 较偏书面说明风格。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
templates.template: [主句动作] in case [可能情况].
slotHints: 前面放预防动作，如 take, save, bring, keep
后面放完整句子，通常用一般现在时，如 it rains / you need it
templates.exampleSentence: Keep my number in case you need it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 存一下我的号码，以防你需要。

templates.label: 句首前置模板
templates.template: In case [可能情况], [主句动作].
slotHints: 前置时先说风险，再说应对动作
句首后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: In case you get lost, send me a message.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果你迷路了，就给我发消息，以防万一。

templates.label: 书面说明模板
templates.template: [人/机构] should [预防动作] in case [可能情况].
slotHints: 适合通知、建议、规则
should让语气更像正式建议
templates.exampleSentence: Travelers should carry ID in case the police ask for it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 旅行者应随身带身份证件，以防警察要求查看。

templates.label: 过去准备模板
templates.template: [过去的准备动作] in case [可能情况].
slotHints: 主句可用过去时，表示当时先做了准备
从句仍常用一般现在时表达之后可能发生的事
templates.exampleSentence: We left early in case the traffic is bad.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们提前出发了，以防路况不好。

items.title: when + clause
items.coreExplanation: when后面接完整从句，用来说明“在……的时候”或“当……时”。它可以表示一次具体时间点，也可表示一般情况下的时间。when从句常放句首，后面主句通常用逗号；也可放句尾，主句在前更像补充时间信息。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都很常用，适合讲日常习惯、回忆经历、故事情节和说明某事发生的时间。若想强调两个动作同时持续进行，通常不用when而多用while。句首时常写“when从句, 主句”；句尾一般不加逗号。初学者先安全使用“when + 一般现在时/一般过去时, 主句”最稳。

slotSchema.label: when 从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整主谓结构，表示时间，如“when I got home”。常错：只放短语，不成句。例：when the class ended

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放主要事件，如“I called my mom”。常错：主句残缺或没有谓语。例：I felt much better

slotSchema.label: 时间含义
slotSchema.hint: 可表示一次事件或一般规律。常错：把所有“同时进行”都用when。例：When I was a child, I lived in Beijing

commonErrors: 错误：when doing my homework, my phone rang. 原因：基础用法里when后应接完整从句，不是随便用现在分词。改法：When I was doing my homework, my phone rang.
错误：when the meeting. 原因：when后面只有名词短语，不是从句。改法：when the meeting started / when the meeting ended.
错误：I was watching TV, when my brother was cooking. 原因：这里想表达两个持续动作同时进行，when不自然。改法：While I was watching TV, my brother was cooking.
错误：When I will arrive, I will call you. 原因：时间从句里通常不用will。改法：When I arrive, I will call you.
错误：When I got home I was tired because忘了句首逗号。 原因：when从句在句首时，主句前通常要断开。改法：When I got home, I was tired.
错误：I called my mom when, I got home. 原因：把逗号放在when后面，切断了从句。改法：I called my mom when I got home.

contrasts.title: while + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，while也接完整从句，但更强调两个动作在同一段时间内持续进行；when更适合某一时点或某事发生时。语气上while更像“与此同时”。对比：When the bell rang, we left. / While the teacher was speaking, we took notes.

contrasts.title: once + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上都可放句首，但once强调“ 一……就……；一旦…… ”，顺序感更强；when只是说明时间，不一定马上发生。场景上once更常见于规则、步骤、结果。对比：When you arrive, call me. / Once you arrive, call me.

contrasts.title: after + clause
contrasts.note: after明确表示“在……之后”，先后关系很清楚；when只说“当……时”，可紧接、可重合、也可泛指时间。写作里after更精确。对比：After I finished dinner, I studied. / When I finished dinner, I studied.

contrasts.title: before + clause
contrasts.note: before表示“在……之前”，和when的时间方向不同。when不表示前后，只定位时间点。场景上安排计划、提醒时before很常用。对比：Finish this before you leave. / Call me when you leave.

contrasts.title: until + clause
contrasts.note: until通常放在主句后，表示动作持续到某时为止；when从句常作时间背景，句首句尾都行。语义上until强调“持续终点”，when强调“发生时间”。对比：I waited until he arrived. / I smiled when he arrived.

examples.sentence: When I get home, I take off my shoes.
examples.translation: 我到家时会脱鞋。
examples.note: 最基础的习惯类用法：when从句在句首。

examples.sentence: I felt relieved when the exam ended.
examples.translation: 考试结束时，我松了一口气。
examples.note: when从句放句尾，更像补充时间信息。

examples.sentence: When he opened the door, everyone became silent.
examples.translation: 当他打开门时，大家都安静了下来。
examples.note: 讲故事时常用，表示某一时点触发后面的事情。

examples.sentence: When I was a child, I visited my grandparents every summer.
examples.translation: 我小时候每年夏天都去看望祖父母。
examples.note: 不是单次事件，也可表示某个一般时期。

examples.sentence: Please switch off your phone when the meeting starts.
examples.translation: 会议开始时，请把手机关掉。
examples.note: 和once相比，这里只是说明时间点，不特别强调“立刻”。

examples.sentence: When the data show a clear trend, the report becomes more convincing.
examples.translation: 当数据呈现出清晰趋势时，报告会更有说服力。
examples.note: 较书面化，但仍是同一基础结构。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: whenClause放完整主谓，如“I get home”
mainClause放主要动作或结果，如“I make tea”
templates.exampleSentence: When I get home, I make tea.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我到家时会泡茶。

templates.label: 句尾模板
slotHints: 主句先说核心信息
whenClause补充时间，如“the class ends”
templates.exampleSentence: I feel relaxed when the class ends.
templates.exampleTranslation: 下课时我会感到放松。

templates.label: 过去经历模板
slotHints: 两个部分常都用一般过去时
适合讲某次经历，如“I saw him / I waved”
templates.exampleSentence: When I saw him, I waved.
templates.exampleTranslation: 当我看见他时，我挥了挥手。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
slotHints: whenClause写条件式时间背景，如“students practice regularly”
主句可写结果、状态变化，如“learning becomes easier”
templates.exampleSentence: When students practice regularly, learning becomes easier.
templates.exampleTranslation: 当学生定期练习时，学习会变得更容易。

items.title: while + clause (time)
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构表示“在……的时候；与……同时”，用来把两个在同一时间进行或持续一段时间的情况连起来。while后面必须接完整从句，通常有主语+动词。while从句常放句首，后面主句用逗号；也可放句末，主句在前更像补充背景。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都很常见，适合讲故事、描述两个同时发生的动作、说明某段时间里的情况。若只是说某一刻“当……时”，很多时候when更自然；若想表达转折对比，while会变成“而、然而”的意思。句首时常写“While ..., ...”；句末一般不加逗号。初学者先安全使用“While + 主语 + be动词/动词, 主语 + 动词”。
sceneTags: 讲故事时交代同时发生的事
描述两个人各自在做什么
说明一个过程中的背景动作
写作中连接两段同一时间的信息
日常聊天描述家里或办公室场景

slotSchema.label: while从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整从句：主语+动词，如 while I was cooking。常见错误是只放名词或动词原形。例：while she studied

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放另一件同时发生的事，也要是完整从句，如 my brother watched TV。常见错误是缺主语。例：my phone rang

slotSchema.label: 位置
slotSchema.hint: 可用句首 While + 从句, 主句；也可 主句 while + 从句。常见错误是句首不用逗号。例：While I was reading, he called.

commonErrors: Wrong: While cooking, my brother watched TV. 原因：本卡基础用法要求while后接完整从句，不要省略主语。改成：While I was cooking, my brother watched TV.
Wrong: While I was tired, I went to bed. 原因：这里更像先后发生，不是两个同时进行的动作；while时间义不自然。改成：When I was tired, I went to bed. 或 I went to bed because I was tired.
Wrong: While I cooked, and he washed the dishes. 原因：用了while后，不需要再加and连接两个分句。改成：While I cooked, he washed the dishes.
Wrong: I was doing homework, while my sister slept. 原因：句中这个逗号常让人读成转折“而”，时间义里放句中通常不用逗号。改成：I was doing homework while my sister slept.
Wrong: While I was cooking dinner. 原因：只有从句，没有主句，句子不完整。改成：While I was cooking dinner, the phone rang.
Wrong: While I arrived home, it started to rain. 原因：arrive是瞬间动作，和while时间义搭配不自然。改成：When I arrived home, it started to rain. 或 While I was walking home, it started to rain.

contrasts.title: while vs when
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接完整从句，但while更强调“同时进行/同一时期”，when更常指“某一时刻发生”。场景上，while适合背景中的持续动作；when适合突然事件或到达某刻。对比：While I was showering, the phone rang. / When the phone rang, I was showering.

contrasts.title: while (time) vs while (contrast)
contrasts.note: 结构一样，都可放句首或句中；区别在意义和语气。时间义表示“同时”，转折义表示“而、然而”，更像比较两件事。对比：While I cooked, he set the table.（同时）/ While I like tea, my sister prefers coffee.（对比）

contrasts.title: while vs as
contrasts.note: 结构上都能接从句；as也可表示“随着”或“当……时”。语气上，while更清楚地表示两个情况并行；as更书面，也更容易有“伴随变化”的感觉。对比：While I was walking, I called her. / As I was walking, I called her.

contrasts.title: while + clause vs during + noun phrase
contrasts.note: 结构差别很大：while后接完整从句，during后接名词短语，不能接主谓结构。场景上，during只标时间段，不直接说动作。对比：While I was in college, I worked part-time. / During college, I worked part-time.

contrasts.title: while vs meanwhile
contrasts.note: while是连词，后面直接接从句；meanwhile通常是副词，常单独放句首或句中连接两句。语气上，meanwhile更像叙述转场。对比：While I cleaned the kitchen, he took out the trash. / I cleaned the kitchen. Meanwhile, he took out the trash.

examples.sentence: While I was cooking, my brother washed the dishes.
examples.translation: 我做饭的时候，我哥哥在洗碗。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：while从句放句首，后面主句。

examples.sentence: My brother washed the dishes while I was cooking.
examples.translation: 我做饭的时候，我哥哥在洗碗。
examples.note: 同样是时间义，但while从句放句末，更像补充背景。

examples.sentence: While the experiment was running, the researchers recorded the data.
examples.translation: 实验进行期间，研究人员记录了数据。
examples.note: 写作里很常见，用来描述同一过程中的另一动作。

examples.sentence: While I was taking a shower, the phone rang.
examples.translation: 我在洗澡的时候，电话响了。
examples.note: while给出持续背景动作；主句是突然发生的事件。和when对比很有代表性。

examples.sentence: While she studied, her friends were chatting outside.
examples.translation: 她学习的时候，她的朋友们在外面聊天。
examples.note: 两个情况在同一时间段内发生。

examples.sentence: While we were waiting for the bus, it started to snow.
examples.translation: 我们等公交的时候，开始下雪了。
examples.note: 日常口语自然；while后面用进行中的背景动作很常见。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: timeClause放完整从句，优先用进行时或be动词，如 I was cooking
mainClause放另一件同时发生的事，如 my brother washed the dishes
templates.exampleSentence: While I was cooking, my brother washed the dishes.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我做饭的时候，我哥哥在洗碗。

templates.label: 句末while模板
slotHints: mainClause先说主要事件
timeClause后补充同时发生的背景，句末一般不加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: My brother washed the dishes while I was cooking.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我做饭的时候，我哥哥在洗碗。

templates.label: 写作过程模板
slotHints: timeClause可写某过程正在进行，如 the meeting was going on
mainClause写同一时期的动作，如 the team discussed the budget
templates.exampleSentence: While the meeting was going on, the team discussed the budget.
templates.exampleTranslation: 会议进行时，团队讨论了预算。

items.title: before + clause
items.coreExplanation: before + 从句表示“在某事之前”。before后面要接完整从句，通常含主语+谓语，如 before I leave。它既可放句首作时间背景，也可放主句后面补充时间；句首时后面常加逗号，句尾更口语、更常见。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常用，适合说步骤、日常安排、提醒、计划等：Finish this before you go. 不适合单独接名词时还按从句规则造句；只有名词时应用 before + 名词。放句首时常写“Before..., 主句”；放句尾时常写“主句 before...”。初学者先稳用“主句 + before + 主语 + 动词”最安全。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放主要动作或结果。常用祈使句、一般现在时或一般将来时。常见错误是把时间信息全塞进before从句而主句不完整。例：Call me / before you leave.

slotSchema.label: before从句主语
slotSchema.hint: 用表示执行较早动作的人或事，如 I, you, we, the meeting。常见错误是省掉主语写成 before leave。例：before I leave.

slotSchema.label: before从句谓语
slotSchema.hint: 用能构成完整从句的动词形式，最常见是一般现在时表示将来安排：before you go。常见错误是用不定式 before to go。例：before she starts.

commonErrors: before leave 为什么错：before后这里需要完整从句，缺主语。怎么改：before I leave / before you leave。
before to go 为什么错：before + clause 不接to不定式。怎么改：before I go；若不用从句，可说 before going。
I finished it before yesterday 为什么错：这里不是从句，而是时间名词短语，意思也容易混乱。怎么改：I finished it before I left yesterday；或 I finished it the day before yesterday。
Before I leave. I will call you. 为什么错：把before从句单独断成碎句。怎么改：Before I leave, I will call you. 或 I will call you before I leave.
I will before you leave call you 为什么错：位置不自然，before从句通常放句尾或句首，不夹在动词中间。怎么改：I will call you before you leave.
Before you will go, turn off the lights. 为什么错：时间从句里通常不用 will 表将来。怎么改：Before you go, turn off the lights.

contrasts.title: after + clause
contrasts.note: 结构相似，都是连词+完整从句；区别在时间先后：before说“更早”，after说“更晚”。场景上，before常用于提醒和预先安排，after常用于回顾顺序。对比：Call me before you leave. / Call me after you arrive.

contrasts.title: until + clause
contrasts.note: before强调某动作发生在另一动作之前；until强调状态持续到某时为止。结构上，until前常是持续性主句。语气上，until更像“到……才/一直到……”。对比：Wait here until I come back. / Finish this before I come back.

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: when只标出“在……时候”，不一定强调先后边界；before明确表示“早于”。场景上，when更中性叙事，before更适合步骤和提醒。对比：When he arrived, we ate. / Before he arrived, we ate.

contrasts.title: while + clause (time)
contrasts.note: while强调两个动作同时进行，常配进行性或持续动作；before强调一前一后，不表示同时。语气上，while更像背景描写，before更像顺序提示。对比：While I was cooking, he set the table. / Before I cooked, he set the table.

contrasts.title: before + noun
contrasts.note: 结构不同：before + clause 后面是完整从句；before + noun 后面只是名词短语。场景上两者都常用，但别混写。对比：before class（课前）/ before class starts（上课前）.

examples.sentence: Call me before you leave.
examples.translation: 你离开前给我打电话。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：主句 + before从句。

examples.sentence: Before you go, turn off the lights.
examples.translation: 你走之前，把灯关掉。
examples.note: 句首位置；before从句后常加逗号。

examples.sentence: Please read the instructions carefully before you begin.
examples.translation: 开始之前，请认真阅读说明。
examples.note: 用于步骤说明很自然。

examples.sentence: We ate dinner before he arrived.
examples.translation: 他到之前，我们已经吃过晚饭了。
examples.note: 与 after/when 容易混淆；这里强调“更早”。

examples.sentence: Before the meeting starts, all participants should sign in.
examples.translation: 会议开始前，所有与会者都应签到。
examples.note: 偏书面、通知风格。

examples.sentence: I always brush my teeth before I go to bed.
examples.translation: 我总是在睡觉前刷牙。
examples.note: 日常习惯表达非常常见。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
slotHints: 主句放主要动作，如 Call me / Finish this / I always brush my teeth
从句主语常用 I/you/he/she/we
从句动词用一般现在时最稳，如 leave, go, start
templates.exampleSentence: Finish your homework before you watch TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 看电视前先完成作业。

templates.label: 句首时间背景模板
slotHints: 先说较早的时间点，后面用逗号接主句
适合提醒、规则、步骤说明
不要把before从句单独写成一句
templates.exampleSentence: Before you submit the form, check your email address.
templates.exampleTranslation: 提交表格前，检查一下你的邮箱地址。

templates.label: 书面说明模板
slotHints: 从句主语可用事物名词，如 the meeting, the class, the train
适合通知、说明、公告
动词常用 starts, begins, leaves 等
templates.exampleSentence: Before the class begins, students must put away their phones.
templates.exampleTranslation: 上课前，学生必须把手机收起来。

items.title: after + clause
items.coreExplanation: after + 从句用来表示“在……之后”，先说明一个较早发生的动作，再接主句说后面的事。after后面通常接完整从句（主语+谓语），也可接动名词短语。它常放句首作时间背景，也可放句尾，句首后通常加逗号。
items.usageNote: 这个结构语气中性，口语和写作都很常见，适合讲步骤、回忆经历、叙述故事中的先后顺序。它不适合单纯表示“同时进行”，那时更自然的是when/while。after-clause放句首时，后面通常用逗号；放句尾时一般不加。初学者最稳妥的做法是：先写After + 一件先发生的事, 再写主句，时态先保持自然一致，再根据上下文调整。
sceneTags: 描述做完一件事再做另一件事

slotSchema.label: after后的较早动作
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句，常见形式是主语+谓语，如 after I finished my homework。常见错误：只放一个动词原形，如 after finish homework。也可用动名词：after finishing my homework。

slotSchema.label: 后发生的主要事件
slotSchema.hint: 这里写after之后发生的事，如 I went out. 常见错误：把顺序写反，导致意思变成“出去后才写作业”。小例：After I finished my homework, I went out.

slotSchema.label: 可替换的动名词短语
slotSchema.hint: 如果after后主语和主句主语一致，常可用动名词短语，如 after eating dinner。常见错误：after eat dinner。小例：After eating dinner, we watched TV.

commonErrors: After finish my homework, I went out.｜错在after后不能直接接动词原形。｜改成After I finished my homework, I went out. 或 After finishing my homework, I went out.
I went out after I finished my homework, then I finished it.｜错在时间顺序自相矛盾，after后应是更早发生的事。｜先理清先后：After I finished my homework, I went out.
After I was tired, I went to bed.｜语法未必错，但常不自然；“累了”是状态，不如直接说原因或时间点。｜更自然：After I finished work, I went to bed. / When I got tired, I went to bed.
After I finish dinner, I watched TV yesterday.｜时态搭配不自然；谈昨天的过去事件时，两边通常都用过去时。｜改成After I finished dinner, I watched TV.
After I finished my homework I went out.｜句首after从句后少逗号，正式写作里不够规范。｜改成After I finished my homework, I went out.
After that I finished my homework.｜after that和after + clause不是一回事；这里没有用从句说明“在什么之后”。｜如果要接从句：After I got home, I finished my homework.

contrasts.title: after + clause vs before + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接完整从句，但时间方向相反。after表示“晚于从句事件”，before表示“早于从句事件”。叙事时别写反。对比：After I ate, I slept.（吃完后睡）/ Before I ate, I slept.（吃前睡）

contrasts.title: after + clause vs once + clause
contrasts.note: 结构都能放句首，但once更强调“一旦完成就立刻进入下一步”，更像步骤触发；after只是表示在……之后，时间间隔可长可短。对比：Once you arrive, call me. / After you arrive, call me.

contrasts.title: after + clause vs when + clause
contrasts.note: after强调先后顺序清楚分开；when只给时间点，常不强调哪件更早，也可表示“当……时”。场景上，after更适合步骤，when更适合时间背景。对比：After I got home, I cooked. / When I got home, I cooked.

contrasts.title: after + clause vs while + clause
contrasts.note: 结构都可接从句，但while强调两个动作同时进行或某段期间内发生；after强调前后相继，不同时。对比：While I was cooking, he called. / After I cooked, he called.

contrasts.title: after + clause vs after + -ing
contrasts.note: 结构差别是after后接完整从句，或接动名词短语。after + -ing通常用于主语相同、表达更紧凑，常见于书面或简洁表达；after + clause更清楚，适合初学者。对比：After I finished work, I went home. / After finishing work, I went home.

examples.sentence: After I finished my homework, I went out.
examples.translation: 我做完作业后出去了。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：先发生的事放after后。

examples.sentence: I went out after I finished my homework.
examples.translation: 我做完作业后出去了。
examples.note: 同样正确，只是把after从句放到句尾。

examples.sentence: After the meeting ended, the manager sent a summary email.
examples.translation: 会议结束后，经理发了一封总结邮件。
examples.note: 偏写作和职场叙述。

examples.sentence: After I arrived, I called her; when I arrived, I was already tired.
examples.translation: 我到达后给她打了电话；我到达时已经很累了。
examples.note: 前半句强调先后，后半句用when强调时间点，对比很明显。

examples.sentence: After eating dinner, we watched TV.
examples.translation: 吃完晚饭后，我们看了电视。
examples.note: 主语一致时可用after + 动名词，更简洁。

examples.sentence: After he got home, he took a shower and went to bed.
examples.translation: 他到家后洗了个澡就睡了。
examples.note: 适合讲一天结束时的连续动作。

templates.label: 最稳妥基础模板
slotHints: [earlierAction]写先发生的完整从句，如 I finished my homework
[mainResult]写之后发生的主句，如 I went out
templates.exampleSentence: After I finished my homework, I went out.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我做完作业后出去了。

templates.label: 句尾位置模板
slotHints: 主句先写结果或后发生的动作
after后仍然接完整从句，不要用动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: I went out after I finished my homework.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我做完作业后出去了。

templates.label: 主语一致的简洁模板
slotHints: [altNouning]用动名词短语，如 finishing work
这个模板通常要求前后主语一致
templates.exampleSentence: After finishing work, I went home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 下班后我回家了。

templates.label: 写作叙述模板
slotHints: 先写时间顺序背景，再写正式叙述内容
适合故事、报告、流程说明
templates.exampleSentence: After the class ended, the teacher answered a few questions.
templates.exampleTranslation: 下课后，老师回答了几个问题。

items.title: until + clause
items.coreExplanation: until + 从句表示“直到……才结束/持续到……为止”。主句先写持续的动作、状态或安排，后面接until引导的完整从句，说明终点时间或事件。最常见位置是主句在前：主句 + until + 从句；也可把until从句放句首，但后面通常要加逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常用，适合表达等待、持续、停留、工作、保持某种状态，也常用于计划安排。它不适合直接接名词或时间点；那时常用until + 名词/时间。句首用Until ...时，后面通常加逗号。初学者最安全的做法是：先写一个能持续的主句，再加until + 主语 + 动词。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放“可持续一段时间”的动作或状态，如 wait, stay, work, be, keep。常见错误：用瞬间动作如 arrive。例：I waited

slotSchema.label: until从句主语
slotSchema.hint: 放触发终点的人或事，如 he, the bus, class。常见错误：省略主语。例：until the bus

slotSchema.label: 终点从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句：主语 + 动词，表示终点事件。常见错误：只写until finished。例：until she came back

commonErrors: I waited until 10 o'clock he came.｜错因：把时间点和完整从句混在一起。｜改法：二选一：I waited until 10 o'clock. / I waited until he came.
I will arrive until 8.｜错因：arrive是瞬间动作，不能表示“持续到”。｜改法：用not...until表示“直到8点才到”：I won't arrive until 8.
Until he came back I waited.｜错因：语法不一定错，但对初学者不自然，且句首后少逗号。｜改法：更安全：I waited until he came back. 或 Until he came back, I waited.
I stayed home until to rain stopped.｜错因：until后不能接to不定式。｜改法：接完整从句：I stayed home until the rain stopped.
I didn't leave until when he called.｜错因：until后面不要再加when。｜改法：I didn't leave until he called.
She studied until finishing dinner.｜错因：这里要表达“直到她吃完饭”，应接从句，不是动名词短语。｜改法：She studied until she finished dinner.

contrasts.title: before + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，until表示“持续到终点”，before表示“在某事之前”。语义方向不同。场景上，until强调结束点；before强调先后顺序。对比：I waited until he arrived.＝我一直等到他到。/ I left before he arrived.＝他到之前我就走了。

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，when从句只给出时间点；until从句强调主句持续到那个点。语气上，when更中性描述发生时间，until更突出“拖到那时”。对比：Call me when you arrive. / I stayed there until you arrived.

contrasts.title: not ... until
contrasts.note: 结构上，main clause until + clause表示“持续到”；not ... until表示“直到……才开始/发生”。场景上，前者常配wait/stay/work，后者常配leave/start/arrive。对比：I slept until 8. / I didn't sleep until 8.

contrasts.title: till
contrasts.note: 结构上，till和until意思几乎一样，但till更口语，正式写作里until更稳妥。位置上，句首更常用until，不太常用Till开头。对比：I'll wait until you call. / I'll wait till you call.

contrasts.title: until + noun/time
contrasts.note: 结构上，until后面可以接从句，也可以直接接名词或时间点；本卡重点是until + clause。场景上，说具体钟点时常直接接时间。对比：I worked until midnight. / I worked until the meeting ended.

examples.sentence: I waited until he called.
examples.translation: 我一直等到他打电话来。
examples.note: 最基础用法：主句表示持续动作，until从句表示终点事件。

examples.sentence: Until the rain stopped, we stayed inside.
examples.translation: 直到雨停，我们都待在里面。
examples.note: 句首位置可以用，但后面通常加逗号。

examples.sentence: The shop will remain closed until the manager returns.
examples.translation: 这家店将在经理回来之前一直关闭。
examples.note: 较书面，remain closed很适合和until搭配。

examples.sentence: I worked until the meeting ended, not before it started.
examples.translation: 我一直工作到会议结束，不是在会议开始前就停下。
examples.note: 用来体会until与before表达方向不同。

examples.sentence: She didn't leave until her friend arrived.
examples.translation: 她直到朋友到了才离开。
examples.note: 这是not...until，对比主句持续型很重要。

examples.sentence: We'll stay here until the bus comes.
examples.translation: 我们会待在这里直到公交车来。
examples.note: 很自然的日常计划句。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
templates.template: I/We + 持续动作 + until + 主语 + 动词
slotHints: 主句用wait, stay, work, live, keep等可持续动作
until后写完整从句，不要只写一个动词
templates.exampleSentence: I waited until she arrived.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我一直等到她到了。

templates.label: 句首替代模板
templates.template: Until + 主语 + 动词, + 主句
slotHints: 句首until从句后通常加逗号
主句仍然写持续动作或状态
templates.exampleSentence: Until class ended, he stayed quiet.
templates.exampleTranslation: 直到下课，他都保持安静。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
templates.template: 主语 + remain/be + 状态 + until + 主语 + 动词
slotHints: 适合公告、说明、书面通知
状态可用 closed, open, available, silent
templates.exampleSentence: The road will remain closed until the work is finished.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这条路将一直封闭到工程结束。

templates.label: 日常计划模板
templates.template: We/I'll + stay/wait + until + 人/事 + 动词
slotHints: 很适合说等人、等车、等消息
不要把until和when连用
templates.exampleSentence: We'll wait until the doctor calls us.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们会一直等到医生叫我们。

items.title: once + clause
items.coreExplanation: once + 从句表示“某事一完成/一发生，主句就会发生”，重点是先后顺序很紧。once后面必须接完整从句，通常是主语+动词，不能只接名词。它常放句首，后面用逗号接主句；也可放主句后面。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，常见于计划、说明、流程、规则、邮件和较正式口语，表示“条件一满足就进行下一步”。不适合只想表达普通“当……时”而没有紧接着发生的感觉，也不用于同时进行。句首时通常写“Once..., ...”；句中时一般不用逗号。初学者最安全：Once + 主语 + 动词, 主语 + will/can + 动词。

slotSchema.label: once从句
slotSchema.hint: 放“先完成的事”的完整从句，常用一般现在时表示将来条件。常错：写成 Once the meeting. 应改为 Once the meeting ends. 例：Once you arrive

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 放“紧接着发生的事”，常用 will/can/imperative。常错：结果太无关或太晚，弱化“一……就……”。例：we’ll start dinner

slotSchema.label: 主语选择
slotSchema.hint: 两边主语可相同也可不同。常错：误以为必须同主语。例：Once I finish, you can check it.

commonErrors: Wrong: Once the meeting, we'll start. — 错因：once后面不能只放名词短语。— 改法：用完整从句：Once the meeting ends, we'll start.
Wrong: Once you will arrive, call me. — 错因：时间/条件从句里通常不用will。— 改法：用一般现在时：Once you arrive, call me.
Wrong: We are cooking once he does homework. — 错因：这里像是同时进行，不是“先完成再发生”。— 改法：若表示之后：Once he finishes his homework, we can cook.
Wrong: Once after you finish, send it. — 错因：once和after不要叠加表达同一层时间关系。— 改法：用其一：Once you finish, send it. / After you finish, send it.
Wrong: Once you finish the report. — 错因：只有从句，没有主句，句子不完整。— 改法：补主句：Once you finish the report, email it to me.
Wrong: Once I finish, I finished it. — 错因：时态搭配不自然；once从句说条件，主句应表示后续结果。— 改法：Once I finish, I'll send it.

contrasts.title: vs when + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整从句；但when只说“当……时”，不一定强调前一件事完成后立刻发生，语气更宽。once更强调“条件一满足就下一步”。对比：When you arrive, call me.（到时打）/ Once you arrive, call me.（一到就打）

contrasts.title: vs after + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上都可放句首或句中；after只是“在……之后”，时间间隔可长可短，once更像“刚一……就……”。场景上after更中性叙述，once更适合流程和安排。对比：After dinner, we talked. / Once dinner was over, we left.

contrasts.title: vs as soon as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构相近，都表示“立刻”；但as soon as更直接口语，once略更简洁，也常带“条件满足”感。说明步骤时once常更稳。对比：As soon as you get home, text me. / Once you get home, text me.

contrasts.title: vs if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上都能引出条件从句；但if强调“是否会发生”不确定，once默认事情会发生，只是等它发生。语气上once更有把握。对比：If he calls, tell me.（如果他打来）/ Once he calls, tell me.（他一打来就告诉我）

examples.sentence: Once you arrive, call me.
examples.translation: 你一到就给我打电话。
examples.note: 最安全的初学者用法：句首once从句+主句祈使句。

examples.sentence: We'll start dinner once Dad gets home.
examples.translation: 爸爸一到家，我们就开饭。
examples.note: once从句放句尾也很自然，通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: Once the payment is received, your order will be processed.
examples.translation: 一旦收到付款，您的订单将被处理。
examples.note: 常见于通知、规则、客服和书面语。

examples.sentence: Once you finish your homework, you can watch TV.
examples.translation: 你一做完作业，就可以看电视。
examples.note: 比when更强调“先完成作业，再看电视”。

examples.sentence: Once I have the final numbers, I'll send the report.
examples.translation: 我一拿到最终数据，就会把报告发出去。
examples.note: 计划与工作安排中很常见。

examples.sentence: Once the rain stops, let's go for a walk.
examples.translation: 雨一停，我们就去散步吧。
examples.note: 主句可用let's表示提议。

templates.label: 安全入门模板
slotHints: [condition]放先发生的完整从句，常用一般现在时
[result]放紧接着的动作，可用will/can/祈使句
templates.exampleSentence: Once you finish, send me a message.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你一完成就给我发个消息。

templates.label: 句尾模板
slotHints: 先说主要安排，再补充触发条件
句尾once从句前通常不加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: I'll call you once I get the update.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我一收到最新消息就给你打电话。

templates.label: 书面通知模板
slotHints: [condition]常用业务流程条件，如payment is received
[result]可用will be processed/sent/updated等正式表达
templates.exampleSentence: Once your application is approved, you will be notified by email.
templates.exampleTranslation: 一旦你的申请获批，你将收到电子邮件通知。

items.title: that-clause after think / say / know
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来把“想法、说的话、知道的内容”接成一个完整内容：verb + that + 完整句子。that后面必须是有主语和谓语的句子，不能只放名词。它通常紧跟在think、say、know、believe、hear等动词后面，最常出现在主句动词后面作宾语。
items.usageNote: 语气通常是neutral，口语和写作都很常见。适合表达观点、转述别人说的话、说明你知道或发现的事实。初学者最常用在 I think that..., She said that..., We know that...。不适合直接接疑问内容；如果是“是否”或“哪里、为什么”等信息，要改用 whether/if 或 wh-从句。that一般放在动词后；口语里常省略that，但学习阶段先保留更安全。

slotSchema.label: 前面的动词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示想、说、知道、相信、听说等动词，如 think, say, know, believe, hear。常错：后面直接接名词而不是完整句。例：I think that he is right.

slotSchema.label: 连接词 that
slotSchema.hint: 用 that 把前面动词和后面的内容连起来。常错：把 that 当代词用，后面不写完整句。例：She said that she was tired.

slotSchema.label: that后面的完整内容
slotSchema.hint: 必须是陈述句结构，要有主语+谓语，如 he is busy, they won the game。常错：写成疑问语序。例：We know that the train is late.

commonErrors: I think that him is nice.｜错因：that后面要接完整句，主语应用主格，不用宾格。｜改法：I think that he is nice.
She said that what time is it.｜错因：that后面不能用直接疑问句语序。｜改法：She asked what time it was. 或 She said that it was late.
We know that whether he will come.｜错因：that和whether不能这样叠用来引导同一个内容。｜改法：We know whether he will come. 或 We know that he will come.
I think that to go now is good.｜错因：这里想表达普通看法时，that后更自然是完整陈述句。｜改法：I think that going now is a good idea. / I think that it is good to go now.
He said me that he was busy.｜错因：say后不能直接接人作双宾语。｜改法：He said to me that he was busy. 或 He told me that he was busy.
I know that where she lives.｜错因：that后不能再接wh-引导的嵌入问句。｜改法：I know where she lives. 或 I know that she lives in Shanghai.

contrasts.title: vs whether / if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：that后接“陈述内容”，whether/if后接“是否”。场景差别：that用于已陈述的内容；whether/if用于不确定、二选一或是否问题。对比：I know that he is busy. / I know whether he is busy.

contrasts.title: vs wh- clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：that + 陈述句；wh-从句用 what/where/why/how 等开头。场景差别：that讲“内容”；wh-从句讲“具体信息点”。对比：She said that he left. / She said why he left.

contrasts.title: vs noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：本卡是动词后接that从句；另一种是名词后接that从句，如 the fact that...。场景差别：动词结构更像“我认为/他说”；名词结构更像“这个事实/这个想法”。对比：I know that he came. / The fact that he came surprised me.

contrasts.title: vs zero that in speech
contrasts.note: 结构差别：口语里that常可省略，如 I think he’s right；完整形式是 I think that he’s right。语气差别：省略更口语、更快；保留that更清楚，尤其长句或学习阶段更安全。对比：She said that she was ready. / She said she was ready.

examples.sentence: I think that this book is useful.
examples.translation: 我认为这本书很有用。
examples.note: 最基础、安全的用法：think后接that从句。

examples.sentence: She said that she would call me later.
examples.translation: 她说她晚一点会给我打电话。
examples.note: 用来转述别人说的话。

examples.sentence: We know that the museum is closed on Monday.
examples.translation: 我们知道博物馆周一闭馆。
examples.note: know后接已知事实内容。

examples.sentence: I believe that he is honest, but I am not sure.
examples.translation: 我相信他是诚实的，但我不确定。
examples.note: 适合比较语气：believe比know确定性弱。

examples.sentence: The report states that sales increased last year.
examples.translation: 报告指出去年销售额上升了。
examples.note: 较偏书面表达，常见于说明文。

examples.sentence: My mom said that dinner is ready.
examples.translation: 我妈妈说晚饭准备好了。
examples.note: 很自然的日常家庭场景。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: [contentClause] 放完整句子，如 it is a good idea / he is busy；不要只放一个名词
templates.exampleSentence: I think that it is a good idea.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为这是个好主意。

templates.label: 转述别人说的话
slotHints: [reportingVerbSubject] 可用 she / he / my teacher；[contentClause] 用完整陈述句，不用疑问语序
templates.exampleSentence: My teacher said that the test is on Friday.
templates.exampleTranslation: 老师说考试在周五。

templates.label: 说明已知事实
slotHints: [subject] 可用 I / we / everyone；[contentClause] 适合放事实、安排、规则等内容
templates.exampleSentence: We know that the store closes at nine.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们知道这家店九点关门。

templates.label: 写作型模板
slotHints: 适合书面说明；[contentClause] 放客观信息，如 prices rose / demand fell
templates.exampleSentence: The report says that demand increased in July.
templates.exampleTranslation: 报告称七月份需求增加了。

items.title: that-clause after fact / idea / belief nouns
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用在fact, idea, belief, possibility, news等“抽象名词”后面，用that从句具体说明“这个名词的内容是什么”。基本形式是名词+that+完整句子。它常出现在主语、宾语或介词后，书面语和说明性表达里尤其常见。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，常见于写作、讨论观点、解释事实、学术说明、新闻转述。适合说“某个想法/事实/消息/证据的内容”。不适合直接接在普通具体名词后，如*the book that...那通常是定语从句。that从句通常紧跟内容名词，不要随意隔太远；句中可作主语如The fact that..., 也可作宾语如We discussed the idea that.... 初学者最安全先用the fact that...。

slotSchema.label: 内容名词
slotSchema.hint: 放fact, idea, belief, possibility, news, evidence, claim这类能承载“内容”的抽象名词。常见错误是放具体名词如book、student。例：the fact / the idea

slotSchema.label: that从句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整陈述句，要有主语+谓语。常见错误是只放短语或疑问语序。例：that he was late / that this plan will work

slotSchema.label: 句子框架
slotSchema.hint: 常见框架有The fact that... is..., We discussed the idea that..., There is a belief that...。常见错误是把that从句放太远，导致名词和内容分开。例：The belief that hard work matters is common.

commonErrors: *The book that he was late surprised me. 错在把内容从句接在具体名词book后，读者会以为是定语从句。改为The fact that he was late surprised me.
*The fact that why he left is unclear. 错在that从句里又用了why，结构重复。改为The fact that he left is unclear. 或 The reason why he left is unclear.
*We discussed the idea that should we move online. 错在that从句用了疑问语序。改为We discussed the idea that we should move online.
*I believe the fact that he is honest. 错在动词搭配不自然，believe通常直接接that从句。改为I believe that he is honest. 或 I accept the fact that he is honest.
*The fact is that he was late. 和 *the fact that he was late 混用成 *The fact that is he was late. 错在把两种结构拼在一起。改为The fact is that he was late. 或 The fact that he was late matters.
*There is an idea that about education is changing. 错在that后不是完整句子。改为There is an idea that education is changing.

contrasts.title: vs the fact that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上它其实是本卡最常见实例：fact + that从句；区别在于the fact that几乎是固定高频搭配，最安全。语气中性偏正式，适合说明已知事实。比较：The fact that she called mattered. / The idea that she called spread quickly.

contrasts.title: vs the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：the reason is that...是系表结构；reason that...通常不是用来装“内容”，而reason后更常接for/why。场景上前者用来解释原因，后者内容名词结构用来说明信息内容。比较：The reason is that we were tired. / The fact is that we were tired.

contrasts.title: vs verb + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：动词直接接that从句，如think that, say that；本卡是名词后接that从句。动词结构更口语直接，名词结构更适合写作和名词化表达。比较：I believe that he is right. / There is a belief that he is right.

contrasts.title: vs 定语从句 noun + that/who...
contrasts.note: 结构上都可能看到that，但功能不同：内容从句说明“内容”，定语从句说明“哪一个人/物”。场景上内容从句多跟抽象名词，定语从句多跟具体名词。比较：the idea that we need change / the idea that changed my life

contrasts.title: vs whether + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：that表示陈述内容，whether表示“是否”。语义上如果内容是不确定的两种可能，常用whether，不用that。比较：the belief that he is honest / the question whether he is honest

examples.sentence: The fact that he was late surprised everyone.
examples.translation: 他迟到了这一事实让所有人都很惊讶。
examples.note: 最安全、最核心的用法：the fact that + 完整句子。

examples.sentence: We discussed the idea that students should have more free time.
examples.translation: 我们讨论了“学生应该有更多自由时间”这个想法。
examples.note: 名词短语作宾语时，that从句紧跟idea。

examples.sentence: The belief that hard work leads to success is still common.
examples.translation: “努力会带来成功”这一信念仍然很常见。
examples.note: 主语较长，常见于说明文和议论文。

examples.sentence: There is growing evidence that the plan will fail.
examples.translation: 越来越多的证据表明这个计划会失败。
examples.note: writing风格明显，常见于报道、分析、学术表达。

examples.sentence: I accept the fact that this is cheaper, but it is less reliable.
examples.translation: 我接受这东西更便宜这个事实，但它没那么可靠。
examples.note: 适合做比较；不要误换成the reason is that。

examples.sentence: She shared the news that her sister got the job.
examples.translation: 她分享了她姐姐得到那份工作的消息。
examples.note: 日常也能用，news后接that从句很自然。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: {thatClause}放完整陈述句，如 he was late；后面verb/rest写这个事实的结果，如 surprised me, matters, is important
templates.exampleSentence: The fact that we finished early saved a lot of time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们提前完成这一事实节省了很多时间。

templates.label: 宾语位置模板
slotHints: {contentNoun}可用 idea, possibility, claim；{thatClause}用正常陈述语序，如 we should wait
templates.exampleSentence: We discussed the idea that we should wait a little longer.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们讨论了我们应该再等一会儿这个想法。

templates.label: 写作表达模板
slotHints: {modifier}可用 growing, clear, strong；{contentNoun}常用 evidence, belief, possibility；{thatClause}写具体内容
templates.exampleSentence: There is strong evidence that the method is effective.
templates.exampleTranslation: 有强有力的证据表明这种方法是有效的。

templates.label: 系动词后总结模板
slotHints: 适合把整个名词结构当主语；{contentNoun}可用 belief, idea, claim；后面常接 is common / is wrong / is important
templates.exampleSentence: The belief that money solves everything is naive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 认为金钱能解决一切的这种想法很天真。

items.title: what + clause
items.coreExplanation: what-clause 表示“……的东西 / 事情”，把一个完整意思变成名词成分来当主语、宾语或表语。基本结构是 what + 从句，从句里通常缺少 thing / something 所代表的内容。它常放在动词后作宾语，也常放句首作主语。
items.usageNote: 语气通常中性，口语和写作都很常见，适合解释内容、指出重点、说明需要什么或真正发生了什么。不适合只想单纯连接一个完整事实时；那时常用 that-clause。what-clause 本身就是一个整体，不要再在前面加 the thing 或 that。放句首时后面正常接谓语；句中作宾语时直接放在动词后，不需要逗号。初学者先安全使用 I know what... / What... is...。

slotSchema.label: what 从句
slotSchema.hint: 放 what + 完整从句，但从句里缺少“东西/内容”这一项：what he said。常错：写成 what did he say。小例：I remember what she bought.

slotSchema.label: 主句动词/系动词
slotSchema.hint: 常用 know, remember, need, want, is, matters 等。常错：主句和 what 从句都缺谓语。小例：What I need is time.

slotSchema.label: 补足部分
slotSchema.hint: 可放宾语、表语或其他补充信息。常错：句首 what-clause 后忘记接真正谓语。小例：What he said surprised me.

commonErrors: the thing what I need is time.｜错因：what 已经包含“the thing that”的意思，前面不能再加 the thing。｜改法：What I need is time. 或 The thing that I need is time.
I know what do you mean.｜错因：嵌入从句不用疑问句语序。｜改法：I know what you mean.
What he said it was true.｜错因：what-clause 已经是主语，后面不能再多一个 it 作形式主语。｜改法：What he said was true.
I need that you bought.｜错因：that-clause 不能直接表示“你买的那个东西”。｜改法：I need what you bought.
What he wants, a new phone.｜错因：句首 what-clause 作主语后，后面必须有谓语，不能只放名词。｜改法：What he wants is a new phone.
I don't know what is he doing.｜错因：嵌入从句应用陈述语序。｜改法：I don't know what he is doing.

contrasts.title: vs that-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，that-clause 后面是完整句子；what-clause 后面是带缺口的从句，表示“……的事/东西”。语气上，that 更像陈述事实，what 更像指出具体内容。对比：I know that he left.（我知道他离开了）/ I know what he left.（我知道他留下了什么）

contrasts.title: vs whether/if-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，whether/if 表示“是否”，what 表示“什么东西/事情”。场景上，whether/if 用于不确定、二选一；what 用于内容本身。对比：I don't know whether he came. / I don't know what he brought.

contrasts.title: vs other wh-clauses
contrasts.note: 结构上，what 常指事物或内容； who 指人，where 指地点，why 指原因。语气和场景都类似，但问的空位不同。对比：I know what she wants. / I know why she left.

contrasts.title: vs noun + that/which clause
contrasts.note: what 不能先有明确先行词；如果前面已经有 a thing / the book 等名词，要用 that/which。场景上，what 更简洁口语化；名词+关系从句更明确。对比：Take what you need. / Take the book that you need.

examples.sentence: What I need is more time.
examples.translation: 我需要的是更多时间。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：What... is...

examples.sentence: I know what you mean.
examples.translation: 我知道你的意思。
examples.note: what-clause 放在动词 know 后作宾语。

examples.sentence: What he said surprised everyone.
examples.translation: 他说的话让大家都很惊讶。
examples.note: what-clause 在句首作主语。

examples.sentence: What matters most is honesty.
examples.translation: 最重要的是诚实。
examples.note: 常见于较书面或总结性的表达。

examples.sentence: I remember what she bought yesterday.
examples.translation: 我记得她昨天买了什么。
examples.note: 和疑问句不同，这里用陈述语序 she bought。

examples.sentence: Take what you need.
examples.translation: 拿你需要的东西。
examples.note: what 表示“你需要的东西”，不是单纯“是否需要”。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: 前半部分写“某人/某事……的内容”，后半部分写结果、定义或真正重点。
templates.exampleSentence: What we need is a clear plan.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们需要的是一个清晰的计划。

templates.label: 宾语位置模板
slotHints: 主句常用 know, remember, understand, say, explain；what 从句用陈述语序，不用倒装。
templates.exampleSentence: I understand what he wants.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我明白他想要什么。

templates.label: 写作概括模板
slotHints: 适合总结观点、强调重点；后面常接抽象名词，如 honesty, timing, communication。
templates.exampleSentence: What is important is clear communication.
templates.exampleTranslation: 重要的是清晰的沟通。

items.title: whether / if + clause
items.coreExplanation: whether / if + 从句用来表示“是否……”，把一般疑问句变成句子里的内容部分，常放在动词后面，如 know, ask, wonder, decide 后。后面要接完整陈述语序的从句，不用倒装：I know whether he is ready，不说 whether is he ready。
items.usageNote: 语气通常中性，口语里 if 很常见，whether 稍正式，也更适合写作。适合表达不知道、询问、讨论、决定等场景。不适合直接提问给对方；直接问要用 Is he ready? 这类句子。它常在主句动词后，也可放介词后或句首时更常用 whether。初学者先安全地用“主语 + know/ask/wonder + if/whether + 陈述句”模板。

slotSchema.label: 主句动词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示知道、询问、思考、决定的动词或短语，如 know, ask, wonder, decide。常错：后面直接接疑问句倒装。例：I wonder whether he will come.

slotSchema.label: 连接词
slotSchema.hint: 用 if 或 whether 表示“是否”。常错：在介词后还用 if。介词后改用 whether。例：We talked about whether it was safe.

slotSchema.label: 内容从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整陈述语序的小句，时态按意思决定。常错：写成 whether did he go。应为 whether he went。例：She asked if I needed help.

commonErrors: I don't know whether is he ready.｜错在从句用了疑问句倒装；宾语从句要用陈述语序。｜改为：I don't know whether he is ready.
She asked me whether did I finish it.｜错在 whether 从句里用了 did 倒装。｜改为：She asked me whether I finished it.
We talked about if we should leave.｜错在介词 about 后通常不用 if，应用 whether。｜改为：We talked about whether we should leave.
Whether or not he will come, I don't know if.｜错在 if 不能单独留在句尾代替完整从句。｜改为：I don't know whether or not he will come.
I asked whether to he go.｜错在把 to-infinitive 结构写坏了；不能说 to he go。｜改为：I asked whether he would go / I wondered whether to go.
Do you know whether he is ready? yes, I know whether he is ready.｜第二句意思不自然；know whether 强调“知道是否”，若已知答案，常直接说 know that / know he is ready。｜可改为：Yes, I know that he is ready.

contrasts.title: vs that-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，that + 从句表示“某事这个内容”，whether/if + 从句表示“是否”。语气上，that 更像陈述事实，whether/if 带不确定或二选一色彩。对比：I know that he is coming.（我知道他会来）/ I know whether he is coming.（我知道他是否会来）

contrasts.title: vs wh- clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，wh- 从句用 what/where/when/why/how 等，询问具体信息；whether/if 只问“是不是”。场景上，前者要具体答案，后者只要 yes/no。对比：I asked where he was. / I asked whether he was there.

contrasts.title: vs if 条件句
contrasts.note: 结构上，if 既可表示“是否”，也可表示“如果”。内容从句时常接在 know/ask/wonder 后；条件句表示条件关系。语气和场景不同。对比：I don't know if he is free.（是否）/ If he is free, we can go.（如果）

contrasts.title: whether vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者很多时候可互换，但 whether 更正式，也能用于介词后、to do 前、句首；这些位置 if 往往不自然或不对。对比：I’m not sure whether to wait.（对）/ I’m not sure if to wait.（错）

contrasts.title: vs plain whether/if
contrasts.note: 结构上，whether 可直接搭配 or not，if 通常不这样正式稳定。语气上 whether or not 更完整、更强调“不管结果哪一种”。对比：Tell me whether or not you agree. / Tell me if you agree.

examples.sentence: I don't know if he is busy.
examples.translation: 我不知道他是否忙。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：动词后接 if + 陈述语序从句。

examples.sentence: She asked whether I needed more time.
examples.translation: 她问我是否还需要更多时间。
examples.note: 转述别人的是/否问题，用 whether 很自然。

examples.sentence: We talked about whether the plan was realistic.
examples.translation: 我们讨论了这个计划是否现实。
examples.note: 介词 about 后优先用 whether，不用 if。

examples.sentence: Whether the policy will work remains unclear.
examples.translation: 这项政策是否有效仍不清楚。
examples.note: 句首位置更常用 whether，带一点书面感。

examples.sentence: I can't decide whether I should go now.
examples.translation: 我拿不定主意现在是否该走。
examples.note: 和 decide 连用，表达“是否要做”。

examples.sentence: Please let me know whether or not you can come.
examples.translation: 请告诉我你能不能来。
examples.note: whether or not 比 if 更完整，常见于通知、邮件。

templates.label: 初学者安全模板
slotHints: 主语用 I/you/she 等
主句动词先用 know, ask, wonder 最稳
从句一定用陈述语序，如 he is ready，不用 is he ready
templates.exampleSentence: I wonder whether she likes it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我想知道她是否喜欢它。

templates.label: 介词后模板
slotHints: 常见介词有 about, on
介词后用 whether，不要用 if
从句继续保持完整陈述语序
templates.exampleSentence: We argued about whether the price was fair.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们争论了这个价格是否合理。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
slotHints: 句首用 whether 更自然、更正式
逗号后接主句
适合写作或正式发言，不是最口语的说法
templates.exampleSentence: Whether the project succeeds is still uncertain.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个项目是否成功仍不确定。

templates.label: 强调两种结果模板
templates.template: Subject + tell/let someone know + whether or not + clause
slotHints: whether or not 表示“不管是哪一种结果都告诉我”
常用于邮件、通知、安排
不要把 if 放到 whether or not 的位置
templates.exampleSentence: Let me know whether or not you need a ride.
templates.exampleTranslation: 告诉我你是否需要搭车。

items.title: wh- clause after ask / know / tell
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构把特殊疑问句放进更大的句子里，表示“知道/问/告诉/解释……怎样、哪里、为什么”等信息。基本形式是动词+wh-从句，常见于ask, know, tell, explain, show后。注意从句里用陈述语序，不用疑问语序，通常放在主句动词后面。
items.usageNote: 语气一般是中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合提问转述、说明步骤、表达不知道、请求说明等场景。不适合用来提出直接问题；如果你是在直接问别人，就用正常问句。wh-从句通常紧跟在ask/know/tell等动词后，不要在从句里再加问号；整句只有在整句本身是问句时才加问号。初学者最安全的做法是先用“主语+动词+how/what/where...+主语+谓语”。

slotSchema.label: 主句动词
slotSchema.hint: 可用ask, know, tell, explain, show, remember等表示“问/知道/告诉/说明”的动词。常见错误是选不能自然接疑问信息的动词。例：I know ... / She explained ...

slotSchema.label: 疑问词
slotSchema.hint: 可用how, what, where, when, why, who等。常见错误是把whether/if当成wh-词；是否类问题要用whether/if。例：how to start / where he lives

slotSchema.label: wh-从句内容
slotSchema.hint: 放完整信息，通常用陈述语序：wh-词+主语+谓语，或wh-词+to do。常见错误是写成疑问语序。例：where she works，不是where does she work

slotSchema.label: 间接受词人
slotSchema.hint: tell, show等后面常先放人，再放wh-从句。常见错误是漏掉必要的人称对象。例：Please tell me how this works.

commonErrors: I know how does it work.｜错在嵌入问句里用了疑问语序。｜改为：I know how it works.
She asked where is the station.｜错在where从句仍然倒装。｜改为：She asked where the station was.
Can you tell me how can I open it?｜错在从句里又用了can I倒装。｜改为：Can you tell me how I can open it?
I don't know if to do.｜错在if不能这样直接接to do。｜改为：I don't know what to do. / I don't know whether I should do it.
He explained me how to use it.｜错在explain不能直接接人作双宾语。｜改为：He explained to me how to use it. 或 He showed me how to use it.
Please ask him that where he lives.｜错在that和wh-从句不能这样叠加。｜改为：Please ask him where he lives.

contrasts.title: whether / if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，wh-从句问“什么/哪里/怎样”等具体信息；whether/if只问“是否”。语气上，whether/if更像二选一或是非判断。对比：I know where he is.（我知道他在哪）/ I know whether he is here.（我知道他是否在这）

contrasts.title: what + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，what也属于wh-从句的一种，但what常直接充当从句里的宾语；how/where/why分别表示方式、地点、原因。场景上要按信息类型选词。对比：Tell me what he bought. / Tell me how he paid.

contrasts.title: 直接问句
contrasts.note: 结构上，直接问句用疑问语序；嵌入问句用陈述语序。场景上，直接问别人时更自然；放进大句子里转述或说明时用嵌入问句。对比：Where does he live? / I know where he lives.

contrasts.title: that-clause after think / say / know
contrasts.note: 结构上，that从句陈述一个事实或看法，不表达未知信息点；wh-从句带有“疑问词信息缺口”。语气上，that更像报告内容。对比：I know that he is busy. / I know why he is busy.

contrasts.title: wh- + to do
contrasts.note: 结构上，某些wh-从句可简化成wh- + to do，常在主语与动作执行者明确或泛指时使用。语气上更简洁，常见于说明、求助。对比：I don't know how I should start. / I don't know how to start.

examples.sentence: I know how it works.
examples.translation: 我知道它是怎么运作的。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的用法：know后接how从句，用陈述语序。

examples.sentence: Can you tell me where she lives?
examples.translation: 你能告诉我她住在哪里吗？
examples.note: 整句是问句，所以句末有问号；嵌入从句本身不用疑问语序。

examples.sentence: She explained how the system was designed.
examples.translation: 她解释了这个系统是如何设计的。
examples.note: 较常见于书面或说明语境。

examples.sentence: I know that he left, but I don't know why he left.
examples.translation: 我知道他离开了，但我不知道他为什么离开。
examples.note: 对比that从句与wh-从句：前者陈述事实，后者补充未知信息点。

examples.sentence: Please show me how to use this app.
examples.translation: 请告诉我这个应用怎么用。
examples.note: how to do是非常实用的初学者形式。

examples.sentence: We haven't decided where we will eat tonight.
examples.translation: 我们还没决定今晚在哪里吃饭。
examples.note: 嵌入问句也可放在decide后，表示未确定的信息。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: 主语用I/you/she等
动词可用know, ask, tell
wh-词按信息类型选how/where/why/what
从句一定用陈述语序，不要倒装
templates.exampleSentence: I know where he works.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我知道他在哪里工作。

templates.label: 带受词人的模板
templates.template: S + tell/show + 人 + wh- + S + V
slotHints: tell/show后常先放me/us/him等
wh-从句紧跟在人后面
从句里不要写does/do/is提前
templates.exampleSentence: Can you show me how this machine works?
templates.exampleTranslation: 你能给我演示这台机器怎么运作吗？

templates.label: 简洁实用模板
templates.template: S + don't know + wh- + to do
slotHints: 适合表达不知道该怎么做
常用how/what/where + to do
比完整从句更短，更适合初学者
templates.exampleSentence: I don't know what to say.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不知道该说什么。

templates.label: 书面说明模板
slotHints: 适合说明原因、过程、方式
explain后可直接接wh-从句
若要加人，用explain to + 人
templates.exampleSentence: The guide explains why the rule is important.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这份指南解释了这条规则为什么重要。

items.title: the fact that + clause
items.coreExplanation: the fact that + 从句 用来把后面的内容当作“一个事实/一个已知情况”来谈论。that后面必须是完整句子，整个短语在句中通常作主语、宾语或介词后成分，常见于句首或动词、介词后面。
items.usageNote: 这个结构语气偏中性到较正式，常见于议论文、说明文、学术写作、讨论原因影响时。口语里也能用，但太日常的小事常直接说从句，更自然。它后面不用逗号隔开that从句；放句首时，整个短语后面再接谓语。初学者最安全的做法是先用 The fact that... shows/means/is... 造句。

slotSchema.label: that从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整陈述句，要有主语和谓语，如 that he was late。常见错误是只放短语或疑问语序，如 that why he was late。小例：the fact that she apologized

slotSchema.label: 主句谓语
slotSchema.hint: 常接 shows, means, explains, proves, suggests, is 等，用来评论这个事实。常见错误是 fact后面没有主句。小例：The fact that sales fell shows...

slotSchema.label: 句中位置
slotSchema.hint: 可放句首作主语，也可放在介词后或作宾语，如 despite the fact that... / ignore the fact that...。常见错误是位置对了但后面结构不完整。小例：We ignored the fact that...

commonErrors: × the fact that why he left is unclear.｜错因：that后面要接正常陈述句，不能再接why引导的疑问结构。｜改法：the fact that he left is unclear / the reason why he left is unclear.
× the fact of that he left surprised us.｜错因：固定说法是 the fact that，不加 of。｜改法：the fact that he left surprised us.
× The fact that he left, surprised us.｜错因：主语内部不要在that从句后随便加逗号。｜改法：The fact that he left surprised us.
× I think the fact that.｜错因：the fact that 后面必须有完整从句，不能停在that。｜改法：I think the fact that he apologized matters.
× Because the fact that he was sick, he stayed home.｜错因：because后面直接接从句更自然，the fact that不这样搭配。｜改法：Because he was sick, he stayed home. / The fact that he was sick explains why he stayed home.
× The fact that he is honest, so I trust him.｜错因：一个句子里不能把主语结构和so连接成两个主句的混合结构。｜改法：The fact that he is honest is why I trust him. / He is honest, so I trust him.

contrasts.title: vs that-clause after verbs
contrasts.note: 结构上，verb + that从句 直接跟在 think/know/say 后；the fact that + 从句 是名词短语。语气上前者像“认为/知道”，后者像“把内容当事实来讨论”，更客观。对比：I know that he is busy. / The fact that he is busy doesn't change the deadline.

contrasts.title: vs noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，idea/belief/claim that... 里的名词可变；the fact that... 里的 fact 基本固定。语气上 fact 表示“已知事实”，而 idea/belief/claim 不一定真实。对比：the belief that luck matters / the fact that luck mattered little.

contrasts.title: vs the reason is that
contrasts.note: 结构上，the reason is that + 从句 用来回答“为什么”；the fact that + 从句 用来指出“什么情况是真的”。场景上前者重原因说明，后者重把内容当论点。对比：The reason is that he was tired. / The fact that he was tired was obvious.

contrasts.title: vs because + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，because直接引原因状语从句；the fact that... 是名词结构，可作主语宾语。语气上 because更直接口语，the fact that更书面、更适合分析。对比：We left because it was late. / The fact that it was late influenced our decision.

contrasts.title: vs what + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，what + 从句 常表示“……的事情/东西”；the fact that + 从句 明确表示“这个事实”。语气上 what 更泛，fact 更强调真实性和讨论性。对比：What he said was surprising. / The fact that he said it was surprising.

examples.sentence: The fact that she passed the exam surprised everyone.
examples.translation: 她通过了考试这一事实让所有人都很惊讶。
examples.note: 最基础用法：整个短语作主语。

examples.sentence: We cannot ignore the fact that many students need more time.
examples.translation: 我们不能忽视很多学生需要更多时间这一事实。
examples.note: 放在动词后作宾语。

examples.sentence: Despite the fact that it was raining, they continued the game.
examples.translation: 尽管当时在下雨，他们还是继续了比赛。
examples.note: 固定搭配 despite the fact that，表示让步。

examples.sentence: The fact that prices are rising means families are under pressure.
examples.translation: 物价正在上涨这一事实意味着家庭正承受压力。
examples.note: 适合分析、写作场景。

examples.sentence: I was upset by the fact that he didn't call me back.
examples.translation: 他没有给我回电话这件事让我很难受。
examples.note: 口语也能用，但比直接说 he didn't call me back 更郑重。

examples.sentence: The fact that the results were consistent strengthens the argument.
examples.translation: 结果一致这一事实增强了这个论点。
examples.note: 典型书面表达。

templates.label: 安全初学者模板
slotHints: [fact_clause] 放完整陈述句，如 he was late；[main_verb] 可用 shows/means/proves/is；[result] 放你的结论或评价。
templates.exampleSentence: The fact that he was late shows that he needs better time management.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他迟到这一事实说明他需要更好的时间管理。

templates.label: 宾语位置模板
slotHints: 适合表达“不能忽视某个事实”；[fact_clause] 仍然必须是完整句子。
templates.exampleSentence: We cannot ignore the fact that online classes are difficult for some students.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们不能忽视线上课程对一些学生来说很难这一事实。

templates.label: 让步位置模板
slotHints: 用于“尽管……”；后面主句写结果。不要写成 despite of the fact that。
templates.exampleSentence: Despite the fact that she was tired, she finished the report.
templates.exampleTranslation: 尽管她很累，她还是完成了报告。

templates.label: 写作表达模板
slotHints: 适合议论文和分析文；suggests that 后写较谨慎的结论。
templates.exampleSentence: The fact that fewer people are applying suggests that the policy is unpopular.
templates.exampleTranslation: 申请人数减少这一事实表明这项政策不受欢迎。

items.title: it is + adjective + that...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来对“某件事/一个情况”做评价或表态，如重要、奇怪、明显。基本形式是 It is + 形容词 + that + 完整句子。that 后面必须是完整从句，it 放句首作形式主语，真正内容通常放在后面。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，常见于说明、议论文、报告、课堂表达，也能用于日常较完整的发言。它适合评价一件事本身，不适合直接说人的动作计划；那时常用 it is + adj + to do。that 从句通常放在句末；口语里 that 有时可省，但初学者先保留 that 最安全。句号前不要再插入多余连接词。

slotSchema.label: 评价形容词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示评价或立场的形容词，如 important, clear, strange, possible。常见错误：用成副词，如 importantly。例：It is important that we act now.

slotSchema.label: that 从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整句子，要有主语和谓语。常见错误：只放短语或动词原形。例：that he was late / that we need more time。

slotSchema.label: that
slotSchema.hint: 固定用 that 引出内容。口语可偶尔省略，但初学阶段建议保留。常见错误：误换成 what。例：It is obvious that she knows the answer.

commonErrors: It is important that to finish the work today.｜错因：that 后面不能直接接 to do，不是完整从句。｜改法：It is important to finish the work today. / It is important that we finish the work today.
It is clearly that he is wrong.｜错因：这里要用形容词，不用副词 clearly。｜改法：It is clear that he is wrong.
It is important what we finish the work today.｜错因：引导评价内容时这里不用 what。｜改法：It is important that we finish the work today.
Is important that we leave now.｜错因：缺少形式主语 it。｜改法：It is important that we leave now.
I think it is important that.｜错因：that 后面缺完整内容，从句不完整。｜改法：I think it is important that everyone arrives on time.
It is important that everyone on time.｜错因：that 从句缺谓语。｜改法：It is important that everyone is on time.

contrasts.title: vs. it is + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：that 后接完整句子；to 后接动词原形。场景区别：that 更适合评价一个事实或情况，语气常更书面；to 更适合说一般行为。对比：It is important that we wear masks. / It is important to wear masks.

contrasts.title: vs. think/say/know + that clause
contrasts.note: 结构区别：本结构是 It is + 形容词 + that...；另一类是动词直接接 that 从句。语气区别：本结构突出“评价”，think/say 只是说明“谁认为/说”。对比：I think that he is right. / It is clear that he is right.

contrasts.title: vs. noun + that clause
contrasts.note: 结构区别：这里用形容词作评价；另一类用 fact, idea, belief 等名词承载内容。场景区别：It is adj that 更像即时判断；the fact that 更像点出一个名词化事实。对比：It is surprising that he left early. / The fact that he left early surprised me.

contrasts.title: vs. whether/if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构区别：that 陈述事实；whether/if 表示“是否”的问题。场景区别：评价已知/陈述内容用 that；讨论不确定选择用 whether。对比：It is clear that she lied. / It is unclear whether she lied.

examples.sentence: It is important that we finish the report today.
examples.translation: 我们今天完成这份报告是很重要的。
examples.note: 最安全基础句：形容词后接 that 完整从句。

examples.sentence: I think it is strange that he didn't reply.
examples.translation: 我觉得他没有回复很奇怪。
examples.note: 可放在更大句子中，不一定总在整句最前面。

examples.sentence: It is clear that the policy has improved public safety.
examples.translation: 很明显，这项政策提升了公共安全。
examples.note: 常见于说明文、报告、议论文。

examples.sentence: It is important that students ask questions, not just listen quietly.
examples.translation: 学生提出问题而不只是安静听讲，这一点很重要。
examples.note: 适合与别的表达对比，突出“评价一个情况”。

examples.sentence: It is surprising that she knows my name.
examples.translation: 她知道我的名字，这很令人惊讶。
examples.note: 日常可用，但语气比简单口语稍完整。

examples.sentence: It is possible that the train will be delayed.
examples.translation: 火车可能会晚点。
examples.note: 表示判断或可能性时也很自然。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
slotHints: [adjective] 放 important/clear/strange/possible 等评价词；[clause] 放完整句子，如 we need more time
templates.exampleSentence: It is obvious that we need more time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 很明显，我们还需要更多时间。

templates.label: 嵌入主句模板
slotHints: 前面加 I think 可让语气更自然；that 从句仍要完整
templates.exampleSentence: I think it is unfair that only one team gets extra help.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我认为只有一个队得到额外帮助是不公平的。

templates.label: 写作常用模板
slotHints: 先评价，再补原因；because 后面再写完整原因句
templates.exampleSentence: It is important that children read daily, because regular practice builds vocabulary.
templates.exampleTranslation: 孩子每天阅读很重要，因为规律练习能积累词汇。

templates.label: 比较提醒模板
slotHints: 当你在评价“某个具体事实”时优先用 that；若只是说一般行为，可考虑 it is adj to do
templates.exampleSentence: It is strange that the store is closed today.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这家店今天关门很奇怪。

items.title: who relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来在名词后面补充“这个人做什么/是什么样的人”，帮助你准确指明或描述某个人。基本形式是“表示人的名词 + who + 动词成分…”。who 后面通常接完整的小句，而且 who 在从句里常作主语。它一般紧跟在被修饰的人后面，放在句子中间或句尾。
items.usageNote: 语气通常是中性，口语和写作都很常见。适合介绍人、讲故事、说明“哪一个人”。不适合修饰物、地点，也不用于单独成句。逗号很重要：不给逗号时常表示“限定是哪一个人”，加逗号时多是补充说明。初学者最安全的做法是先用“the person/student/man who + 动词...”造句，不要在 who 后面再重复主语。

slotSchema.label: 被修饰的人
slotSchema.hint: 放表示人的名词，如 the girl, my friend, a teacher。常见错误：用物作先行词，如 the book who。应改为 the person/girl who… 或用 which/that。例：the teacher who helped me

slotSchema.label: who 后面的从句
slotSchema.hint: 通常放动词成分，who 常在从句里作主语，所以后面直接接动词，不要再加 he/she。常见错误：the boy who he runs。应改为 the boy who runs。例：who lives next door

slotSchema.label: 主句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 整个“名词 + who 从句”之后，再接主句内容。常见错误：相对从句写完就停，句子不完整。例：The man who called you is outside.

commonErrors: the book who is on the table：who 只能修饰人，不能修饰物；改为 the book that/which is on the table。
the boy who he plays basketball：who 已经在从句里代表 the boy，再加 he 属于主语重复；改为 the boy who plays basketball。
I met a man who yesterday at the station：who 后面缺少清楚的动词结构，信息顺序也别扭；改为 I met a man who was waiting at the station yesterday。
My teacher, who teaches us math is kind：非限定性从句前有逗号时，后面也要用逗号与主句隔开；改为 My teacher, who teaches us math, is kind。
The person who I saw him yesterday is my uncle：从句里已经有宾语 who/whom 位置的信息，又加 him 重复；改为 The person who I saw yesterday is my uncle。
She is who helps me a lot：who relative clause 不能单独放在 be 后面直接代替表语名词；改为 She is the one who helps me a lot 或 She is a person who helps me a lot。

contrasts.title: who vs that
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可修饰人，但 who 更明确表示“人”，that 更通用。语气上，who 更自然、更常用于介绍人物；that 稍更口语或更压缩。对比：the woman who called / the woman that called。说人时初学者优先用 who。

contrasts.title: who vs which
contrasts.note: 结构上，who 修饰人，which 修饰物或整件事。场景上，混用会直接出错。对比：the girl who sings 很自然；the song which she sings 修饰的是歌，不是人。记住：人 who，物 which。

contrasts.title: who vs whose + noun
contrasts.note: 结构上，who 后接从句并描述“这个人做什么”；whose 后面先接名词，再说明“这个人的某个东西”。语义上，who 讲人本身，whose 讲所属关系。对比：the student who studies hard / the student whose bag is blue。

contrasts.title: who vs where
contrasts.note: 结构上，who 接在人后，where 接在地点后。场景上，一个描述人，一个描述地方。最小对比：the man who works here / the office where he works。不要说 the office who…。

contrasts.title: no comma vs comma + who
contrasts.note: 结构上，不加逗号常用来限定“哪一个人”；加逗号则是补充信息。语气上，不加逗号更像识别对象，加逗号更像顺带说明。对比：The student who won the prize is my friend. / Tom, who won the prize, is my friend.

examples.sentence: The girl who is wearing a red hat is my sister.
examples.translation: 戴红帽子的那个女孩是我妹妹。
examples.note: 最基础用法：who 后直接接动词短语，说明这个人。

examples.sentence: I talked to a teacher who works at our school.
examples.translation: 我和一位在我们学校工作的老师聊了聊。
examples.note: 相对从句放在名词后，整个名词短语位于句中。

examples.sentence: The man who lives next door is very friendly.
examples.translation: 住在隔壁的那位男人很友好。
examples.note: 日常介绍邻居时很自然。

examples.sentence: Students who need extra help can stay after class.
examples.translation: 需要额外帮助的学生可以下课后留下。
examples.note: 写作里常用来概括一类人；这里不加逗号，表示限定。

examples.sentence: My uncle, who works in Beijing, is visiting us this week.
examples.translation: 我叔叔在北京工作，他这周要来看我们。
examples.note: 带逗号，属于补充说明，不是用来区分“哪一个叔叔”。

examples.sentence: The student who won the prize is different from the student whose project got praised.
examples.translation: 获奖的那个学生和项目受到表扬的那个学生不是同一个人。
examples.note: 用来对比 who 与 whose：前者描述人做了什么，后者描述人的所属事物。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: 先用明确的人类名词；who 后直接接动词，不要再加 he/she。
templates.exampleSentence: The student who studies hard will improve quickly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 学习努力的学生会进步很快。

templates.label: 句中位置模板
templates.template: I met/talked to/saw + a person + who + verb ...
slotHints: 适合说“我遇到/看到/聊到的那个人”；相对从句紧跟在 person 后。
templates.exampleSentence: I saw a woman who was crying.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我看到一位正在哭的女士。

templates.label: 写作概括模板
slotHints: 适合写作中概括一类人；通常不用逗号，表示限定这一类人。
templates.exampleSentence: People who exercise regularly often sleep better.
templates.exampleTranslation: 经常锻炼的人通常睡得更好。

templates.label: 补充说明模板
templates.template: Name/kinship term, who + verb ..., + main clause
slotHints: 用于已知人物的补充说明；注意前后两个逗号都要有。
templates.exampleSentence: My father, who loves cooking, made dinner tonight.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我爸爸很喜欢做饭，今晚他做了晚餐。

items.title: which relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用which把前面的事物、事情或想法和后面的说明连起来，意思是“……的那个/那些……”。基本形式是“先行词+which+从句”。which一般放在它修饰的名词后面，常见于句中，也可出现在主语或宾语名词后继续补充信息。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，口语能用，但在说明、定义、写作、介绍物品特点时特别自然。适合修饰“物、事、想法、情况”，不适合直接修饰“人”，也不用于单独另起一句。初学者先安全使用“名词+which+动词……”。注意which前通常没有逗号时是在限定这个名词；若前面有逗号，意思会变成补充说明，语气和信息重点不同。

slotSchema.label: 先行词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示物、事、想法的名词，如 the book, an idea, a system。常见错误：用人作先行词却还用which。应改who/that。例：the machine which saves time

slotSchema.label: which后面的从句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个缺少“这个事物”位置的从句，which在从句里可作主语或宾语。常见错误：which后面再重复同一个名词或代词。例：which works well / which I bought yesterday

slotSchema.label: 主句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 可在整个“名词+which从句”后继续完成主句。常见错误：把定语从句单独当完整句。例：The app which I use every day is free.

commonErrors: wrong pattern: the person which helped me；why wrong: which通常不用来修饰人；how to fix it: 改为the person who helped me或the person that helped me。
wrong pattern: the place which I was born；why wrong: 表示“在这个地方”时更自然用where，which前通常要有介词；how to fix it: 改为the place where I was born或the place in which I was born。
wrong pattern: the book which it is on the table；why wrong: which已代表the book，从句里又用it重复；how to fix it: 改为the book which is on the table。
wrong pattern: This is a phone. Which is very useful.；why wrong: 把定语从句拆成独立句不成立；how to fix it: 改为This is a phone which is very useful. 若想另起句，要用It is very useful.或This phone is very useful.
wrong pattern: the idea which about we talked；why wrong: 介词位置错误；how to fix it: 改为the idea which we talked about，或较正式的the idea about which we talked。
wrong pattern: the car which is red one；why wrong: 从句结构不完整，red前后搭配错误；how to fix it: 改为the car which is red，或the red car。

contrasts.title: which vs that
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可修饰物：noun + which/that + clause。语气上which稍正式、书面感更强，that更常见于日常口语。比较：the book which I bought yesterday（较书面）/ the book that I bought yesterday（更口语自然）。

contrasts.title: which vs who
contrasts.note: 结构区别是which修饰物或事，who修饰人。场景上说人物身份、动作时必须优先用who。对比：the teacher who helped me，不说the teacher which helped me；the tool which helped me则正确，因为tool是物。

contrasts.title: which vs where
contrasts.note: 结构上where直接表示“在……的地方”；which修饰地点名词时常要配介词。语感上where更自然、更简洁。对比：the room where I study = the room in which I study；初学者见到“地方+在里面/在那里做事”优先用where。

contrasts.title: 限定用法 vs 非限定用法
contrasts.note: 结构上无逗号的which定语从句用于限定“哪一个”；有逗号的, which...是补充说明。语气上后者更书面。对比：The car which is outside is mine.（外面的那辆）/ My car, which is outside, is mine.（顺便补充：我的车在外面）

contrasts.title: which vs whose
contrasts.note: 结构上which直接连接说明从句；whose后面必须接名词，表示所属关系。场景上谈“这个东西的某个部分/属性”时常用whose。对比：a company which makes software（公司做软件）/ a company whose products are popular（公司的产品很受欢迎）。

examples.sentence: I need a book which explains this grammar clearly.
examples.translation: 我需要一本把这个语法讲清楚的书。
examples.note: 最基础用法：which作从句主语，修饰book。

examples.sentence: The app which I use every day is free.
examples.translation: 我每天用的那个应用是免费的。
examples.note: which作从句宾语，口语里也很常见。

examples.sentence: We proposed a system which reduces waste in small offices.
examples.translation: 我们提出了一个能减少小型办公室浪费的系统。
examples.note: 较适合说明、报告、写作。

examples.sentence: This is the place where I study, not the room which stores old files.
examples.translation: 这是我学习的地方，不是存放旧文件的那个房间。
examples.note: 用where和which对比：地点作“在……做事”时where更自然；房间作主语时which可用。

examples.sentence: She showed me a machine which can cut vegetables very quickly.
examples.translation: 她给我看了一台能很快切菜的机器。
examples.note: 描述物品功能，非常自然。

examples.sentence: The report, which was finished yesterday, will be sent this afternoon.
examples.translation: 这份报告昨天已经完成，今天下午会被发送。
examples.note: 逗号后是补充说明，语气较书面。

templates.label: 安全基础模板
slotHints: [headNoun] 放物品或抽象名词，如 book, tool, idea；[relativeClause] 用动词开头，说明这个东西做什么，如 explains this well
templates.exampleSentence: I need a tool which saves time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我需要一个能节省时间的工具。

templates.label: 主语位置模板
slotHints: 前半部分整个作主语；[complement] 可放形容词或名词短语，如 useful, expensive, a good choice
templates.exampleSentence: The phone which I bought last week is expensive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我上周买的那部手机很贵。

templates.label: 写作用模板
slotHints: 适合介绍产品、方法、系统；从句里常用 improves, reduces, helps, allows 等动词
templates.exampleSentence: We developed a system which reduces costs.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们开发了一个能降低成本的系统。

templates.label: 补充说明模板
slotHints: 前面逗号表示附加信息，不是用来区分哪一个；更适合书面语
templates.exampleSentence: The report, which was checked twice, is ready.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这份报告已经检查过两次，准备好了。

items.title: that relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用that把后面的从句接到前面的名词上，用来“限定并说明是哪一个人/东西”。基本形式是名词+that+从句；that后面要接完整谓语部分，但从句里会缺主语或宾语。它通常紧跟在被修饰名词后面，不单独放句首。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和普通写作都很常见，适合介绍、辨认、定义某个人或东西，如the book that I bought。它用于限制性信息：去掉后意思会变得不清楚。一般不用逗号把that从句隔开；非限制性补充说明不用that。初学者最稳妥的做法是先写“the+noun+that+主语+动词”。

slotSchema.label: 被修饰名词
slotSchema.hint: 放需要被进一步说明的人或物，如the book, the girl, the food。常见错误是直接用代词如it/he后接that从句；更自然是先用名词。例：the movie that we watched

slotSchema.label: that后面的从句
slotSchema.hint: 放一个带谓语的从句，用来说明前面的名词。从句里通常缺主语或宾语之一。常见错误是把句子写得完全不缺成分。例：that I like / that is on the table

slotSchema.label: that代表的成分
slotSchema.hint: that可以代表从句中的主语或宾语。常见错误是主语位置还再加一个he/she/it。例：the man that helped me；the cake that I made

commonErrors: wrong: the book that I bought it；错因：that已经代替从句中的宾语，不能再加it。改法：the book that I bought。
wrong: my father, that lives in Beijing, is a doctor；错因：that一般不用在非限制性定语从句里，逗号也不对。改法：my father, who lives in Beijing, is a doctor。
wrong: the person which helped me；错因：指人时that或who自然，which通常指物。改法：the person who helped me / the person that helped me。
wrong: the place that I went to there；错因：关系从句里不要重复地点副词there。改法：the place that I went to。
wrong: the thing that what I need；错因：that和what功能重复，不能并用。改法：the thing that I need / what I need。
wrong: that book is on the table；错因：这里that是指示词“那本”，不是定语从句。改法：若要用本语法，说the book that is on the table。

contrasts.title: that vs who
contrasts.note: 结构上，两者都可修饰人；who更明确指人，that可指人也可指物。语气上，who稍正式、书面更常见；口语里that很自然。对比：the man who called me / the man that called me。

contrasts.title: that vs which
contrasts.note: 结构上，which主要指物，that也可指物。语气上，that在口语和限制性从句里很常见；which在较正式写作里更多。对比：the book that I bought / the book which I bought。

contrasts.title: that限制性 vs 非限制性who/which
contrasts.note: 结构上，that通常不用在带逗号的补充说明从句中；非限制性多用who/which。语气上，非限制性像“顺便补充”。对比：the car that I bought is red / my car, which I bought last year, is red。

contrasts.title: that vs whose
contrasts.note: 结构上，that直接代替人或物本身；whose后面必须接名词，表示“某人的/某物的”。场景上，想表达所属关系时不能只用that。对比：the boy that called me / the boy whose phone rang。

contrasts.title: that vs where
contrasts.note: 结构上，修饰地点时可用where，或that+从句（常保留介词）。语气上，where更直接自然；that结构更基础。对比：the place where I work / the place that I work in。

examples.sentence: This is the book that I bought yesterday.
examples.translation: 这是我昨天买的那本书。
examples.note: 最安全的基础型：that在从句中作宾语。

examples.sentence: The man that helped me was very kind.
examples.translation: 帮助我的那个人很亲切。
examples.note: that在从句中作主语，此时后面直接接动词。

examples.sentence: I can't find the keys that were on the table.
examples.translation: 我找不到刚才在桌上的那串钥匙了。
examples.note: 先主句，后名词+that从句，是非常常见的位置。

examples.sentence: Students need books that explain grammar clearly.
examples.translation: 学生需要能清楚讲解语法的书。
examples.note: 较适合说明性写作，表示“哪一类书”。

examples.sentence: The movie that we watched was better than the one we saw last week.
examples.translation: 我们看的那部电影比上周看的那部更好。
examples.note: 适合和其他对象比较，突出“具体是哪一部”。

examples.sentence: Do you know a cafe that stays open late?
examples.translation: 你知道有哪家营业到很晚的咖啡馆吗？
examples.note: 用于寻找符合条件的人或物。

templates.label: 最稳妥基础模板
templates.template: the + 名词 + that + 主语 + 动词
slotHints: 先写一个具体名词，如book, food, movie
that后写完整谓语部分，但不要把前面名词再重复成宾语
templates.exampleSentence: the cake that I made
templates.exampleTranslation: 我做的那个蛋糕

templates.label: that作主语模板
templates.template: the + 名词 + that + 动词 + 其他
slotHints: 用于that在从句里当主语时
不要再加he/she/it等重复主语
templates.exampleSentence: the girl that lives next door
templates.exampleTranslation: 住在隔壁的那个女孩

templates.label: 句中常用模板
templates.template: 主句 + the + 名词 + that + 从句
slotHints: 先说主要动作，再补充说明是哪一个人或物
that从句紧跟名词，不要隔太远
templates.exampleSentence: I lost the phone that my sister gave me.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我把我妹妹送我的那部手机弄丢了。

templates.label: 写作用定义模板
templates.template: 名词复数 + need/use/like + 名词 + that + 从句
slotHints: 适合写“什么样的……才合适”
常用于定义类别或提出要求
templates.exampleSentence: Children need games that are easy to understand.
templates.exampleTranslation: 孩子们需要容易理解的游戏。

items.title: whose + noun
items.coreExplanation: whose 用来把“拥有者”和“被拥有的东西”连进同一个名词短语，意思接近“其……的/他的……的”。基本结构是：先说人或物，再接 whose + 名词 + 其余从句。它通常紧跟在被修饰名词后面，放在句子中间作定语，也可出现在句首主语里。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都能用，但在正式写作里尤其常见，因为它比拆成两个句子更紧凑。适合介绍人物、说明物品特征、下定义；不适合没有“所属关系”时硬用。whose 后面一般必须立刻接名词，不能只写 whose。关系从句通常紧贴先行词，不要被逗号或别的成分隔太远。初学者最稳妥的做法是先套用：the person/the company/the house whose + 名词 + 谓语。

slotSchema.label: 被修饰的名词
slotSchema.hint: 放你要说明的人或物，如 the student, a company, the house。常错：先行词和后面所属关系不搭。例：the girl whose bike was stolen

slotSchema.label: 所属的名词
slotSchema.hint: 放属于前面人或物的东西，如 name, car, roof, policy。常错：whose 后面不接名词，写成 whose is...。例：whose name I forgot

slotSchema.label: 后半个从句
slotSchema.hint: 写关于 ownedNoun 的动作或状态，通常要有谓语。常错：从句不完整，只剩一个名词。例：whose car broke down yesterday

commonErrors: × the man whose is wearing a hat；错因：whose 后必须接名词，不能直接接 be/动词。✓ the man who is wearing a hat；如果要表示所属：the man whose son is wearing a hat。
× a company whose very famous；错因：whose 后缺少所属名词，而且从句没有正确主语。✓ a company that is very famous；或：a company whose products are very famous。
× the student whose I met yesterday；错因：这里需要宾语关系，不是所属关系。✓ the student whom/who/that I met yesterday。
× the house whose roof red；错因：从句缺谓语，不能只放形容词。✓ the house whose roof is red。
× the teacher whose his class is interesting；错因：whose 已经表示“他的”，后面不能再加 his。✓ the teacher whose class is interesting。
× I know a girl, whose brother lives in Canada.；错因：限定性关系从句中逗号通常不加，逗号会改变语气。✓ I know a girl whose brother lives in Canada.

contrasts.title: vs who relative clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，who 后直接接动词或完整从句；whose 后必须先接名词。语义上，who 讲“这个人做什么”，whose 讲“这个人有什么/谁的……”。对比：the girl who called me / the girl whose phone rang。

contrasts.title: vs that relative clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，that 不表示所属，后面通常直接接主语缺失或宾语缺失的从句；whose 明确表示“……的”。语气上 that 更普通，whose 更适合精确描写。对比：the car that hit the tree / the car whose door was broken。

contrasts.title: vs of which
contrasts.note: 结构上，whose + 名词 更自然简洁；of which 常是 the + 名词 + of which，多见正式书面语，主要修饰物。语体上 of which 更正式、更书面。对比：a house whose roof collapsed / a house the roof of which collapsed。

contrasts.title: vs where relative clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，where 修饰地点并表示“在那个地方”；whose 表示所属，不强调地点。场景上，where 常讲发生地点，whose 常讲地点拥有的部分。对比：the school where I studied / the school whose library is huge。

examples.sentence: I met a woman whose son studies in Beijing.
examples.translation: 我遇到了一位女士，她的儿子在北京读书。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：先行词是人，whose 后接家属名词。

examples.sentence: The student whose phone rang apologized to the class.
examples.translation: 手机响了的那个学生向全班道了歉。
examples.note: whose 从句紧跟先行词 the student，整个短语作主语。

examples.sentence: The company whose products are sold worldwide plans to expand.
examples.translation: 那家产品销往全球的公司计划扩张。
examples.note: 常见于说明文和新闻写作。

examples.sentence: This is the house whose roof was damaged, not the one that was painted last year.
examples.translation: 这是那座屋顶受损的房子，不是去年粉刷的那座。
examples.note: 对比 whose 和 that：前者讲所属部分，后者讲动作。

examples.sentence: Do you know the girl whose bike was stolen yesterday?
examples.translation: 你认识昨天自行车被偷的那个女孩吗？
examples.note: 日常口语中也很自然，尤其在介绍人时。

examples.sentence: We visited a village whose history goes back hundreds of years.
examples.translation: 我们参观了一个有数百年历史的村庄。
examples.note: 先行词也可以是物或地点，不只限于人。

templates.label: 最稳妥基础模板
templates.template: the/person/thing + whose + 名词 + 谓语
slotHints: 先放你要描述的人或物
whose 后立刻放所属名词，如 son, name, door
后面写这个所属名词发生了什么
templates.exampleSentence: the woman whose son studies in Beijing
templates.exampleTranslation: 儿子在北京读书的那位女士

templates.label: 作主语的模板
templates.template: [先行词 + whose + 名词 + 谓语] + 主句谓语
slotHints: 把整个含 whose 的短语当主语
主句再接主要动作
注意 whose 从句不要写成独立句
templates.exampleSentence: The student whose phone rang apologized.
templates.exampleTranslation: 手机响了的那个学生道歉了。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: the + 物/机构/地点 + whose + 名词 + be/动词 + 其他
slotHints: 适合公司、建筑、城市、学校等
所属名词常用 products, roof, population, library 等
让句子更紧凑，少拆成两个句子
templates.exampleSentence: the company whose products are sold worldwide
templates.exampleTranslation: 产品销往全球的那家公司

items.title: where relative clause
items.coreExplanation: where relative clause 用来在一个地点名词后面补充“在那个地方发生什么”。基本结构是 place + where + 完整分句；where 后通常要有主语和动词。它最常紧跟在地点词后，放在句中修饰前面的 place，如 the cafe where we met.
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面都很常见，适合介绍城市、学校、房间、网站、国家等“地点”或把活动看作发生地点的词。一般不用于纯时间词；时间要优先考虑 when。where 从句通常紧贴它修饰的地点词，不要随便隔开。逗号前后会影响信息类型，但初学者先安全使用不加逗号的限定性结构：the place where....

slotSchema.label: 地点名词
slotSchema.hint: 可放具体地点或被当作地点的名词，如 place, city, room, cafe, school。常错：放时间词如 day。例：the park

slotSchema.label: where后分句
slotSchema.hint: 要放完整分句，通常含主语+动词，表示在那个地方发生的事。常错：只放短语。例：we had a picnic

slotSchema.label: 主句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 可在整个名词短语后接主句。常错：把 where 从句单独当完整句。例：is beautiful

commonErrors: the place where we went to there is beautiful.｜错因：where 已经表示“在那个地方”，不能再加 there。｜改法：the place where we went is beautiful.
I remember the day where we met.｜错因：day 是时间词，通常不用 where。｜改法：I remember the day when we met.
This is the room where very quiet.｜错因：where 后缺少完整分句，只有形容词。｜改法：This is the room where I study, 或 This room is very quiet.
The cafe we met where is closed.｜错因：where 从句位置错了，必须紧跟被修饰的地点词。｜改法：The cafe where we met is closed.
The city where I was born in is large.｜错因：where 与 in 重复表达地点。｜改法：The city where I was born is large. 或 The city in which I was born is large.
This is where place I work.｜错因：where 不能直接放在 place 前面这样连用。｜改法：This is the place where I work. 或 This is where I work.

contrasts.title: where vs which
contrasts.note: 结构上，where = in/at/on which 的意思，专门连地点；which 本身不带地点介词。语气上 where 更自然、更口语，in which 更书面。比较：the house where I grew up = the house in which I grew up；但不能说 the house which I grew up。

contrasts.title: where vs when
contrasts.note: 结构上 where 修饰地点名词，when 修饰时间名词。场景上说地点用 where，说日期、时代、时刻用 when。比较：the summer when we met；the town where we met。不要把 day/month/year 和 where 混用。

contrasts.title: where vs that
contrasts.note: 结构上 that 后面不能直接表示“在那个地方”，常需要保留介词或省略关系词后的完整搭配；where 直接表示地点。口语里 where 更省事。比较：the cafe where we met；the cafe that we met in。前者更适合初学者。

contrasts.title: where clause vs two simple sentences
contrasts.note: 结构上 where 从句把两句压成一个名词短语，更紧凑；两句简单句更分散。书面和描述性表达里 where 更自然。比较：I visited the village where my grandfather lived. vs I visited the village. My grandfather lived there.

examples.sentence: This is the cafe where we met.
examples.translation: 这就是我们见面的那家咖啡馆。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：地点名词 + where + 完整分句。

examples.sentence: The house where she grew up is on this street.
examples.translation: 她长大的那栋房子就在这条街上。
examples.note: where 从句紧跟 house，整个名词短语再作主语。

examples.sentence: Paris is a city where old and new exist together.
examples.translation: 巴黎是一座新旧并存的城市。
examples.note: 较常见于写作和介绍性文字。

examples.sentence: This is the day when we met, not the place where we met.
examples.translation: 这是我们见面的那一天，不是我们见面的那个地方。
examples.note: 对比 when 和 where 的典型用法。

examples.sentence: Do you know a park where we can have a picnic?
examples.translation: 你知道一个我们可以野餐的公园吗？
examples.note: 日常问路/找地点时很自然。

examples.sentence: The website where I booked the tickets was easy to use.
examples.translation: 我订票的那个网站很好用。
