examples.note: website 也可被当作“使用发生的地方”。

templates.label: 新手最稳模板
slotHints: PLACE 放地点名词，如 park/cafe/school
SUBJECT + VERB 放完整分句，如 we met / I study
templates.exampleSentence: the school where I study
templates.exampleTranslation: 我就读的学校

templates.label: 句中作主语模板
templates.template: The + PLACE + where + SUBJECT + VERB + is/looks/seems + ...
slotHints: 前半部分是被修饰的地点
后面接主句评价或描述
templates.exampleSentence: The room where I work is very quiet.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我工作的房间很安静。

templates.label: 写作介绍模板
templates.template: X is a PLACE where + SUBJECT + VERB
slotHints: X 是你要介绍的城市/学校/空间
where 从句说明这个地方的特点或常见活动
templates.exampleSentence: Kyoto is a city where tradition is still alive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 京都是一座传统仍然鲜活的城市。

items.title: when relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用when引导定语从句，放在表示时间的名词后面，用来补充“在那个时间发生了什么”。基本形式是time word + when + 完整从句，如the day when we met。它通常紧跟被修饰的时间词，常出现在主句中间或句末。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合讲故事、回忆经历、说明某个时期或历史阶段。它最自然地修饰day,time,moment,year,period等时间名词；不要拿它直接修饰地点或原因。when从句通常紧跟时间名词，不要被逗号随便隔开。初学者最安全的做法是先写the day/time/year when + 主语 + 动词。

slotSchema.label: 时间名词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示时间或时期的名词，如the day,the time,the year,a period。常见错误是放地点名词；不能说the place when...，要说the place where...。例：the year when I moved here

slotSchema.label: when从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要放完整从句，通常有主语和动词。常见错误是只放短语或少主语。例：when we first met，不是when first met

slotSchema.label: 主句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 整个“时间名词+when从句”可作主语、宾语或表语的一部分。常见错误是写完定语从句后句子不完整。例：I remember the day when we first met

commonErrors: the place when we met is small.｜错因：when修饰时间，不修饰地点。｜改法：用where，the place where we met is small.
I remember the day when met you.｜错因：when后面需要完整从句，语序不能错。｜改法：I remember the day when I met you.
the time when to leave is now.｜错因：when后不能直接接to不定式来作这个定语从句。｜改法：the time when we should leave is now，或it is time to leave.
2010 when I was born was important.｜错因：裸年份前通常需要限定词或更自然的名词结构。｜改法：The year when I was born was 2010，或2010 was the year when I was born.
I miss the day, when we played outside.｜错因：限制性定语从句里通常不要随便加逗号。｜改法：I miss the day when we played outside.
This is when day we met.｜错因：when的位置错了，应该放在时间名词后引导从句。｜改法：This is the day when we met.

contrasts.title: when vs where
contrasts.note: 结构上，when接时间名词，where接地点名词。语义上，when说“在那时”，where说“在那儿”。比较：the day when we met（我们见面的那天）/ the café where we met（我们见面的咖啡馆）。

contrasts.title: when vs why
contrasts.note: 结构上，when修饰time，why通常修饰reason。场景上，when讲时间背景，why讲原因说明。比较：the day when he left / the reason why he left。

contrasts.title: when vs that after time nouns
contrasts.note: 结构上，时间名词后可用when，也常可用that，甚至省略that。语气上，when更明确、更清楚；that更口语。比较：the day when we met = the day that we met；初学者用when更稳。

contrasts.title: when vs on/in/at which
contrasts.note: 结构上，when常可改成介词+which：on which,in which,at which。语体上，介词+which更正式，多见于书面。比较：the day when we arrived = the day on which we arrived。

contrasts.title: relative when vs question when
contrasts.note: 结构上，定语从句里的when前面有时间名词；疑问词when通常单独引导问题或宾语从句。功能上，一个是修饰名词，一个是问时间。比较：the time when he called / When did he call?

examples.sentence: I remember the day when we first met.
examples.translation: 我记得我们第一次见面的那天。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的用法：the day when + 完整从句。

examples.sentence: The year when I lived in Beijing changed me a lot.
examples.translation: 我住在北京的那一年让我改变了很多。
examples.note: 整个“the year when...”作主语的一部分，展示可放在句首。

examples.sentence: This museum tells the story of a period when the city grew rapidly.
examples.translation: 这座博物馆讲述了这座城市快速发展的那个时期。
examples.note: 较书面，适合作文或说明文。

examples.sentence: The day when we met was unforgettable, but the place where we met was noisy.
examples.translation: 我们见面的那天很难忘，但我们见面的地方很吵。
examples.note: 和where对比，帮助区分时间与地点。

examples.sentence: I miss the time when my family had dinner together every night.
examples.translation: 我怀念我们家每晚一起吃晚饭的那段时光。
examples.note: 适合表达怀念、童年、过去的习惯。

examples.sentence: There was a moment when everyone stopped talking.
examples.translation: 有那么一刻，所有人都停止了说话。
examples.note: 常见搭配a moment when，叙事里很自然。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
templates.template: the day when + 主语 + 动词
slotHints: 先用day/time/year等时间名词；when后写完整从句，如we met，不要只写短语
templates.exampleSentence: the day when we met
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们见面的那天

templates.label: 完整句模板
templates.template: I remember the + 时间名词 + when + 从句
slotHints: 适合写回忆类句子；时间名词后立刻接when从句，后面主句保持完整
templates.exampleSentence: I remember the time when we studied together.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我记得我们一起学习的那段时间。

templates.label: 句首主语模板
templates.template: The + 时间名词 + when + 从句 + 动词 ...
slotHints: 让“时间名词+when从句”整体作主语；适合写较完整的叙述句
templates.exampleSentence: The year when she moved abroad was difficult for her.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她搬到国外的那一年对她来说很艰难。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: a period when + 主语 + 动词
slotHints: 常用于历史、社会、成长类作文；period/era/time都可以，但后面仍要完整从句
templates.exampleSentence: It was a period when the town was changing quickly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 那是这个小镇快速变化的一个时期。

items.title: why relative clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用在reason后面：reason + why + 从句，用来具体说明“原因是什么”。why后面要接完整意思的从句，常见为主语+谓语。整个结构通常放在句中或句尾，作主语、表语或宾语中的名词部分，如The reason why... is...。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都能用，尤其适合解释决定、结果、现象的原因。自然场景有说明、讨论、作业写作。若只是直接说原因，because往往更简单；若reason后面已经接that从句，就不要再加why。它通常紧跟reason，不要被逗号隔开。初学者最稳妥的做法是先用The reason why ... is ... 这个句型。

slotSchema.label: reason
slotSchema.hint: 通常就是reason或the reason。常错：漏冠词说成reason why...，很多场合更自然是the reason why...。例：The reason why I left early...

slotSchema.label: why后面的从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句，常见是主语+谓语。常错：why后只放短语或不完整结构。例：why she was late，不是why her lateness。

slotSchema.label: 后半句说明部分
slotSchema.hint: 常接is/was...来下定义或总结原因。常错：前面写了The reason why...后，后面没有完整谓语。例：The reason why he resigned was stress.

commonErrors: Wrong: The reason why because I was tired.｜错因：why从句和because重复表达原因。｜改法：用The reason why I left was that I was tired.或I left because I was tired.
Wrong: The reason why he left early because he was sick.｜错因：前面已有主语结构，后面缺少系动词。｜改法：The reason why he left early was that he was sick.
Wrong: The reason why of his failure was unclear.｜错因：why后不能直接接of短语。｜改法：The reason for his failure was unclear. 或 The reason why he failed was unclear.
Wrong: I know the reason why his absence.｜错因：why后面不是完整从句。｜改法：I know the reason why he was absent. 或 I know the reason for his absence.
Wrong: The reason, why I came late, is traffic.｜错因：reason和why从句一般不被逗号拆开。｜改法：The reason why I came late is traffic.
Wrong: The reason why is unclear.｜错因：why后缺少内容，信息不完整。｜改法：The reason why they canceled the trip is unclear.

contrasts.title: the reason is that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：the reason why + 从句 + is... 强调“那个原因”；the reason is that + 从句 直接给出原因内容。语气上后者更直给，写作和说明都常见。对比：The reason why he left is unclear. / The reason is that he was ill.

contrasts.title: because + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：because直接连接原因从句，不需要reason。场景上because更口语、更省事；reason why更适合把“原因”当名词来谈。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / The reason why I stayed home was the rain.

contrasts.title: reason for + noun
contrasts.note: 结构不同：reason for后接名词/名词短语；reason why后接完整从句。场景上如果你只有一个名词原因，用for更自然。对比：the reason for the delay / the reason why the train was delayed.

contrasts.title: fact/idea/belief + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：fact, idea, belief等名词后通常用that从句，不用why。语义上those nouns不是“原因”，所以不能随便套why。对比：the fact that he lied（对） / the fact why he lied（错）；the reason why he lied（对）。

contrasts.title: why疑问句
contrasts.note: 结构不同：疑问句Why did he leave?是在提问；reason why clause是在修饰reason，不是提问。语气上前者求信息，后者组织说明。对比：Why did he leave? / The reason why he left is unknown.

examples.sentence: The reason why I study English is to work abroad.
examples.translation: 我学英语的原因是为了去国外工作。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：The reason why ... is ...

examples.sentence: I finally understood the reason why she was upset.
examples.translation: 我终于明白她难过的原因了。
examples.note: 整个结构放在宾语位置，出现在句中。

examples.sentence: The reason why the experiment failed remains unclear.
examples.translation: 这项实验失败的原因仍不清楚。
examples.note: 较常见于书面或分析性表达。

examples.sentence: The reason for the delay was heavy traffic, but the reason why we missed the meeting was poor planning.
examples.translation: 延误的原因是严重堵车，但我们错过会议的原因是计划不周。
examples.note: 对比reason for + 名词 与 reason why + 从句。

examples.sentence: That is the reason why I don't want to move.
examples.translation: 那就是我不想搬家的原因。
examples.note: 可放在be动词后作表语补足部分。

examples.sentence: The reason why he didn't reply was that his phone died.
examples.translation: 他没回复的原因是手机没电了。
examples.note: 后半句常接was that...，很自然。

templates.label: 最稳妥基础模板
templates.template: The reason why [从句] is [结果/原因说明].
slotHints: [从句]放完整句子，如 I left early；[结果/原因说明]可放名词、to do或that从句
templates.exampleSentence: The reason why I left early is that I felt sick.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我早退的原因是我觉得不舒服。

templates.label: 句中宾语模板
templates.template: I know/understand the reason why [从句].
slotHints: 前面常用know, understand, explain；[从句]必须完整，不能只放名词
templates.exampleSentence: I understand the reason why he refused.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我理解他拒绝的原因。

templates.label: 写作分析模板
templates.template: The reason why [现象从句] remains [形容词].
slotHints: 适合写作：remains unclear/unknown/debatable；[现象从句]写需要分析的事实
templates.exampleSentence: The reason why sales dropped remains unclear.
templates.exampleTranslation: 销量下降的原因仍不清楚。

templates.label: 表语说明模板
templates.template: That is the reason why [从句].
slotHints: 用来总结前文；that常指前一句提到的情况
templates.exampleSentence: That is the reason why I chose this major.
templates.exampleTranslation: 那就是我选择这个专业的原因。

items.title: object relative clause with omitted pronoun
items.shortLabel: 省略关系代词的宾语从句式定语从句
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来补充说明前面的名词，意思是“我买的书、你昨天看的电影”这类。它出现在名词后面，后接一个从句；如果关系代词在从句里作宾语，that/which常可省略。但从句里必须还有自己的主语和谓语，不能把主语一起省掉。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和一般写作都很常见，尤其适合让句子更自然、更简洁。最常见于 conversation、邮件、说明、一般叙述；正式学术写作里常保留 which/that 以求清楚。它通常紧跟被修饰名词，不要和名词隔太远；句中照常标点，不因为省略而额外加逗号。初学者最安全的做法：先确认“被省掉的词在从句里是宾语，不是主语”，再套用 noun + 主语 + 动词。

slotSchema.label: 被修饰名词
slotSchema.hint: 这里放你要说明的名词，如 the book, a movie, the person。常见错误：名词后直接接完整新句子却没有定语关系。小例：the book I bought

slotSchema.label: 从句主语
slotSchema.hint: 这里放从句里的执行者，如 I, she, we, Tom。常见错误：把主语也省掉，变成 *the book bought yesterday。小例：the song we heard

slotSchema.label: 从句其余部分
slotSchema.hint: 这里放动词及其他成分，但要让前面的名词在这里充当宾语，如 bought yesterday, saw on TV。常见错误：用了不及物动词，导致前面名词无法作宾语。小例：the movie I watched last night

commonErrors: *The man helped me was kind. 错在省略后从句里缺主语；这里被省略的不是宾语而是主语。应改为 The man who/that helped me was kind.
*The book I bought it yesterday is expensive. 错在宾语重复；前面的 the book 已经是从句里 bought 的宾语。应改为 The book I bought yesterday is expensive.
*The place I went was crowded. 错在 go 常不直接带宾语，前面的 place 不能直接作 went 的宾语。应改为 The place I went to was crowded.
*This is the car, I bought yesterday. 错在随意加逗号把限定性定语从句拆开了。应改为 This is the car I bought yesterday.
*The person which I met was friendly. 错在人通常不用 which。应改为 The person I met was friendly. 或 The person that/who I met was friendly.
*The book what I read was interesting. 错在标准英语里关系代词不用 what 来连接这种先行词。应改为 The book that/which I read was interesting. 或 The book I read was interesting.

contrasts.title: vs noun + that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：这里可省掉作宾语的 that；that 版把连接词说出来。语气/场景：两者都常见，保留 that 略更清楚，省略更自然简洁。对比：the movie that we saw = the movie we saw。

contrasts.title: vs noun + which + clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：which 作宾语时也可省，但多用于物，不用于人。语气/场景：which 稍偏书面；省略后更口语自然。对比：the book which I bought = the book I bought。

contrasts.title: vs noun + who + clause
contrasts.note: 结构差别：who 常用于人；当 who 在从句里作宾语时，口语里也常省。语气/场景：保留 who 更明确；省略更像自然口语。对比：the person who I met = the person I met。

contrasts.title: vs 主语关系从句
contrasts.note: 结构差别：只有关系词作宾语时才能省；若它作主语，不能省。语气/场景：这是最关键的判断点，和正式/口语无关。对比：the book I bought ✔；the man helped me ✘，应为 the man who helped me。

contrasts.title: vs 带介词的相关结构
contrasts.note: 结构差别：如果动词要介词，省略后介词通常留在从句后面；不能一起消失。语气/场景：口语里介词后置最自然，正式写作可能用 to which。对比：the chair I sat on；更正式：the chair on which I sat。

examples.sentence: The book I bought yesterday is very useful.
examples.translation: 我昨天买的那本书很有用。
examples.note: 最安全的核心用法：book 是 bought 的宾语，所以可以省略 that/which。

examples.sentence: The movie we watched last night was better than I expected.
examples.translation: 我们昨晚看的电影比我预想的更好。
examples.note: 关系从句紧跟名词 movie，位置最自然。

examples.sentence: The person I spoke to was the manager.
examples.translation: 我交谈的那个人是经理。
examples.note: 动词短语需要介词 to，省略后介词保留在句尾。

examples.sentence: The solution we discussed is cheaper than the one they suggested.
examples.translation: 我们讨论的方案比他们提出的那个更便宜。
examples.note: 适合比较句，能清楚区分两个名词。

examples.sentence: The device the company developed reduced costs significantly.
examples.translation: 该公司研发的设备显著降低了成本。
examples.note: 一般写作风格中也常见，语气简洁。

examples.sentence: Do you still have the charger I lent you?
examples.translation: 我借给你的那个充电器你还留着吗？
examples.note: 非常常见的日常表达。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
templates.template: the + 名词 + 主语 + 及物动词 + 其他
slotHints: 先选一个能被后面动词直接作用的名词；从句里一定保留主语；不要再补一个 it/them 作宾语。
templates.exampleSentence: the cake I made this morning
templates.exampleTranslation: 我今天早上做的蛋糕

templates.label: 介词后置模板
templates.template: the + 名词 + 主语 + 动词 + 介词
slotHints: 当动词需要介词时，把介词留在后面，如 talk to, sit on, look at；不要把介词省掉。
templates.exampleSentence: the friend I talked to yesterday
templates.exampleTranslation: 我昨天聊天的那个朋友

templates.label: 完整句模板
templates.template: This is the + 名词 + 主语 + 动词 + 其他.
slotHints: 适合直接造句；先说 This is...，再放定语从句。确保被修饰名词紧跟从句。
templates.exampleSentence: This is the photo we took at the beach.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这是我们在海边拍的那张照片。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: the + 名词 + 主语 + 动词 + 过去时间/地点信息
slotHints: 写作里可用这个模板压缩两个句子；时间地点放后面最稳。
templates.exampleSentence: the report we finished last week
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们上周完成的报告

items.title: so that + clause
items.coreExplanation: so that + 从句用来说明“为了让……/以便……”，表示目的。前面先说主要动作，后面接完整从句，通常有主语，常配 can, could, will, would 等情态动词。它一般放在主句后面：main clause + so that + clause；也可按写作需要前置。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都常见，特别适合解释安排、步骤、计划、提醒等“这样做是为了什么”。如果后面只是同一主语的简单动作，初学者常用 in order to 更省事。不要把它当结果 so 用。so that 从句前通常不用逗号；若整句较长或把 so that 从句放句首，注意逗号和主句边界。最稳妥做法：主句后直接接 so that + 主语 + can/could + 动词。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放实际动作或安排。常见错误：主句写成目的本身而没有动作。例：I left early ...

slotSchema.label: 从句主语
slotSchema.hint: 放目的从句里的执行者，可以和主句相同也可以不同。常见错误：漏掉主语。例：so that everyone ...

slotSchema.label: 情态+动词
slotSchema.hint: 最常见是 can/could/will/would + 动词原形。常见错误：写成 to do 或动词单三。例：can hear / could finish

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 放宾语、地点、时间等。常见错误：把完整句子硬塞进短语位置。例：the teacher clearly / before noon

commonErrors: wrong: I left early so that to catch the train.｜why: so that 后面要接完整从句，不接 to do。｜fix: I left early so that I could catch the train. / I left early in order to catch the train.
wrong: Speak louder so that hear you.｜why: 从句缺主语，英语里完整从句通常要有主语。｜fix: Speak louder so that we can hear you.
wrong: I saved the file so that I don't lose it.｜why: 表目的时更自然用 can/could/won't 等，不直接用一般现在时表示目的。｜fix: I saved the file so that I won't lose it.
wrong: It was raining, so that we stayed home.｜why: 这里表达结果，不是目的；so that 不能这样代替 so。｜fix: It was raining, so we stayed home.
wrong: So that I can pass the exam I study every day.｜why: 句首目的从句可以用，但此句少了逗号，且对初学者不够稳。｜fix: So that I can pass the exam, I study every day. 更自然：I study every day so that I can pass the exam.
wrong: I bought a notebook so that write notes.｜why: so that 后要主语+情态+动词，不能直接用动词原形。｜fix: I bought a notebook so that I could write notes.

contrasts.title: vs in order to + base
contrasts.note: 结构上，in order to 后接动词原形；so that 后接完整从句。语气都中性，但 so that 更适合后面需要另一个主语或情态动词时。对比：I left early in order to catch the train. / I left early so that my son could catch the train.

contrasts.title: vs so + result clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，so that 表目的，so 表结果。场景上，so that 回答“为了什么”，so 回答“结果怎样”。最小对比：I whispered so that the baby could sleep. / The baby was tired, so he slept early.

contrasts.title: vs to + base (simple purpose)
contrasts.note: 结构上，不定式更短；so that 更完整。语气上，不定式更简洁，适合同主语简单目的；so that 更清楚。对比：I went to the bank to get cash. / I went to the bank so that my wife could get cash too.

contrasts.title: vs enough/too ... to
contrasts.note: enough/too ... to 常跟能力、程度结果相关；so that 直接说明人为目的。场景不同：前者说“达到/没达到某程度”，后者说“这样做是为了……”。对比：He was tall enough to reach it. / He stood on a chair so that he could reach it.

examples.sentence: I left early so that I could catch the train.
examples.translation: 我早早出发了，以便能赶上火车。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：主句 + so that + I could + 动词。

examples.sentence: So that everyone can see the screen, please sit closer.
examples.translation: 为了让大家都能看清屏幕，请坐近一点。
examples.note: 从句前置时，后面通常加逗号。

examples.sentence: The report includes a short summary so that busy readers can grasp the main points quickly.
examples.translation: 这份报告包含一个简短摘要，以便忙碌的读者能迅速抓住要点。
examples.note: 书面说明里很常见。

examples.sentence: I set two alarms so that I won't oversleep, not so I can wake up earlier than everyone else.
examples.translation: 我设了两个闹钟，是为了不睡过头，不是为了比所有人起得更早。
examples.note: 和结果、比较类表达放在一起时，目的意义更清楚。

examples.sentence: Please email me the file so that I can check it tonight.
examples.translation: 请把文件发邮件给我，这样我今晚就能检查。
examples.note: 常用于请求别人配合。

examples.sentence: She spoke slowly so that her grandparents could understand her.
examples.translation: 她说得很慢，以便她的祖父母能听懂她。
examples.note: 适合“主句主语”和“从句主语”不同的情况。

templates.label: 最稳妥初学者模板
templates.template: [主句] so that [主语] can/could [动词原形]
slotHints: 主句写实际动作
从句主语不要省略
can/could 后用动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: I wrote it down so that I could remember it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我把它记下来了，以便自己能记住。

templates.label: 前置目的模板
templates.template: So that [主语] can/could [动词原形], [主句]
slotHints: 前置时后面通常加逗号
适合指示、通知、正式说明
templates.exampleSentence: So that everyone can join, we moved the meeting online.
templates.exampleTranslation: 为了让大家都能参加，我们把会议改成线上了。

templates.label: 书面说明模板
templates.template: [事物/安排] includes/uses [内容] so that [主语] can [动词原形]
slotHints: 适合介绍设计、功能、报告结构
后面写使用者能做到什么
templates.exampleSentence: The app uses large buttons so that older users can navigate it easily.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个应用使用大按钮，以便年长用户能轻松操作。

items.title: in order to + base
items.pattern: main clause in order to + base
items.coreExplanation: in order to + 动词原形表示“为了……”，比单独的to更明确、更正式。后面必须接动词原形，不能接完整从句。它最常放在主句后说明目的，也可放句首作目的状语，后面通常用逗号隔开。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式，常见于书面说明、步骤、邮件、报告、规定，也可用于较认真地解释做某事的目的。日常随口对话里更常用to；如果后面有主语和谓语，就不要用in order to。放句首时常写成“In order to ..., 主句”；放句中时直接接动词原形。初学者最安全的做法是先写主句，再加in order to + 动词原形。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放已经发生或将要做的动作。常见错误：主句写成不完整片段。例：We left early ...

slotSchema.label: 目的动作原形
slotSchema.hint: 这里必须用动词原形，不要用to后再加完整句子，也不要用动名词。例：in order to catch the train

slotSchema.label: 宾语/补足信息
slotSchema.hint: 可补充宾语、时间、地点等。常见错误：缺少必要宾语导致意思不完整。例：in order to finish the report on time

commonErrors: in order to he can pass the exam：错在后面接了完整从句；in order to后只能接动词原形。改为in order to pass the exam，或so that he can pass the exam。
We study hard in order to passing the test：错在to后用了passing；这里要用动词原形。改为in order to pass the test。
In order to, we saved money：错在in order to后没有动作，结构不完整。改为In order to buy a car, we saved money。
We went to the bank in order to for opening an account：错在to后又加for并改成动名词，结构混乱。改为in order to open an account。
I called her in order to that she knew the news：错在把that从句接在in order to后。改为I called her so that she would know the news，或I called her in order to tell her the news。
In order to improve my English. I read every day：错在句首目的状语后断成残句；不能只写从句样片。改为In order to improve my English, I read every day。

contrasts.title: vs to + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接动词原形，但in order to更长、更明确；语气更正式，常用于写作或强调目的，to更自然口语。对比：I went early to get a seat. / I went early in order to get a seat.

contrasts.title: vs so that + 从句
contrasts.note: 结构上in order to后接动词原形，so that后接完整从句。场景上，so that适合需要表达主语、情态或结果条件时；in order to更紧凑。对比：I left early in order to catch the bus. / I left early so that I could catch the bus.

contrasts.title: vs for + 名词
contrasts.note: 结构上for后常接名词，不接动词原形；in order to专门接动作。语气上for更像说明用途或目的对象，in order to更像说明“去做什么”。对比：a tool for cutting paper / He bought a tool in order to cut paper.

contrasts.title: vs in order that + 从句
contrasts.note: 结构上in order that后接完整从句，较老派、更正式；in order to后接动词原形，更常见。写作中两者都可见，但日常学习优先掌握in order to。对比：We spoke quietly in order not to wake her. / We spoke quietly in order that we might not wake her.

examples.sentence: I study every night in order to improve my English.
examples.translation: 我每天晚上学习，为了提高英语。
examples.note: 最核心、安全的主句后置用法。

examples.sentence: In order to catch the first bus, we left home at six.
examples.translation: 为了赶上第一班公交车，我们六点就出门了。
examples.note: 句首位置更书面，后面通常加逗号。

examples.sentence: The company updated its website in order to provide clearer information to customers.
examples.translation: 公司更新了网站，以便向客户提供更清晰的信息。
examples.note: 正式写作和工作汇报里很自然。

examples.sentence: I took notes in order to remember the key points, not just to look busy.
examples.translation: 我做笔记是为了记住重点，而不只是看起来很忙。
examples.note: 和普通to相比，这里用in order to更强调真实目的。

examples.sentence: She wore gloves in order to keep her hands warm.
examples.translation: 她戴手套是为了让手保暖。
examples.note: 日常生活场景，也可换成更口语的to keep。

examples.sentence: Please save your work frequently in order to avoid losing data.
examples.translation: 请经常保存你的工作内容，以免丢失数据。
examples.note: 说明、指示、操作提示中很常见。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: [主句] + in order to + [动词原形] + [宾语/补充]
slotHints: 先写完整主句，再补目的；to后面只能用动词原形，如 pass, finish, buy。
templates.exampleSentence: We left early in order to avoid traffic.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们早点出发，为了避开交通高峰。

templates.label: 句首目的模板
templates.template: In order to + [动词原形] + [宾语/补充], + [主句]
slotHints: 句首更书面；目的部分后通常加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: In order to save money, I cook at home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 为了省钱，我在家做饭。

templates.label: 写作用正式模板
templates.template: [机构/人] + [动作] + in order to + [更正式的动词短语]
slotHints: 适合邮件、报告、通知，如 improve efficiency, provide support, reduce risk。
templates.exampleSentence: The team changed the schedule in order to reduce delays.
templates.exampleTranslation: 团队调整了日程安排，以减少延误。

items.title: too + adjective + to...
items.coreExplanation: too + 形容词 + to + 动词原形 表示“太……而不能……”，重点是程度过高，导致后面的动作无法或不适合发生。形容词放在 too 后，to 后必须接动词原形。最常见放在 be 动词后：主语 + be + too + 形容词 + to...。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都常见，适合描述能力不足、时间不够、东西过热过重等“因程度过头而做不了”的场景。不适合表示目的，也不用于单纯说明结果时的正式长句。通常放在句子主体里，不单独成句；too 前后不要加多余逗号。初学者先稳用：人/物 + be + too + 形容词 + to + 动词原形。
sceneTags: 说明东西太热太重太贵

slotSchema.label: 形容词
slotSchema.hint: 这里放表示程度的形容词，如 tired, hot, expensive, young。常见错误是放副词或直接放名词。例：too tired to study。

slotSchema.label: 动词原形
slotSchema.hint: to 后必须接动词原形，如 go, carry, understand。常见错误是用 doing 或过去式。例：too heavy to carry。

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 通常是人、物、时间等名词短语。常见错误是主语和后面动作逻辑不一致。例：The box is too big to lift。

commonErrors: wrong: too tired to studying；why: to 后面不能接 doing；fix: too tired to study。
wrong: very tired to study；why: very 只表示“很”，不表示“太……而不能……”；fix: too tired to study。
wrong: too enough young to drive；why: too 和 enough 不能这样连用；fix: too young to drive / old enough to drive。
wrong: The water is too hot drink；why: 缺少 to；fix: The water is too hot to drink。
wrong: I am too happy to go to the party（想表达“很开心所以去”）；why: too...to 常带“过度导致不能/不适合”，意思反了；fix: I am happy enough to go / I am so happy that I want to go。
wrong: This bag is too heavy to me carry；why: 宾语位置错；fix: This bag is too heavy for me to carry。

contrasts.title: adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: 结构上是 形容词 + enough + to + 动词原形，不是 too + 形容词。语气上 enough 表示“足够，可以做到”，too 表示“过头，做不到”。对比：He is too young to drive. / He is old enough to drive.

contrasts.title: so + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: 结构上 so 后接形容词，再接 that 从句；too...to 后面接 to + 动词原形。so...that 更适合强调结果本身，too...to 更强调“无法进行”。对比：The box was so heavy that I dropped it. / The box was too heavy to carry.

contrasts.title: very + adjective
contrasts.note: 结构上 very + 形容词 只表示程度高，不带“不能做”的含义。语气更普通，适合单纯描述。对比：I am very tired. / I am too tired to work.

contrasts.title: not + adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: 结构上是否定式：not + 形容词 + enough + to。意思常接近 too...to，但视角不同：too...to 强调“太过”，not enough to 强调“不够”。对比：The tea is too hot to drink. / The tea is not cool enough to drink.

contrasts.title: too + adjective + for someone to...
contrasts.note: 结构上如果要明确“谁做不了”，可在形容词后加 for + 人 + to + 动词。语气更完整，常用于说明他人的限制。对比：The bag is too heavy to carry. / The bag is too heavy for me to carry.

examples.sentence: I am too tired to study.
examples.translation: 我太累了，没法学习。
examples.note: 最基础模板：be + too + 形容词 + to + 动词原形。

examples.sentence: This box is too heavy for me to lift.
examples.translation: 这个箱子太重了，我搬不起来。
examples.note: 加入 for me，明确是谁做不到。

examples.sentence: The water was too hot to drink.
examples.translation: 这水太烫了，不能喝。
examples.note: 主语也可以是物，不一定是人。

examples.sentence: He is too young to drive.
examples.translation: 他太小了，还不能开车。
examples.note: 和 enough 对比时很常见。

examples.sentence: The issue is too complex to explain briefly here.
examples.translation: 这个问题太复杂了，无法在这里简要说明。
examples.note: 较偏书面表达。

examples.sentence: I was too busy to reply to your message yesterday.
examples.translation: 我昨天太忙了，没法回复你的消息。
examples.note: 日常口语很自然。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: [subject] 放人或物，如 I / The bag
[adj] 放形容词，如 tired, heavy
[verb] 用动词原形，如 study, carry
templates.exampleSentence: I am too tired to cook.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我太累了，做不了饭。

templates.label: 带执行者模板
slotHints: [someone] 放动作执行者，如 me, him, the kids
常用于主语不是人，或想明确“谁做不到”
templates.exampleSentence: The table is too heavy for us to move.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这张桌子太重了，我们搬不动。

templates.label: 过去情况模板
slotHints: 描述过去某时做不到的事
[adj] 常用 tired, busy, nervous 等
templates.exampleSentence: She was too nervous to speak.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她太紧张了，说不出话。

templates.label: 书面说明模板
slotHints: 主语可用 problem, issue, task, question 等
适合说明事情因复杂/困难而无法处理
templates.exampleSentence: The problem is too difficult to solve quickly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个问题太难了，无法迅速解决。

items.title: adjective + enough to...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构表示“程度足以做某事”。基本形式是“形容词 + enough + to + 动词原形”，形容词在前，enough 放在形容词后面，to 后面只能接动词原形。它常放在系动词后作表语，如 be old enough to...，也常接人物主语。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合表达能力、条件、年龄、大小、速度等“够不够”。不适合直接表示目的；那时更常用 to / in order to。注意 enough 不放在形容词前，逗号通常不需要。初学者最安全的做法是先用“be + 形容词 + enough to + 动词原形”，如 She is tall enough to reach it.

slotSchema.label: 形容词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示程度或条件的形容词，如 old, tall, strong, careful。常见错误是写成 enough old。要说 old enough。例：careful enough to notice it

slotSchema.label: to后动词原形
slotSchema.hint: 这里接动作动词原形，如 to drive, to lift, to understand。常见错误是加 -ing 或过去式。例：to carry the box

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 常用人、动物、物作主语；人最自然。常见错误是主语和动作逻辑不匹配。例：The boy is old enough to travel alone

commonErrors: wrong: enough tall to reach the shelf；why: enough 修饰形容词时要放在形容词后面；fix: tall enough to reach the shelf
wrong: she is enough careful to avoid mistakes；why: 词序错，不能说 enough careful；fix: she is careful enough to avoid mistakes
wrong: he is strong enough lifting it；why: enough to 后面要接 to + 动词原形；fix: he is strong enough to lift it
wrong: this box is enough heavy to break the table；why: 这是另一种逻辑，heavy enough to... 才对；fix: this box is heavy enough to break the table
wrong: I studied enough hard to pass；why: hard 是副词，这张卡的结构是形容词 + enough to；fix: I studied hard enough to pass 或 I was prepared enough to pass
wrong: he is too old enough to play here；why: too 和 enough 不能这样叠加，意思也冲突；fix: he is old enough to play here / he is too old to play here

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构差别：too 放在形容词前，enough 放在形容词后。语义差别：too...to 表示“太……而不能”，偏否定；adj enough to 表示“足够……可以”，偏肯定。对比：He is too young to drive. / He is old enough to drive.

contrasts.title: enough + noun
contrasts.note: 结构差别：修饰名词时 enough 放前面，如 enough time；修饰形容词时放后面，如 old enough。场景差别：enough + noun 强调数量；adj enough to 强调程度。对比：We have enough time to leave. / We are ready enough to leave.

contrasts.title: so...that / so that
contrasts.note: 结构差别：adj enough to 后面接 to + 动词原形；so...that / so that 后面接完整从句。场景差别：enough to 更简洁，常说条件足够；so...that 更强调结果。对比：She was calm enough to answer. / She was so calm that she answered clearly.

contrasts.title: in order to
contrasts.note: 结构差别：adj enough to 前面必须有形容词；in order to 直接接动词原形。场景差别：adj enough to 说“条件足够”，in order to 说“为了目的”。对比：He is strong enough to carry it. / He exercises in order to get stronger.

examples.sentence: She is old enough to drive.
examples.translation: 她年龄够大，可以开车了。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的入门句型：be + 形容词 + enough to。

examples.sentence: The rope is strong enough to hold the boat.
examples.translation: 这根绳子够结实，可以固定住那条船。
examples.note: 主语也可以是物，只要后面的动作逻辑成立。

examples.sentence: Are you well enough to go to school today?
examples.translation: 你今天身体状况够好吗，能去上学吗？
examples.note: 可用于问句，仍保持“形容词后 + enough”。

examples.sentence: The evidence was clear enough to support the claim.
examples.translation: 证据足够清楚，可以支持这个观点。
examples.note: 较常见于书面表达和议论文。

examples.sentence: He is careful enough to notice small mistakes, but not experienced enough to fix all of them.
examples.translation: 他足够细心，能注意到小错误，但还不够有经验，不能把它们全改好。
examples.note: 适合和 not enough to 对比使用，表达“够……但还不够……”。

examples.sentence: The soup is cool enough to eat now.
examples.translation: 汤现在够凉了，可以喝了。
examples.note: 非常自然的生活场景表达。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: [subject] 常用人或物，如 she / the box
[adj] 用 old, tall, strong, careful 等形容词
[toVerb] 用动词原形，如 drive, lift, understand
templates.exampleSentence: My brother is tall enough to reach the shelf.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我哥哥个子够高，能够到那个架子。

templates.label: 否定对比模板
templates.template: [subject] + be + [adj1] + enough to + [toVerb1] + but not + [adj2] + enough to + [toVerb2]
slotHints: 先说“足够做某事”，再说“还不够做另一事”
第二部分仍然是形容词 + enough to
适合表达部分满足、部分不足
templates.exampleSentence: She is brave enough to speak, but not confident enough to lead the meeting.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她有足够的勇气发言，但还不够自信，不能主持会议。

templates.label: 问句模板
slotHints: 适合询问健康、年龄、能力、准备程度
不要把 enough 放到形容词前
to 后面仍用动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: Is the water warm enough to swim in?
templates.exampleTranslation: 水够暖和，可以下去游泳吗？

templates.label: 写作表达模板
slotHints: 主语可用抽象名词，如 evidence, data, explanation
形容词常用 clear, strong, detailed, simple
适合客观评价，不带强烈口语情绪
templates.exampleSentence: The explanation is simple enough to follow.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个解释足够简单，便于理解。

items.title: rather than
items.coreExplanation: rather than 用来表示“选A，不选B”或“与其B，不如A”。最常见结构是 A rather than B，A和B通常要词性平行，如名词对名词、动词原形对动词原形、短语对短语。它多放在被比较的两项之间，也常出现在句中作简洁对比。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都常见；在表达偏好、建议、立场、说明选择标准时很自然。它适合比较两个可替换选项，不适合单纯表示时间先后或原因。写作里常放在两个并列成分之间；句子较长时注意让A、B结构对称。初学者最安全的做法是先用名词或动词原形做比较，如 tea rather than coffee, stay rather than go。

slotSchema.label: 首选项
slotSchema.hint: 这里放你真正选择的一项；常见错误是A、B词性不一致。小例子：walk rather than drive。

slotSchema.label: 被放弃项
slotSchema.hint: 这里放你不选的一项；常见错误是把完整从句硬塞进简短结构里。小例子：tea rather than coffee。

slotSchema.label: 动词原形（可选）
slotSchema.hint: 当比较动作时，优先用动词原形对动词原形；常见错误是一个用to do，另一个用doing。小例子：stay rather than leave。

commonErrors: wrong: I prefer to walk rather than driving.｜错因：rather than 前后动作形式不平行，一个是 to walk，一个是 driving。｜改法：用平行结构：I prefer to walk rather than drive. 或 I walk rather than drive.
wrong: Rather than I go by bus, I take the train.｜错因：把完整从句直接放在 rather than 后面，结构别扭。｜改法：改成平行成分：I take the train rather than go by bus.
wrong: She chose quickly rather than careful.｜错因：副词和形容词不平行，quickly 对 careful 不对应。｜改法：两边统一：She chose quickly rather than carefully. 或 She was quick rather than careful.
wrong: I bought a notebook, rather than because it was cheap.｜错因：B部分变成原因从句，rather than 不能这样接“because...”。｜改法：比较两个选项：I bought a notebook rather than a tablet.
wrong: He rather than is lazy.｜错因：rather than 不能单独放在系动词前后充当完整表语。｜改法：提供被比较的两项：He is cautious rather than lazy.
wrong: We went home rather than to eat outside.｜错因：A是完整谓语，B是不定式短语，重心不对称。｜改法：We went home rather than ate outside. 更自然可改为 We went home instead of eating out.

contrasts.title: rather than vs instead of
contrasts.note: 结构上，rather than 可接名词、短语，也常接动词原形；instead of 后面更稳妥的是名词或 v-ing。语气上，rather than 稍更书面、更像“对比选择”；instead of 更日常。对比：I stayed home rather than go out. / I stayed home instead of going out.

contrasts.title: rather than vs prefer A to B
contrasts.note: 结构上，prefer 明确带“更喜欢”，常用 prefer A to B 或 prefer to do A rather than do B；rather than 本身只是做选择对比。语气上，prefer 更直接说偏好，rather than 更适合压缩对比。对比：I prefer tea to coffee. / I drink tea rather than coffee.

contrasts.title: rather than vs or
contrasts.note: 结构上，or 只是列出两个可能；rather than 明确表示排除B、选择A。语气上，or 中性开放，rather than 有立场。对比：Tea or coffee?（都可能）/ Tea rather than coffee.（我选茶）

contrasts.title: rather than vs not A but B
contrasts.note: 结构上，not A but B 通常先否定前项再强调后项；rather than 直接把首选项放前面。语气上，not A but B 更强调纠正，rather than 更平衡。对比：Not coffee but tea. / Tea rather than coffee.

contrasts.title: A rather than B vs Rather than B, A
contrasts.note: 结构上，A rather than B 把比较项放中间；Rather than B, A 把“不要B”提前。语气上，前者更紧凑，后者更强调前置对比，常见于写作或建议。对比：I stayed home rather than go out. / Rather than go out, I stayed home.

examples.sentence: I want tea rather than coffee.
examples.translation: 我想要茶，不要咖啡。
examples.note: 最安全的初学者用法：名词对名词。

examples.sentence: Rather than take a taxi, we walked home.
examples.translation: 我们没打车，而是走路回家。
examples.note: 前置位置，更强调“不要B”。

examples.sentence: The policy aims to support prevention rather than treatment.
examples.translation: 这项政策旨在支持预防，而不是治疗。
examples.note: 较常见于说明文和正式写作。

examples.sentence: She chose to wait rather than complain.
examples.translation: 她选择等待，而不是抱怨。
examples.note: 与 prefer 相近，但这里重点是当下选择。

examples.sentence: I study at home rather than in the library.
examples.translation: 我在家学习，而不是在图书馆。
examples.note: 介词短语也可以平行比较。

examples.sentence: He emailed me rather than called me.
examples.translation: 他给我发了邮件，而不是给我打电话。
examples.note: 比较两种联系方式，口语自然。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: A、B先用名词或短语，保持同类：tea / coffee
如果比较动作，也可用动词原形：stay / leave
templates.exampleSentence: I chose the blue one rather than the black one.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我选了蓝色那个，而不是黑色那个。

templates.label: 前置强调模板
slotHints: 把不选的项目B提前，后面再说真正选择A
B和A最好仍然是对应动作或对应短语
templates.exampleSentence: Rather than argue, he listened quietly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他没有争论，而是安静地听着。

templates.label: 动作比较模板
templates.template: subject + verb + do A rather than do B
slotHints: 两个动作都用动词原形，最不容易出错
不要写成 do A rather than doing B
templates.exampleSentence: We decided to stay rather than leave early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们决定留下，而不是早早离开。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: subject + aim/seek/focus on A rather than B
slotHints: 适合说明目标、政策、方法，A和B多用名词
常见于议论文、说明文
templates.exampleSentence: The report focuses on solutions rather than blame.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这份报告关注的是解决方案，而不是指责。

items.title: instead of + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: instead of 表示“用A代替B”或“做A而不做B”。它后面通常接名词、代词或动词-ing，不能直接接动词原形。常放在句中主句后面，也可放句首作对比，后面常接逗号。最安全用法是：主语 + 动词 + A instead of B。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合谈选择、建议、习惯改变、安排替换。它强调“替代的对象”，不单独表示“反而”；如果句子里没有被替代的内容，通常用 instead 更自然。放句首时常写成 Instead of ..., 主句。初学者先牢牢记住：of 后不用动词原形，动作要改成 -ing，如 instead of go 要改成 instead of going。

slotSchema.label: main action/result
slotSchema.hint: 这里放主要发生的动作或结果，通常是完整主句。常见错误是只写 instead of 短语而没有主句。例：I took the bus instead of driving.

slotSchema.label: replaced noun or action
slotSchema.hint: 这里放被替代的内容，可用名词/代词，或动词-ing。常见错误是用动词原形。例：instead of coffee / instead of driving.

slotSchema.label: fronted instead of phrase
slotSchema.hint: 句首时放 Instead of + 名词或-ing，然后用逗号接主句。常见错误是句首后不加逗号。例：Instead of taking a taxi, we walked home.

commonErrors: wrong: I went out instead of stay at home.｜why: of 后面不能直接接动词原形 stay。｜fix: I went out instead of staying at home.
wrong: Instead of, I studied at home.｜why: instead of 后面必须有被替代的内容，不能单独出现。｜fix: Instead of going out, I studied at home.
wrong: I used tea instead coffee.｜why: instead 后如果要接被替代对象，必须用 instead of。｜fix: I used tea instead of coffee.
wrong: Instead of take the train, we drove.｜why: 句首也一样，of 后要用 -ing。｜fix: Instead of taking the train, we drove.
wrong: I stayed home, instead of I went out.｜why: instead of 后面一般不直接接完整独立句 I went out。｜fix: I stayed home instead of going out.
wrong: Instead of taking a bus we walked.｜why: 句首前置时通常用逗号帮助分清结构。｜fix: Instead of taking a bus, we walked.

contrasts.title: instead
contrasts.note: 结构不同：instead 常单独使用，不直接接名词或-ing；instead of 后面必须接替代对象。语气上，instead 更像“反而/改成那样”。比较：We didn't go out. We stayed home instead. / We stayed home instead of going out.

contrasts.title: rather than
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可表示选择，但 rather than 更常见于较正式或书面表达，也常和平行成分连用。instead of 更直白，适合日常替代。比较：I chose tea rather than coffee. / I had tea instead of coffee.

contrasts.title: not...instead of...
contrasts.note: 结构上 instead of 本身不等于否定句；前面主句通常已经说明真正发生的事。场景上，初学者别把它当成单独的“不”。比较：He called me instead of texting me. 不是 He instead of texted me.

contrasts.title: in place of
contrasts.note: 结构上 in place of 后也接名词，意思接近；但它更书面，常用于正式说明或物品替换。instead of 更常用于动作和日常选择。比较：Use water in place of milk. / Use water instead of milk.

examples.sentence: I drank tea instead of coffee.
examples.translation: 我喝了茶，而不是咖啡。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：动词 + A instead of B。

examples.sentence: Instead of driving, we took the bus.
examples.translation: 我们没开车，而是坐了公交。
examples.note: 句首前置时，用逗号接主句。

examples.sentence: The report uses charts instead of long paragraphs.
examples.translation: 这份报告用图表代替了长段文字。
examples.note: 书面说明里也很自然。

examples.sentence: She studied at home instead of going to the library.
examples.translation: 她在家学习，没有去图书馆。
examples.note: 和 instead 对比时，这句明确说出了被替代的动作。

examples.sentence: Let's cook at home instead of ordering takeout.
examples.translation: 我们在家做饭吧，别点外卖了。
examples.note: 很常见的建议表达。

examples.sentence: I listened to the recording instead of reading the transcript.
examples.translation: 我听了录音，而没有读文字稿。
examples.note: 两个动作都用名词化/ing结构更清楚。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: A 和 B 放两个可对比的东西；B 可是名词，也可是 -ing 形式；不要在 B 位置用动词原形。
templates.exampleSentence: I ate fruit instead of chips.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我吃了水果，而不是薯片。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
slotHints: 把被替代内容放句首形成对比；[B] 后常加逗号；如果 B 是动作，要用 -ing。
templates.exampleSentence: Instead of taking a taxi, she walked.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她没打车，而是走路了。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
slotHints: 适合说明方案、报告、产品设计等；A/B 多用名词短语。
templates.exampleSentence: This app uses icons instead of text.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个应用用图标代替文字。

items.title: prefer A to B
items.coreExplanation: prefer A to B 用来表达“比起B，更喜欢A”的稳定偏好。A和B通常要在形式上平行：都用名词短语，或都用动词-ing，不用两个不对称成分。它常放在主语后作谓语，如 I prefer tea to coffee；也常接时间、地点等补充信息。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合谈个人习惯、口味、学习方式、生活选择等。它强调“比较后的偏好”，不是临时现场决定。不要把它和 would rather 混用；说动作偏好时优先用 prefer doing A to doing B。to 后面这里是介词，不是不定式标记，所以后面不能直接接动词原形。初学者最安全的做法是先写名词对名词，或动名词对动名词。

slotSchema.label: 更喜欢的对象
slotSchema.hint: 可放名词短语或动词-ing；常见错误是A用名词、B用动词，结构不平行。例：prefer tea / prefer reading

slotSchema.label: 相比之下不那么喜欢的对象
slotSchema.hint: to 后也要放与A同类的成分；常见错误是 to 后接动词原形。例：to coffee / to watching TV

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 可加时间、地点、原因等补充成分；常见错误是把补充语插进 prefer A to B 中间打乱结构。例：on weekdays / at home

commonErrors: prefer A than B：错在把固定介词 to 换成 than。应改为 prefer A to B，如 I prefer tea to coffee.
prefer do A to do B：错在 to 前后都不能直接用动词原形表示动作偏好。应改为 prefer doing A to doing B，如 I prefer walking to driving.
prefer A to do B：错在把后半部分误当不定式；这里的 to 是介词。应改为 prefer A to B，或 prefer doing A to doing B。
prefer to A than B：错在把 prefer to do 和 prefer A to B 两个结构混在一起。应改为 prefer to do A rather than do B，或 prefer A to B。
I prefer coffee than tea：错在比较对象前用了 than。应改为 I prefer coffee to tea.
I prefer reading to watch TV：错在前后不平行，前面是动名词，后面是动词原形。应改为 I prefer reading to watching TV.

contrasts.title: would rather
contrasts.note: 结构不同：would rather do A than do B；prefer A to B / prefer doing A to doing B。语气场景不同：would rather 更常说当下、这次、主观选择；prefer 更像一般习惯。对比：Today I'd rather stay home than go out. / I prefer staying home to going out.

contrasts.title: rather than
contrasts.note: 结构不同：rather than 常连接并列成分，常见于 would prefer to do A rather than do B 或名词后补充选择；prefer A to B 本身就是谓语结构。语气上 rather than 更突出“而不是”。对比：I would prefer to walk rather than drive. / I prefer walking to driving.

contrasts.title: like ... better than
contrasts.note: 结构不同：like A better than B 里用副词 better；prefer A to B 用介词 to。语气上 prefer 稍正式、更简洁；like ... better than 更口语。对比：I like tea better than coffee. / I prefer tea to coffee.

contrasts.title: prefer to do
contrasts.note: 结构不同：prefer to do 表示“更愿意做某事”；prefer A to B 表示“两者比较下更喜欢A”。场景上，前者可不说比较对象，后者必须有对比项。对比：I prefer to study at night. / I prefer studying at night to studying in the morning.

examples.sentence: I prefer tea to coffee.
examples.translation: 比起咖啡，我更喜欢茶。
examples.note: 最安全的名词对名词基础句。

examples.sentence: On weekends, I prefer walking to driving.
examples.translation: 周末时，比起开车，我更喜欢走路。
examples.note: 前面可先放时间状语，核心结构仍是 prefer A to B。

examples.sentence: Many students prefer studying alone to working in groups.
examples.translation: 许多学生比起小组合作，更喜欢独自学习。
examples.note: 动作对比时用动词-ing 对 动词-ing。

examples.sentence: In formal writing, some people prefer “however” to “but”.
examples.translation: 在正式写作中，有些人比起 but 更喜欢使用 however。
examples.note: 书面语场景，比较语言选择。

examples.sentence: I prefer reading at home to watching TV outside.
examples.translation: 比起在外面看电视，我更喜欢在家看书。
examples.note: 容易和 would rather 混淆；这里表达一般偏好，不是临时决定。

examples.sentence: She prefers buses to taxis because they are cheaper.
examples.translation: 比起出租车，她更喜欢坐公交车，因为更便宜。
examples.note: 后面可接原因说明。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: A、B先都用名词短语，最不容易错，如 tea / coffee。
templates.exampleSentence: I prefer summer to winter.
templates.exampleTranslation: 比起冬天，我更喜欢夏天。

templates.label: 动作偏好模板
templates.template: Subject + prefer + doing A + to + doing B.
slotHints: A、B都用动词-ing，不能一个用 doing、一个用动词原形。
templates.exampleSentence: I prefer listening to music to watching TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 比起看电视，我更喜欢听音乐。

templates.label: 前置状语模板
templates.template: Time/Place phrase, + subject + prefer + A + to + B.
slotHints: 可先放 On weekends, At home, In class 等；逗号后再放完整 prefer 结构。
templates.exampleSentence: At home, I prefer rice to bread.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在家里，比起面包我更喜欢米饭。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
templates.template: In writing/formal situations, + subject + prefer + A + to + B.
slotHints: 适合写作、报告、说明风格选择；A、B常是词语、表达方式或做法。
templates.exampleSentence: In academic writing, people often prefer clear examples to vague claims.
templates.exampleTranslation: 在学术写作中，人们常常比起含糊的说法更喜欢清晰的例子。

items.title: it's + adjective + to...
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用it is/was + 形容词 + to + 动词原形来评价“做某事”怎么样，如容易、重要、危险、礼貌。真正的动作放在to后面，it只是形式主语。最常见放在句首：It is hard to..., 也可接for/of短语补充人。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常用，适合表达看法、建议、提醒、说明规则：It’s important to sleep early. 不适合直接叙述已经发生的具体动作结果，这时更自然用普通主语。常放句首，句子较长时不要把to短语拆开。初学者先稳用“It's + easy/important/hard/safe + to + 动词原形”。

slotSchema.label: 形容词
slotSchema.hint: 放评价性形容词，如 important, easy, hard, useful, dangerous。常见错误：用副词，如 ~importantly。应说 It is important to study. 不是 It is importantly to study.

slotSchema.label: 动词原形
slotSchema.hint: 放to后面的动词原形，如 learn, drive, arrive。常见错误：to后用ing或过去式，如 ~to പഠing/~to studied。应说 It is useful to learn English.

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 可加宾语、地点、时间等，如 to finish the work on time。常见错误：只放名词不放动词，如 ~It is important to homework。应改为 It is important to do homework.

slotSchema.label: for/of短语
slotSchema.hint: 可加 for + 人 表示“对某人来说”，或 of + 人 表示评价人的品质。常见错误：混用，如 ~It is kind for you to help。应说 It is kind of you to help / It is hard for you to help.

commonErrors: It is important study English.｜错因：to不能省略，后面需要不定式。｜改法：It is important to study English.
It is important to studying English.｜错因：to后要接动词原形，不接动名词。｜改法：It is important to study English.
Is important to be on time.｜错因：缺少形式主语it。｜改法：It is important to be on time.
It is kindly to answer quickly.｜错因：kindly通常是副词；这里要用形容词。｜改法：It is kind to answer quickly. 若评价人，更自然：It is kind of you to answer quickly.
It is important that to sleep early.｜错因：that从句和to不定式不能这样混搭。｜改法：It is important to sleep early. / It is important that you sleep early.
It is very difficult for understand this.｜错因：for后面若出现，通常要接人；动词前仍需to。｜改法：It is very difficult to understand this. / It is very difficult for me to understand this.

contrasts.title: it is + adjective + that...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：本结构后接to + 动词原形；that结构后接完整从句。语气上，that更像正式说明或要求。比较：It is important to arrive early.（早点到很重要）/ It is important that you arrive early.（你必须早点到，书面感更强）

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：too + 形容词 + to表示“太……而不能……”，本结构只是评价动作。语气上too更强调负面结果。比较：It is hard to carry the box.（搬箱子很难）/ The box is too heavy to carry.（箱子太重，搬不了）

contrasts.title: for + 人 vs of + 人
contrasts.note: 结构区别：for + 人 评价动作对谁难/重要；of + 人 评价人的性格或行为品质。语气区别明显，不能随便换。比较：It is hard for me to say no.（对我来说难）/ It is kind of you to help.（你人很好）

contrasts.title: V-ing 作主语
contrasts.note: 结构区别：也可用Studying English is important. 但本结构更常见、更自然，尤其句子长时。写作中两者都可；口语里it结构更顺。比较：To learn English is useful.（较生硬）/ It is useful to learn English.（更自然）

contrasts.title: 普通主语句
contrasts.note: 结构区别：本结构在先给评价，再说动作；普通主语句先说谁做什么。场景上，描述事实时普通句更自然。比较：It was difficult to open the door.（评价动作）/ I couldn’t open the door.（叙述事实结果）

examples.sentence: It is easy to make this salad.
examples.translation: 做这个沙拉很容易。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：形容词 + to + 动词原形。

examples.sentence: For beginners, it is important to practice every day.
examples.translation: 对初学者来说，每天练习很重要。
examples.note: 加入for短语，放句首也很自然。

examples.sentence: It is necessary to protect personal information online.
examples.translation: 在网上保护个人信息是必要的。
examples.note: 较常见于说明、写作、规则表达。

examples.sentence: It is hard to answer this question, but it is not impossible.
examples.translation: 回答这个问题很难，但也不是不可能。
examples.note: 适合和其他评价词对比使用。

examples.sentence: It is dangerous to drive when you are tired.
examples.translation: 累的时候开车很危险。
examples.note: 日常提醒和安全提示很常用。

examples.sentence: It was kind of you to wait for me.
examples.translation: 你愿意等我，真体贴。
examples.note: of you表示评价人的品质，不是单纯评价动作难易。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: [adj] 用 easy/important/hard/safe/useful 最稳
[verb] 一定用动词原形
[extra] 可加宾语、地点、时间，如 English / at night / on time
templates.exampleSentence: It is important to drink enough water.
templates.exampleTranslation: 喝足够的水很重要。

templates.label: 带对象模板
slotHints: [adj] 常用 difficult/easy/important/necessary
[person] 放 me/us/students/children 等
这是“对某人来说……”，不要用来夸人的品质
templates.exampleSentence: It is difficult for children to sit still for a long time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 对孩子来说，长时间坐着不动很难。

templates.label: 评价人的模板
slotHints: [adj] 常用 kind, nice, rude, careless, generous
[person] 常用 you/him/her/them
这是评价人的行为品质，不是表达难易
templates.exampleSentence: It is rude of him to interrupt others.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他打断别人很没礼貌。

templates.label: 写作常用模板
slotHints: 前半句先给总体评价
especially when 后面补充场景
适合说明文和短文写作
templates.exampleSentence: It is important to stay calm, especially when problems appear suddenly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 保持冷静很重要，尤其是在问题突然出现时。

items.title: there is / there are
items.coreExplanation: there is / there are 用来先介绍“某处有某人/某物/某情况存在”。基本结构是 There is/are + 名词(+ 地点/时间)。there 放句首作形式主语，真正的信息通常在后面的名词；地点常放句末，也可先说地点再接完整句。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常用，适合描述房间、城市、活动安排、问题或变化。它用于“介绍存在”，不用于直接说明“谁拥有某物”。正式写作里也自然，但别和 there 表示“那里”的副词意思混淆。标点上通常独立成句；若加地点短语，最常放句末。初学者最安全的做法是先写 There is/are + 名词 + 地点。

slotSchema.label: be形式
slotSchema.hint: 用 is 或 are，按后面第一个名词决定单复数。常错：被后面的第二个名词干扰。例：There is a book and two pens on the desk.

slotSchema.label: 存在的对象
slotSchema.hint: 放人、物、事件、问题等名词短语，常配 a/an/some/no。常错：直接放动词。例：There are some students outside.

slotSchema.label: 地点/时间补充
slotSchema.hint: 常用 in/on/at/near 等短语说明“在哪儿/何时”。常错：漏掉必要地点，句子信息太空。例：There is a bank near my school.

commonErrors: ✗ There have a park near my home.：这是“存在”句，不用 have。✓ There is a park near my home.
✗ There is two books on the table.：be 动词和后面第一个名词不一致，two books 是复数。✓ There are two books on the table.
✗ There is go to school at eight.：there is/are 后面要接名词性内容，不直接接动词原形。✓ There is a school bus at eight. / We go to school at eight.
✗ A cat is there on the roof.：这句更像在说“猫在那里”，不是自然地引出新信息。✓ There is a cat on the roof.
✗ There are a lot of people is waiting.：一个存在句里不要再重复加 is。✓ There are a lot of people waiting.
✗ There is the book on the desk.：初次介绍某物时通常不用特指 the，听起来像双方已知。✓ There is a book on the desk.

contrasts.title: there is/are vs have
contrasts.note: 结构上，there is/are 表“某处存在”，have 表“某人拥有”。场景上，描述环境用 there is/are；谈所属关系用 have。对比：There is a bike outside.（外面有一辆车）/ I have a bike.（我有一辆车）

contrasts.title: there is/are vs 名词 + be + 地点
contrasts.note: 结构上，there is/are 先引出新事物；名词 + be + 地点是先说已知对象的位置。语气上，前者像“有……”，后者像“……在……”。对比：There is a cat under the bed. / The cat is under the bed.

contrasts.title: there is/are vs there is no need to
contrasts.note: 两者都以 there is 开头，但后者是固定表达，后面接 no need to + 动词，表示“没必要”。前者后面接名词，表示存在。对比：There is a problem. / There is no need to worry.

contrasts.title: there is/are vs 地点作主语 + has
contrasts.note: 英语里地点一般不用 has 来表示“某地有……”，更自然是 there is/are。口语里某些宣传语会说 The hotel has a pool，但那是“酒店配有”。对比：There is a pool at the hotel. / The hotel has a pool.

examples.sentence: There is a book on the desk.
examples.translation: 桌子上有一本书。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的单数模板。

examples.sentence: On the desk, there are two books and a notebook.
examples.translation: 桌子上有两本书和一本笔记本。
examples.note: 地点短语可前置；be 仍按后面第一个名词 two books 用 are。

examples.sentence: There is no clear answer in the report.
examples.translation: 报告中没有明确的答案。
examples.note: 书面语常见，用 no + 名词表示“不存在”。

examples.sentence: There is a meeting at three, but there are no classes after lunch.
examples.translation: 三点有个会议，不过午饭后没有课。
examples.note: 同一句里对比 is 和 are，注意单复数。

examples.sentence: There are too many cars on this street.
examples.translation: 这条街上车太多了。
examples.note: 适合描述问题或现象，不表示“拥有”。

examples.sentence: There is a new café near my apartment.
examples.translation: 我公寓附近新开了一家咖啡馆。
examples.note: 日常介绍附近新事物很自然。

templates.label: 最安全入门模板
slotHints: [noun] 放要介绍的某人/某物，先看单复数决定 is/are
[place_time] 常用地点短语，如 on the table, in my bag
templates.exampleSentence: There is a pen in my bag.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我包里有一支笔。

templates.label: 地点前置模板
slotHints: [place_time] 可前置来突出地点，后面主句不变
[noun] 仍是新信息，别改成动词
templates.exampleSentence: In our classroom, there are twenty desks.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们教室里有二十张课桌。

templates.label: 写作描述模板
slotHints: [noun] 可用抽象名词，如 a problem, no evidence
[place_time] 在写作中常是 the report, this area, the system
templates.exampleSentence: There is a serious problem in this system.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个系统里有一个严重的问题。

templates.label: 数量表达模板
slotHints: [noun] 可带 many, some, a lot of 等数量词
注意复数名词要和 are 搭配
templates.exampleSentence: There are a lot of students in the library.
templates.exampleTranslation: 图书馆里有很多学生。

items.title: there is no need to...
items.pattern: there is no need to + base
items.coreExplanation: there is no need to + 动词原形 用来表示“没有必要做某事”，语气比直接命令更委婉，常见于句首作完整句，也可接 for someone：there is no need for you to...。to 后面必须接动词原形，不能接 v-ing 或完整从句。
items.usageNote: 这个结构语气偏中性到稍正式，常用于建议、安慰、说明要求不必做，如邮件、课堂说明、礼貌提醒。口语也能用，但日常随意对话里 you don't need to / you don't have to 更常见。它通常单独放在句首成句；若前面已有主语，一般不要硬塞进去。写作中常后接逗号再补充原因：There is no need to hurry, because... 初学者先稳用 there is no need to + 动词原形。

slotSchema.label: 对象（可选）
slotSchema.hint: 可放 for + 人，表示“对谁来说没必要”。常错：直接说 there is no need you to...；应改为 there is no need for you to...。例：for you

slotSchema.label: 动作
slotSchema.hint: 这里放动词原形。常错：用 to waiting / to waited；应改为 to wait。例：wait

slotSchema.label: 补充内容
slotSchema.hint: 可放宾语、时间、原因等，让句子更完整。常错：把完整 that 从句直接接在 to 后；应改为动词短语。例：for the bus

commonErrors: there is no need to waiting now.｜错在 to 后面不能接 v-ing。｜改为 there is no need to wait now.
there is no need you to apologize.｜错在表示对象时缺少 for。｜改为 there is no need for you to apologize.
there is no need that you explain everything.｜错在这个结构后面不接 that 从句。｜改为 there is no need for you to explain everything.
you are no need to come early.｜错在把 there is 结构误写成主语补语。｜改为 there is no need to come early. 或 you don't need to come early.
there has no need to worry.｜错在 there be 结构里不能用 has。｜改为 there is no need to worry.
there is no need to be hurry.｜错在 hurry 这里是动词时不加 be；be 后应接形容词 hurried，但不自然。｜改为 there is no need to hurry.

contrasts.title: vs. you don't have to
contrasts.note: 结构上：you don't have to + 动词原形，主语明确；there is no need to + 动词原形，用 there 引出“没必要”。语气上前者更口语直接，后者更客观委婉。对比：You don't have to come. / There is no need to come.

contrasts.title: vs. you don't need to
contrasts.note: 结构上都接动词原形，但 you don't need to 以人作主语；there is no need to 不强调谁，而强调“没有必要”。场景上前者更日常，后者更像说明或安抚。对比：You don't need to explain. / There is no need to explain.

contrasts.title: vs. there is no point in
contrasts.note: 结构上：no need to + 动词原形；no point in + v-ing。意思也不同：no need 表示“不必”，no point in 表示“做了也没意义”。语气上 no point in 更否定、更强。对比：There is no need to rush. / There is no point in rushing.

contrasts.title: vs. it is + adjective + to
contrasts.note: 结构上：it is unnecessary to + 动词原形 在意义上接近，但更书面；there is no need to 更常用、更自然。前者是评价动作，后者是说“没必要做”。对比：It is unnecessary to repeat this. / There is no need to repeat this.

examples.sentence: There is no need to hurry.
examples.translation: 没有必要着急。
examples.note: 最安全的核心句型，直接表达“没必要做某事”。

examples.sentence: There is no need for you to wait outside.
examples.translation: 你没有必要在外面等。
examples.note: 加入 for + 人，明确对象。

examples.sentence: There is no need to repeat the same point in the report.
examples.translation: 没有必要在报告里重复同一个观点。
examples.note: 偏书面，适合写作或工作场景。

examples.sentence: There is no need to rush; we still have plenty of time.
examples.translation: 没必要赶，我们还有很多时间。
examples.note: 和 rush 搭配自然，也体现它不是“没意义”，只是“不必”。

examples.sentence: There is no need to bring any food because dinner will be provided.
examples.translation: 没有必要带食物，因为会提供晚餐。
examples.note: 常见于通知、活动说明。

examples.sentence: There is no need to apologize.
examples.translation: 没有必要道歉。
examples.note: 常用于安慰或缓和气氛，语气比 Don't apologize 更柔和。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
templates.template: There is no need to + VERB.
slotHints: VERB 用动词原形，表示不必做的动作，如 worry, hurry, apologize
templates.exampleSentence: There is no need to worry.
templates.exampleTranslation: 没有必要担心。

templates.label: 带对象模板
templates.template: There is no need for + PERSON + to + VERB.
slotHints: PERSON 前面一定要有 for；VERB 仍用动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: There is no need for him to come so early.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他没有必要来这么早。

templates.label: 补充原因模板
templates.template: There is no need to + VERB, because + CLAUSE.
slotHints: 前半句说“不必做”，后半句补原因；逗号后再说明更清楚
templates.exampleSentence: There is no need to call me, because I already know.
templates.exampleTranslation: 没有必要给我打电话，因为我已经知道了。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
templates.template: There is no need to + VERB + in/for + NOUN.
slotHints: 适合通知、说明文；后面可加场景词，如 in the report, for this task
templates.exampleSentence: There is no need to include personal details in the form.
templates.exampleTranslation: 没有必要在表格中填写个人细节。

items.title: the reason is that...
items.coreExplanation: the reason is that + 从句 用来把“原因”明确说出来。that 后面必须是完整句子，通常有主语和谓语，如 the reason is that I was late。它常放在句首或句中，先点出“原因”，再补充细节，适合解释、说明、写作。
items.usageNote: 这个句型语气中性偏正式，常见于解释原因、做陈述、写邮件、作文和讨论观点。口语也能用，但日常闲聊里常直接说 because。它适合回答“为什么”，不适合直接给结果或建议。that 后接完整句子，不要只放名词。the reason 后面常直接接 is that，不要乱加逗号；句首句中都可以。初学者先安全套用：The reason is that + 主语 + 动词...

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 放人、事、代词都可以，如 I / she / the train。常见错误是省略主语，导致 that 后不是完整句子。例：that I missed the bus。

slotSchema.label: 谓语部分
slotSchema.hint: 放完整谓语，如 was late / forgot my keys / costs so much。常见错误是只放名词或形容词片段。例：that it was expensive。

slotSchema.label: 前文要解释的事
slotSchema.hint: 可在前面加 The reason I was late is that... 或单独用 The reason is that...。常见错误是前文和原因不对应。例：The reason I left early is that I felt sick。

commonErrors: ✗ The reason is because I was tired. —— 错在把 reason 和 because 重复表达了原因。✓ 改成 The reason is that I was tired. 或 I left because I was tired.
✗ The reason is that my lateness. —— 错在 that 后面不是完整句子，只是名词短语。✓ 改成 The reason is that I was late. 或 The reason for my lateness is traffic.
✗ The reason, is that I missed the bus. —— 错在主干中间随意加逗号，读起来不自然。✓ 改成 The reason is that I missed the bus.
✗ The reason I was late is because I missed the bus. —— 虽口语里有人说，但基础学习中不推荐；reason 后面更稳妥的是 that。✓ 改成 The reason I was late is that I missed the bus.
✗ The reason is that because it rained. —— 错在 that 和 because 不能一起引出同一原因。✓ 改成 The reason is that it rained. 或 Because it rained, ...
✗ The reason is that to save money. —— 错在 that 后不能直接接 to do。✓ 改成 The reason is that I want to save money. 或 The reason is to save money.

contrasts.title: that’s why + 结果
contrasts.note: 结构上，the reason is that + 原因从句；that’s why + 结果分句。语用上，前者强调“原因是什么”，后者强调“所以怎样”。比较：The reason is that I was sick. / I was sick. That’s why I stayed home.

contrasts.title: because + 原因
contrasts.note: 结构上，because 常直接接在结果句后：结果 + because + 原因；the reason is that 是名词框架句。语气上，because 更日常，the reason is that 更明确、更适合写作。比较：I left because I was tired. / The reason is that I was tired.

contrasts.title: the fact that + 从句
contrasts.note: 结构上，the fact that 表示“这个事实”，不是专门讲原因；the reason is that 专门回答“为什么”。语气上，the fact that 更书面、更强调事实本身。比较：The fact that he lied shocked me. / The reason is that he was afraid.

contrasts.title: the reason for + 名词
contrasts.note: 结构上，the reason is that 后接完整句；the reason for 后接名词或动名词。场景上，for 结构更适合短语化表达。比较：The reason is that prices went up. / The reason for the increase is higher costs.

examples.sentence: The reason is that I missed the bus.
examples.translation: 原因是我错过了公交车。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：that 后面放完整句子。

examples.sentence: The reason I was late is that I missed the bus.
examples.translation: 我迟到的原因是我错过了公交车。
examples.note: 把要解释的事情放在 reason 前面，更具体。

examples.sentence: The main reason is that many students lack enough sleep.
examples.translation: 主要原因是许多学生睡眠不足。
examples.note: 写作中常在 reason 前加 main / real / simple 等词。

examples.sentence: The reason is that it is cheap, not that it is good.
examples.translation: 原因是它便宜，不是因为它好。
examples.note: 适合与其他原因做对比，注意 not that 的对照。

examples.sentence: I don’t want to go out. The reason is that I’m really tired.
examples.translation: 我不想出门。原因是我真的很累。
examples.note: 可放在前一句之后，单独成句解释。

examples.sentence: The reason for the delay is that the road was closed.
examples.translation: 延误的原因是道路被封了。
examples.note: 前半部分可用 reason for + 名词，后半部分仍用 is that + 从句。

templates.label: 安全基础模板
slotHints: {subject} 放主语，如 I / she / the train
{predicate} 放完整谓语部分，如 was late / broke down
templates.exampleSentence: The reason is that the train was late.
templates.exampleTranslation: 原因是火车晚点了。

templates.label: 句中具体模板
slotHints: {context} 放要解释的事，如 I was late / we changed the plan
{subject} 和 {predicate} 组成完整从句
templates.exampleSentence: The reason we changed the plan is that it was too expensive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们改变计划的原因是它太贵了。

templates.label: 写作常用模板
slotHints: 在 reason 前加 main 可让表达更像书面说明
that 后仍然必须是完整句
templates.exampleSentence: The main reason is that many people do not have enough time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 主要原因是很多人没有足够的时间。

items.title: how about + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: how about后面常接名词或动词-ing，用来随意提出建议、备选方案，意思接近“……怎么样？”。它通常放在句首单独成问句，也可先提话题再接how about。后面不用动词原形，也不要直接接完整陈述句。
items.usageNote: 语气中性偏口语，最常见于聊天、约人、讨论计划、换方案时，听起来比命令更柔和。适合当场提议或顺手补充选项；不太适合非常正式的商务写作或强烈要求别人执行的场景。常单独成一句并加问号。初学者最稳妥的做法是先用“How about + 名词/动词-ing?”，如How about pizza? 或 How about going by bus?

slotSchema.label: 建议内容
slotSchema.hint: 可放名词短语或动词-ing短语。常错：用动词原形。要说How about going home? 不说How about go home?

slotSchema.label: 前置话题
slotSchema.hint: 可先说一个话题，再接How about...。常错：前后内容不连贯。例：We need dinner. How about noodles?

commonErrors: How about go there?｜错在about后不能直接接动词原形。｜改成How about going there?
How about to go there?｜错在这里不用to不定式。｜改成How about going there?
How about we go there?｜这个句子在口语里能听到，但本卡基础用法要求about后接名词或V-ing，更稳妥。｜初学先用How about going there?
How about is pizza?｜错在about后不能接倒装问句。｜改成How about pizza?
I like coffee, how about go out?｜前半句后又接原形动词，不自然。｜改成I like coffee. How about going out?
How about?｜只有框架没有内容，信息不完整。｜至少补一个名词或V-ing，如How about Friday?

contrasts.title: How about vs What about
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接名词或V-ing。语气和场景很接近，但what about更常用于追问“那……呢”，how about更常用于主动提议。对比：How about going now?＝提议；What about Tom?＝那汤姆呢？

contrasts.title: How about vs Why don't we...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：how about后接名词/V-ing，why don't we后接动词原形。后者更直接、更像明确行动提议；how about更像抛出选项。对比：How about taking a taxi? / Why don't we take a taxi?

contrasts.title: How about vs Would you like to...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：how about + V-ing/noun；would you like to + 动词原形。后者更礼貌，更常用于邀请对方；how about更随意，适合共同商量。对比：How about joining us? / Would you like to join us?

contrasts.title: How about vs Let's...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：let's + 动词原形；how about + V-ing/noun。语气上let's更直接，像已经决定往前走；how about更保留，给对方回应空间。对比：Let's leave now. / How about leaving now?

examples.sentence: How about coffee?
examples.translation: 喝咖啡怎么样？
examples.note: 最简单安全：直接接名词。

examples.sentence: We can walk to the station. Or how about taking the bus?
examples.translation: 我们可以走去车站。或者坐公交怎么样？
examples.note: 放在后一句，作为补充方案。

examples.sentence: For the next meeting, how about inviting a local speaker?
examples.translation: 下次会议，请一位本地讲者怎么样？
examples.note: 较适合计划讨论或半书面表达。

examples.sentence: How about going by train instead of driving?
examples.translation: 坐火车代替开车怎么样？
examples.note: 体现与其他方案比较时很常用。

examples.sentence: It's cold outside. How about staying in tonight?
examples.translation: 外面很冷。今晚待在家怎么样？
examples.note: 日常聊天中非常自然。

examples.sentence: We still need dessert. How about some ice cream?
examples.translation: 我们还需要甜点。来点冰淇淋怎么样？
examples.note: 可接some+名词，表示提议。

templates.label: 最稳妥初学模板
slotHints: {x}放名词短语或动词-ing短语，不要用动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: How about watching a movie tonight?
templates.exampleTranslation: 今晚看电影怎么样？

templates.label: 先说情况再提议
slotHints: {topic}放前情或需求；{x}放解决方案，常用V-ing
templates.exampleSentence: We need a quick lunch. How about getting sandwiches?
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们需要快点吃午饭。买三明治怎么样？

templates.label: 备选方案模板
slotHints: {x}常放V-ing或名词；{topic}放原方案，形成对比
templates.exampleSentence: How about meeting online instead of at the office?
templates.exampleTranslation: 不在办公室见，改成线上见怎么样？

templates.label: 讨论/写作用模板
slotHints: {topic}放讨论主题；{x}放建议内容，常见于计划讨论
templates.exampleSentence: For Friday night, how about cooking at home?
templates.exampleTranslation: 周五晚上在家做饭怎么样？

items.title: would you like to...
items.coreExplanation: would you like to + 动词原形 用来礼貌地邀请别人做某事，或礼貌地提出让对方做某事。to 后面必须接动词原形，不能接名词或 -ing。它通常放在句首作完整问句，后面常接时间、地点等补充信息。
items.usageNote: 语气礼貌、自然，常用于邀请、提议、招待客人、安排活动，也可用于服务场景如点餐或提供帮助。比 do you want to 更客气。一般用于问句，句末通常用问号；口语中也可先说 Would you like to... 再补充细节。不适合强硬催促、命令，或已经确定安排时。初学者先安全地用：Would you like to + 动词原形 + 其他信息?

slotSchema.label: 动词原形
slotSchema.hint: 这里放动词原形，如 come, join, try, sit。常错：写成 to going / to went。例：Would you like to join us?

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 这里放时间、地点、对象等，如 tonight, with us, for lunch。常错：补充信息位置太乱。例：Would you like to have dinner with us tonight?

slotSchema.label: 可选宾语或内容
slotSchema.hint: 有些动词后要接宾语，如 try this cake, watch a movie。常错：漏掉必要宾语。例：Would you like to try this cake?

commonErrors: Would you like going with us?｜错在 like to 后面要接动词原形，不接 -ing。｜改为：Would you like to go with us?
Would you like to a coffee?｜错在 to 后面不能直接接名词。表达“想要某物”时常用 would you like + 名词。｜改为：Would you like a coffee? 或 Would you like to have a coffee?
Do you like to join us?｜错在把 would you like to 和 do you like 混了。do you like 是问喜不喜欢，不能自然表示当下邀请。｜改为：Would you like to join us?
Would you like to joined us?｜错在 to 后面不能接过去式。｜改为：Would you like to join us?
You would like to come with us?｜语法不一定错，但作邀请时语气不自然，像在确认对方想法。｜更自然：Would you like to come with us?
Would you like to not go alone?｜否定位置别扭，不像自然邀请。｜更自然：Would you like to come with me? 或 Would you prefer not to go alone?

contrasts.title: vs do you want to...
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接动词原形，但 would you like to 更礼貌、更委婉；do you want to 更直接，常用于熟人。邀请客人时更适合前者。对比：Would you like to sit down?（更客气）/ Do you want to sit down?（更直接）

contrasts.title: vs how about + V-ing
contrasts.note: 结构不同：would you like to + 动词原形；how about + 动词-ing 或名词。前者更像直接询问对方意愿，后者更像提出建议。对比：Would you like to go for a walk? / How about going for a walk?

contrasts.title: vs would you like + noun
contrasts.note: 结构不同：would you like to 后接动作；would you like + 名词 后接东西。前者是“要不要做…”，后者是“要不要某物”。服务场景两者都很常见。对比：Would you like to sit inside? / Would you like a table inside?

contrasts.title: vs can you...
contrasts.note: 结构上 can you + 动词原形 常是请求对方做事；would you like to + 动词原形 是邀请或提议。前者可能像让别人帮忙，后者更像给对方选择。对比：Can you join us?（像在确认能力/安排）/ Would you like to join us?（礼貌邀请）

contrasts.title: vs let's...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：let's + 动词原形 是说话人直接提议“我们来…吧”；would you like to... 是先询问对方意愿。前者更主动，后者更礼貌。对比：Let's have lunch. / Would you like to have lunch with me?

examples.sentence: Would you like to come with us?
examples.translation: 你想跟我们一起去吗？
examples.note: 最安全的基础邀请句。

examples.sentence: Would you like to join us for dinner tonight?
examples.translation: 你今晚想和我们一起吃晚饭吗？
examples.note: 在动词后面加活动和时间信息。

examples.sentence: Would you like to sit by the window?
examples.translation: 您想坐在窗边吗？
examples.note: 常见于餐厅、服务场景。

examples.sentence: Would you like to discuss this later, or do you want to decide now?
examples.translation: 你想稍后讨论这个，还是现在就决定？
examples.note: 同句对比 would like to 和 want to，前者更礼貌。

examples.sentence: Would you like to review the attached document before the meeting?
examples.translation: 您想在会议前查看所附文件吗？
examples.note: 较书面、较正式的工作表达。

examples.sentence: Would you like to try this cake?
examples.translation: 你想尝尝这块蛋糕吗？
examples.note: 日常招待别人时很自然。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: {action} 填动词原形，如 come, join, try
{extra} 填时间、地点或对象，如 with us, tonight
templates.exampleSentence: Would you like to come with us tonight?
templates.exampleTranslation: 你今晚想和我们一起去吗？

templates.label: 带宾语模板
slotHints: {action} 填及物动词原形，如 try, watch, discuss
{subject2} 填宾语，如 this dish, a movie, the plan
templates.exampleSentence: Would you like to watch a movie?
templates.exampleTranslation: 你想看电影吗？

templates.label: 补充地点的替换模板
slotHints: {action} 填动词原形，如 meet, eat, sit
{extra} 可填 with me / for lunch，也可留空
{subject2} 填地点，如 the café, my office
templates.exampleSentence: Would you like to meet me at the café?
templates.exampleTranslation: 你想在咖啡馆见我吗？

templates.label: 较书面模板
slotHints: {action} 填较正式动词原形，如 review, discuss, confirm
{subject2} 填内容，如 the report, the details
{extra} 填时间点，如 the meeting, tomorrow
templates.exampleSentence: Would you like to review the report before the meeting?
templates.exampleTranslation: 您想在会议前看一下这份报告吗？

items.title: make / let / help + object + do
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构表示“使、让、帮某人做某事”。基本形式是make/let/help+宾语+动词原形，宾语通常是人或动物，后面不能直接用动词过去式。它常出现在谓语位置，放在主语后面：主语+make/let/help+人+do...。
items.usageNote: 语气上，make常带“迫使/导致”，let是“允许”，help是“帮助”，都属中性常用口语和书面语。适合写日常安排、规则、影响、协助。不适合把make误当成礼貌请求；请求更常用ask/tell。宾语一般紧跟在三个动词后，不要在中间乱加to；只有help后面to有时可出现，但初学者先统一用do更稳。

slotSchema.label: 使役/许可/帮助动词
slotSchema.hint: 放make、let或help。常错：把三个词混用，尤其把make当“允许”。例：My mom lets me stay up late.

slotSchema.label: 对象
slotSchema.hint: 放人、代词或动物，如me, him, the kids。常错：漏掉宾语。例：The teacher made us rewrite it.

slotSchema.label: 动作
slotSchema.hint: 放动词原形，如go, clean, finish。常错：写成to go或went。例：This app helps me learn words.

commonErrors: make me to go：错在make后面不用to。应改为make me go。
let him goes：错在后面要用动词原形，不用第三人称单数。应改为let him go。
help us finished：错在help后面不是过去式。应改为help us finish。
My teacher made study harder：错在漏了宾语，必须是make+人+do。应改为My teacher made me study harder。
I let to use my phone：错在let后面先接对象，再接原形动词。应改为I let him use my phone. / He let me use his phone。
She made me happy to smile：想表达“让我微笑”时结构放错了。应改为She made me smile；若表达“让我开心”，则是She made me happy。

contrasts.title: vs ask someone to do
contrasts.note: 结构不同：ask+人+to do；本卡是make/let/help+人+do。语气不同：ask是“请求/请”，更礼貌；make是“迫使/导致”，let是“允许”。对比：I asked him to stay.（我请他留下）/ I made him stay.（我逼他留下）

contrasts.title: vs tell someone to do
contrasts.note: 结构不同：tell+人+to do；本卡不用to。场景不同：tell常用于命令、指示；let表示许可。对比：She told me to wait.（她叫我等）/ She let me wait inside.（她让我在里面等）

contrasts.title: help someone do vs help someone to do
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都对，但本卡主推help+人+do，更简洁、更常见于口语。语气差别很小。对比：Can you help me carry this? = Can you help me to carry this? 初学者先固定用carry。

contrasts.title: vs make someone + adjective
contrasts.note: 结构不同：make+人+do表示“使某人做动作”；make+人+形容词表示“使某人处于某状态”。意义不同。对比：The joke made me laugh.（让我笑）/ The joke made me happy.（让我开心）

contrasts.title: let someone do vs allow someone to do
contrasts.note: 结构不同：let+人+do；allow+人+to do。语体不同：let更口语、更自然；allow较正式，常见于规则通知。对比：My dad lets me drive. / The school allows students to use tablets.

examples.sentence: My mom lets me watch TV after dinner.
examples.translation: 我妈妈让我晚饭后看电视。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的let句型。

examples.sentence: This song makes me think of home.
examples.translation: 这首歌让我想到家。
examples.note: 宾语也可以是代词me，后面接动词原形think。

examples.sentence: Can you help me carry this box?
examples.translation: 你能帮我搬这个箱子吗？
examples.note: help常用于日常求助，口语很自然。

examples.sentence: The teacher made us do the exercise again.
examples.translation: 老师让我们把这个练习再做一遍。
examples.note: 这里make带有“要求/迫使”意味，比ask更强。

examples.sentence: These measures help students develop good study habits.
examples.translation: 这些措施有助于帮助学生养成良好的学习习惯。
examples.note: 较偏书面表达，主语可以是抽象事物。

examples.sentence: She asked me to clean the room, but my brother let me rest first.
examples.translation: 她叫我打扫房间，但我哥哥先让我休息。
examples.note: 同时对比ask+to do和let+do，能看清结构差异。

templates.label: 安全基础模板
slotHints: Subject放人或事物，如My mom / This app
object放me/him/us等
base verb用原形，如go, study, carry
templates.exampleSentence: My friend helped me fix my bike.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我朋友帮我修了自行车。

templates.label: 疑问句模板
templates.template: Can/Did + subject + let/make/help + object + base verb?
slotHints: 用Can提请求或询问能力；用Did问过去发生的事
不要把base verb改成过去式
templates.exampleSentence: Did the movie make you cry?
templates.exampleTranslation: 那部电影让你哭了吗？

templates.label: 带时间/地点补充模板
templates.template: Subject + let/make/help + object + base verb + time/place detail
slotHints: 时间地点通常放在动作后面
不要插到object和base verb中间
templates.exampleSentence: My dad lets me use his car on weekends.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我爸爸周末让我用他的车。

templates.label: 写作用模板
slotHints: 主语可用抽象名词，如technology, exercise, rules
group常用students, people, children
templates.exampleSentence: Regular reading helps children build vocabulary.
templates.exampleTranslation: 规律阅读有助于孩子积累词汇。

items.title: have trouble + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: have trouble + v-ing 表示“做某事有困难”。trouble 后面通常接动词的 -ing 形式，不接动词原形。最常见在主语后作谓语，如 I have trouble sleeping. 也常和 with 连用：have trouble with sth / doing sth。
items.usageNote: 这个结构语气中性，适合口语自述、说明学习困难、描述生活问题，如睡眠、记单词、找路。它强调“过程不顺、做起来困难”，不一定表示完全不能做。一般放在主语后面；若句子较长，可在后面接原因或时间。写作和口语都自然。初学者最安全的做法是直接用“主语 + have/has trouble + v-ing”。

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 放人或事物，如 I, she, my dad。常见错误是主谓不一致，如 She have trouble...；应改为 She has trouble...。例：My brother has trouble reading maps.

slotSchema.label: 困难的活动
slotSchema.hint: 放动词的 -ing 形式，表示具体活动。常见错误是用原形，如 have trouble sleep；应改为 have trouble sleeping。例：I have trouble waking up early.

slotSchema.label: 补充信息
slotSchema.hint: 可放时间、地点、对象或原因，如 at night, in class, with names。常见错误是位置太乱；初学者可放句尾。例：She has trouble concentrating in noisy rooms.

commonErrors: have trouble do homework：错在 trouble 后面不能直接接动词原形。应改为 have trouble doing homework。
I am have trouble sleeping：错在 have 结构前又加 am。这里直接用 have/has 作谓语。应改为 I have trouble sleeping。
She have trouble remembering names：错在第三人称单数。应改为 She has trouble remembering names。
I have trouble to understand this word：错在这里不用 to do。应改为 I have trouble understanding this word。
I have a trouble sleeping：错在 trouble 这里通常不可数，不加 a。应改为 I have trouble sleeping。
I have trouble with sleep：表达不自然。若说活动困难，用 have trouble sleeping；若说某方面有问题，可说 have trouble with my sleep。

contrasts.title: can't + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 结构上 can't 后接动词原形，have trouble 后接 v-ing。语气上 can't 更强，常表示“不能/不会”；have trouble 较弱，表示“做起来困难”。比较：I can't sleep.＝我睡不着；I have trouble sleeping.＝我入睡有困难。

contrasts.title: be good at + v-ing
contrasts.note: 两者都可接 v-ing，但意思相反：be good at 表示擅长，have trouble 表示困难。语气上前者偏能力优势，后者偏问题描述。比较：She is good at making friends. / She has trouble making friends.

contrasts.title: have problems + v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构相近，problems 常用复数；have trouble 更固定、更口语化。两者都能说困难，但 trouble 更像“做起来不顺”。比较：I have trouble sleeping. / I have problems sleeping. 前者更常见。

contrasts.title: It's + 形容词 + to do
contrasts.note: It's hard to do 是评价某事“难”，主语常是形式主语 it；have trouble doing 是说某人“做起来有困难”，主语通常是真实的人。比较：It's hard to learn Chinese. / I have trouble learning Chinese.

contrasts.title: have trouble with + 名词
contrasts.note: 结构区别在于后面可接名词而不是 v-ing。with + 名词更强调“在哪方面有问题”；v-ing 更强调“做这个动作有困难”。比较：I have trouble with math. / I have trouble solving math problems.

examples.sentence: I have trouble sleeping.
examples.translation: 我睡觉有困难。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的基础句型。

examples.sentence: She has trouble remembering names at parties.
examples.translation: 她在聚会上记人名有困难。
examples.note: 句尾加场景信息很自然。

examples.sentence: Many students have trouble understanding this grammar point.
examples.translation: 许多学生理解这个语法点有困难。
examples.note: 适合课堂说明或学习反思。

examples.sentence: I can't hear well, so I have trouble following the conversation.
examples.translation: 我听力不太好，所以我很难跟上对话。
examples.note: 和 can't 对比：不是完全不能，只是跟起来吃力。

examples.sentence: He often has trouble getting up early on weekdays.
examples.translation: 他工作日经常起得早有困难。
examples.note: 经常性、重复性动作尤其适合这个结构。

examples.sentence: Some older people may have trouble using this app.
examples.translation: 一些老年人可能使用这个应用有困难。
examples.note: 较书面，但仍很自然。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: subject 放人称或名词；activity 一定用 v-ing，如 sleeping, understanding
templates.exampleSentence: I have trouble understanding fast English.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我理解语速快的英语有困难。

templates.label: 带场景补充模板
slotHints: extra 可放时间、地点、对象，如 at night, in class, with new software
templates.exampleSentence: She has trouble concentrating in class.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她上课时很难集中注意力。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
slotHints: 适合写一般现象；subject 常用 students, children, people 等
templates.exampleSentence: Many students have trouble using articles correctly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 许多学生正确使用冠词有困难。

templates.label: 原因连接模板
slotHints: 前半句写困难，后半句写结果，如 so he asks for help
templates.exampleSentence: I have trouble remembering words, so I review them every day.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我记单词有困难，所以我每天复习。

items.title: not only... but also...
items.pattern: not only A but also B
items.coreExplanation: not only...but also... 用来强调“ 不但A，而且B ”。A和B最好语法上平行：都接名词、形容词、动词短语或分句。最常放在主语后、动词前；若句首用 Not only 开头，前半句常要部分倒装，后半句正常语序。
items.usageNote: 语气比 and、also 更强调，常见于说明优点、列举理由、正式写作、演讲和较认真口语。适合表达“第二点更值得注意”。不适合只随便补一句时，那样用 also 更自然。写作中注意 A 和 B 的结构尽量对称；若句首写 Not only..., 前半句后常加逗号，且前半句要倒装。初学者最安全做法：先别句首前置，直接用 主语 + not only + 动词/补语 + but also + 对应成分。

slotSchema.label: first item
slotSchema.hint: 放第一个信息点，最好和 B 词性一致，如两个名词、两个形容词或两个动词短语。常见错误是 A 用名词而 B 用从句。例：She is not only kind but also patient.

slotSchema.label: second item
slotSchema.hint: 放第二个、被强调的信息点，通常比 A 更“加码”。不要漏掉 also；正式写作里 but also 很常见。例：The job is not only challenging but also rewarding.

slotSchema.label: subject
slotSchema.hint: 若不用句首前置，主语通常放在 not only 前面。常见错误是把 not only 放错到主语前导致倒装需求。例：My phone not only looks good but also works well.

slotSchema.label: fronted clause
slotSchema.hint: 句首用 Not only 开头时，这一部分常要部分倒装：Not only does he sing..., but he also writes.... 常见错误是仍用正常语序。

commonErrors: Wrong: He not only likes music but also to dance. 原因：A 是动词 likes music，B 却是不定式 to dance，结构不平行。修改：He likes not only music but also dancing./He not only likes music but also enjoys dancing.
Wrong: Not only he likes coffee, but also tea. 原因：句首 Not only 引出的前半句需要部分倒装。修改：Not only does he like coffee, but he also likes tea.
Wrong: She is not only smart but beautiful also. 原因：also 位置不对，固定搭配通常是 but also + B。修改：She is not only smart but also beautiful.
Wrong: He not only speaks English, but also can speak French. 原因：后半句助动词位置别扭，前后不够对称。修改：He can speak not only English but also French./He not only speaks English but also speaks French.
Wrong: Not only the room was clean, but also comfortable. 原因：句首前置后前半句缺少倒装，后半句也缺主语系动词。修改：Not only was the room clean, but it was also comfortable.
Wrong: I not only went to the store but also. 原因：but also 后必须有完整对应内容。修改：I not only went to the store but also bought some milk.

contrasts.title: both A and B
contrasts.note: 结构上 both A and B 只表示“两者都”，强调较弱；not only A but also B 有递进感，B 更像补充亮点。场景上，both...and... 更中性日常；not only...but also... 更适合强调。对比：She is both smart and kind. / She is not only smart but also kind.

contrasts.title: also
contrasts.note: 结构上 also 通常只是额外补充一个信息，不要求严格平行；not only...but also... 明显把 A、B 成对并列。语气上 also 更轻，适合普通口语；not only...but also... 更突出。对比：He is busy. He is also tired. / He is not only busy but also tired.

contrasts.title: and
contrasts.note: 结构上 and 只是连接，不自带强调；not only...but also... 要求两边内容尽量平行。语气上 and 最自然随意；not only...but also... 更像“而且更重要的是”。对比：The course is useful and practical. / The course is not only useful but also practical.

contrasts.title: not just... but...
contrasts.note: 结构上 not just... but... 与 not only... but also... 很接近，但前者更口语，后者更完整、更书面。场景上聊天里 not just... but... 常见；作文里 not only...but also... 更稳妥。对比：It’s not just cheap, but reliable. / It’s not only cheap but also reliable.

contrasts.title: fronted Not only...
contrasts.note: 结构差别在位置：普通语序是 Subject + not only... but also...；句首前置是 Not only + 助动词/系动词 + 主语...。前置更正式、更有文采，但更容易错。对比：She not only sings but also writes songs. / Not only does she sing, but she also writes songs.

examples.sentence: She is not only kind but also patient.
examples.translation: 她不仅善良，而且有耐心。
examples.note: 最基础安全例句：连接两个形容词。

examples.sentence: My brother not only cooks dinner but also washes the dishes.
examples.translation: 我哥哥不仅做晚饭，而且还洗碗。
examples.note: 普通语序，主语放前面，最适合初学者。

examples.sentence: Not only did the company reduce costs, but it also improved quality.
examples.translation: 这家公司不仅降低了成本，而且还提高了质量。
examples.note: 句首前置，前半句部分倒装，偏写作和正式表达。

examples.sentence: This book is both useful and interesting, but this course is not only useful but also practical.
examples.translation: 这本书既有用也有趣，但这门课不仅有用，而且很实用。
examples.note: 与 both...and... 对比：后者强调更强。

examples.sentence: He can speak not only English but also Spanish.
examples.translation: 他不仅会说英语，而且还会说西班牙语。
examples.note: 这里连接两个名词，放在动词 speak 后面也很自然。

examples.sentence: The trip was not only fun but also educational.
examples.translation: 这次旅行不仅好玩，而且很有教育意义。
examples.note: 常用于作文和总结感受。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: subject 放人或物；A 和 B 用同类成分，最安全是两个形容词、名词或动词短语。
templates.exampleSentence: The restaurant is not only cheap but also clean.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这家餐馆不仅便宜，而且干净。

templates.label: Alternate-position template
slotHints: 当 A、B 是宾语或介词短语时，这个位置很自然；不要把 A、B 弄成不同结构。
templates.exampleSentence: She bought not only fruit but also some bread.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她不仅买了水果，还买了一些面包。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
slotHints: 前半句要倒装；后半句用正常语序。适合正式写作、演讲、总结。
templates.exampleSentence: Not only was the plan affordable, but it was also effective.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个计划不仅负担得起，而且还有效。

templates.label: Verb pair template
templates.template: [subject] + not only + [verb phrase A] + but also + [verb phrase B]
slotHints: 两个动词短语尽量形式一致，如 both 用原形结构或都用第三人称单数。
templates.exampleSentence: He not only studies hard but also helps his classmates.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他不仅学习努力，而且还帮助同学。

items.title: the more..., the more...
items.pattern: the more X, the more Y
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构表示“X 的变化会带来 Y 的相应变化”，常用于说明规律、趋势或因果联动。基本形式是 the + 比较级..., the + 比较级...；两个部分都要有“比较”的意思。常放在整句开头，用逗号分开，前半写条件变化，后半写结果变化。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都很常见，特别适合讲学习规律、价格趋势、习惯影响、分析结果。它自然用于“越……越……”这类联动，不适合只说一次性的事实，也不适合没有比较含义的普通并列。通常前半句在前、后半句在后，中间用逗号。初学者最安全的做法是先写 the more + 主语 + 动词, the more + 主语 + 动词。

slotSchema.label: 前半变化 X
slotSchema.hint: 这里放一个有比较级的分句，表示“越……”。常见内容：主语 + 动词 + the 比较级。常错：只写 the more 后面不写比较对象。例：you practice, not you practice hard.

slotSchema.label: 后半结果 Y
slotSchema.hint: 这里也放一个有比较级的分句，表示“就越……”。常见内容：主语 + 动词/be + the 比较级。常错：后半句漏掉 the。例：you become, the more confident.

slotSchema.label: 比较内容
slotSchema.hint: 常放形容词或副词比较级，如 better, faster, more confident。常错：把原级直接放进去。例：more carefully，不是 carefully.
