commonErrors: the more you practice, you become better：后半句缺少对应的 the + 比较级结构，两个部分没有对称起来；改为 the more you practice, the better you become.
the more you are careful, the fewer mistakes you make：careful 通常不用 are careful 来做这个结构里的自然表达，听起来别扭；改为 the more careful you are, the fewer mistakes you make.
the more you practice, the more better you get：better 已经是比较级，前面不能再加 more；改为 the more you practice, the better you get.
you practice more, the more you improve：前半句没有用固定结构 the + 比较级，形式不完整；改为 the more you practice, the more you improve.
the more expensive it is the less people buy it：两个分句之间通常要用逗号分开，尤其在写作中；改为 the more expensive it is, the less people buy it.
the more you study, the more you will pass the exam：后半句不是可渐变的比较结果，pass 也不是比较概念；改为 the more you study, the more confident/prepared you will feel.

contrasts.title: vs as...as...
contrasts.note: 结构上，as...as... 是“同等比较”，the more..., the more... 是“双向变化”。语气上，前者只是说一样，后者强调“越X越Y”的联动。对比：She is as tall as me. / The taller she gets, the more confident she becomes.

contrasts.title: vs simple comparative
contrasts.note: 结构上，普通比较句只比两者高低，如 A is better than B；本结构要有两个比较分句。场景上，普通比较用于静态比较，本结构用于趋势和规律。对比：This method is better than that one. / The more you use this method, the better your results are.

contrasts.title: vs because
contrasts.note: 结构上，because 后面接原因从句；the more..., the more... 接两个比较分句。语气上，because 更直接讲原因，本结构更像总结规律。对比：Because I practiced, I improved. / The more I practiced, the more I improved.

contrasts.title: vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上，if 表示条件，后面不要求比较级；the more..., the more... 两边通常都要有比较级。场景上，if 常说单次条件，本结构常说持续变化。对比：If you practice, you will improve. / The more you practice, the more you improve.

contrasts.title: full clause vs shortened form
contrasts.note: 结构上，可用完整分句，也可省略成短语：The more you read, the smarter you get. / The more, the better. 语气上，完整句更清楚，短式更像固定说法。初学者先用完整句最安全。

examples.sentence: The more you practice, the better you become.
examples.translation: 你练习得越多，就会变得越好。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：两个分句都完整。

examples.sentence: The more careful you are, the fewer mistakes you make.
examples.translation: 你越仔细，犯的错误就越少。
examples.note: 前半句用形容词比较级放在主语前，属于常见变体。

examples.sentence: The more data we collect, the more accurate the analysis becomes.
examples.translation: 我们收集的数据越多，分析就越准确。
examples.note: 适合分析、报告、写作场景。

examples.sentence: The more expensive it is, the less likely people are to buy it.
examples.translation: 它越贵，人们就越不可能购买。
examples.note: 展示 more... less... 的对比关系，常用于比较敏感语境。

examples.sentence: The earlier you leave, the faster you’ll get there.
examples.translation: 你越早出发，就会越快到那里。
examples.note: 日常生活中很自然；后半句可带 will。

examples.sentence: The more I think about it, the more I agree with you.
examples.translation: 我越想这件事，就越赞同你。
examples.note: 很常见的口语表达，表示想法逐渐变化。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: {xClause} 写“主语 + 动词”，并带出比较变化，如 you practice；{yClause} 写结果分句，如 you improve。两边都要有比较含义。
templates.exampleSentence: The more you practice, the more you improve.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你练习得越多，进步就越大。

templates.label: 形容词变体模板
templates.template: The more {xAdjAdv} + subject + be, the more {yClause}.
slotHints: {xAdjAdv} 常用 careful, patient, confident 等比较级意义表达，如 careful 在这里写成 The more careful you are。后半句写结果。
templates.exampleSentence: The more careful you are, the fewer mistakes you make.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你越仔细，犯错就越少。

templates.label: 写作用分析模板
templates.template: The more {xClause}, the more {xAdjAdv} the {noun phrase} becomes.
slotHints: 前半句写变化来源，如 we invest in training；后半句写某个结果越来越怎样，如 efficient the team becomes。注意后半句仍要有比较意义。
templates.exampleSentence: The more we invest in training, the more efficient the team becomes.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们在培训上投入越多，团队就越高效。

templates.label: 反向结果模板
slotHints: 当结果是减少时，用 less/fewer。可写数量减少、可能性降低、问题变少等。
templates.exampleSentence: The more you wait, the less time you have.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你等得越久，时间就越少。

items.title: it is / was ... that ...
items.pattern: it is / was + focus + that + clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来把句子里的某一部分单独突出出来，意思相当于“正是……”。基本形式是 it is/was + 被强调部分 + that + 其余完整信息。被强调部分常是主语、宾语、时间、地点、原因状语，通常放在句首作整句框架。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性到较正式，常见于纠正别人、做对比、演讲、写作中强调重点：It was John that called. 不适合每句话都用，否则会显得刻意、重。that 后面要接完整从句，不要只接短语；整个强调框架通常放在句首。初学者先安全使用“it is/was + 时间/人/地点 + that + 完整句子”最不容易出错。

slotSchema.label: 被强调部分
slotSchema.hint: 可放人、事物、时间、地点、原因等名词性或状语成分。常见错误是把完整句子整个塞进这里。例：It was yesterday that we met.

slotSchema.label: that后的完整从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里要保留其余完整信息，通常应有主语和谓语。常见错误是 that 后只放短语。例：that I met her at the station.

slotSchema.label: is / was
slotSchema.hint: 现在强调常用 is，过去事件常用 was。常见错误是明明说过去却用 is。例：It was Tom that broke the window.

commonErrors: Wrong: It is because he was late. 为什么错：这里只有原因说明，没有 that 从句，不是完整强调句。怎么改：It is because he was late that we missed the train.
Wrong: It was went home that John. 为什么错：被强调部分和从句顺序乱了，that 后也不是完整从句。怎么改：It was John that went home.
Wrong: It is yesterday that we met? 为什么错：一般疑问句不能只靠问号；若陈述强调应用句号。怎么改：It was yesterday that we met. / Was it yesterday that we met?
Wrong: It was in the park where we met. 为什么错：强调句固定用 that；where 常用于定语从句。怎么改：It was in the park that we met.
Wrong: It is Tom that clever. 为什么错：that 后缺少完整谓语信息。怎么改：It is Tom that is clever. 或更自然：Tom is the one who is clever.
Wrong: It was because of rain that. 为什么错：that 后没有完整句子，信息不完整。怎么改：It was because of rain that the game was canceled.

contrasts.title: vs. the reason is that...
contrasts.note: 结构不同：the reason is that 后面说明“原因内容”；强调句是 it is/was + 原因 + that + 结果。语气上前者是在解释，后者是在突出。对比：The reason we left early is that it was late. / It was because it was late that we left early.

contrasts.title: vs. think/say/know + that-clause
contrasts.note: 结构不同：I think that... 里的 that 只是连接宾语从句；强调句里 that 前面必须有 it is/was + 焦点。功能上前者是表达观点，后者是突出成分。对比：I know that he called. / It was he that called.

contrasts.title: vs. normal word order
contrasts.note: 结构不同：普通句直接陈述；强调句把焦点提前到 it is/was 后。语气上普通句自然平稳，强调句更突出、常带纠正意味。对比：John called me yesterday. / It was John that called me yesterday.

contrasts.title: vs. there is/are
contrasts.note: 结构不同：there be 用来引出“存在”，强调句用来突出已知句子中的一部分。场景上 there be 介绍新信息，强调句聚焦重点。对比：There was a man at the door. / It was the man at the door that scared me.

examples.sentence: It was John that called me last night.
examples.translation: 昨晚给我打电话的正是约翰。
examples.note: 最基础用法：强调主语。

examples.sentence: It was last night that John called me.
examples.translation: 约翰正是昨晚给我打电话的。
examples.note: 同一事实，改为强调时间。

examples.sentence: It is in daily practice that students improve their writing.
examples.translation: 学生正是在日常练习中提高写作的。
examples.note: 较书面，强调地点/条件成分。

examples.sentence: It was because the road was closed that we turned back.
examples.translation: 我们折返正是因为道路封闭。
examples.note: 和 the reason is that 对比时很常见。

examples.sentence: It was at the station that I met her.
examples.translation: 我正是在车站遇见她的。
examples.note: 强调地点，适合回答 Where...?

examples.sentence: It was this book that the teacher recommended, not that one.
examples.translation: 老师推荐的正是这本书，不是那本。
examples.note: 适合对比和纠正。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: {focus} 放时间/地点/人最安全，如 yesterday, at school, Tom；{clause} 写其余完整句子
templates.exampleSentence: It was yesterday that we met.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我们正是昨天见面的。

templates.label: 强调人物模板
slotHints: {focus} 常放人名或代词；{clause} 保留动作信息，如 called me, solved the problem
templates.exampleSentence: It is Mary that helps me most.
templates.exampleTranslation: 最常帮助我的正是玛丽。

templates.label: 替代焦点位置模板
slotHints: {focus} 可放地点/时间/原因；后面从句继续正常语序
templates.exampleSentence: It was in Paris that she learned French.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她正是在巴黎学的法语。

templates.label: 写作型模板
slotHints: {focus} 放你要突出的重点；not 后放对比对象，适合纠正或论证
templates.exampleSentence: It is practice that builds confidence, not talent alone.
templates.exampleTranslation: 建立自信的正是练习，而不只是天赋。

items.title: therefore (result adverb)
items.coreExplanation: therefore 用来把前一句的原因、事实或论点，和后面的结果清楚连起来，意思接近“因此、所以”。它后面通常接完整句子，最常放在第二句句首，后面常加逗号：Statement. Therefore, result. 也可放句中作插入语。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式，常见于论文、报告、说明文、书面论证，也可用于较正式发言。日常口语里一般更自然的是 so。它不适合特别随意的聊天，也不要直接拿来连接名词短语。句首最稳妥，前面常是句号，therefore 后面常加逗号；放句中时前后也常用逗号。初学者先固定用“完整句。Therefore, 完整结果句。”最安全。

slotSchema.label: 前文原因/事实句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句，说明前提、原因或事实。常错：只放短语，如“Because the rain”不完整。小例：The road was icy.

slotSchema.label: 结果句
slotSchema.hint: therefore 后面通常放完整结果句。常错：只接名词短语，如“therefore the delay”不自然。小例：The game was canceled.

slotSchema.label: therefore的位置
slotSchema.hint: 初学先放第二句句首 Therefore, ...。常错：逗号位置乱，如“Therefore the game was canceled”在正式写作中不如加逗号稳妥。小例：Therefore, we changed the plan.

commonErrors: ✗ Because it was raining, therefore we stayed inside. —— because 已经表达原因，再加 therefore 造成重复连接。✓ 用 Because it was raining, we stayed inside. 或 It was raining. Therefore, we stayed inside.
✗ Therefore of the rain, the game was canceled. —— therefore 不能像 because of 一样直接接名词短语。✓ 用 Because of the rain, the game was canceled. 或 It rained heavily. Therefore, the game was canceled.
✗ The train was late, therefore missing many passengers. —— therefore 后面通常应接完整句，不要只接现在分词短语。✓ The train was late. Therefore, many passengers missed the meeting.
✗ I was tired. Therefore so I went home. —— therefore 和 so 不要并用表达同一层结果。✓ I was tired. Therefore, I went home. 或 I was tired, so I went home.
✗ We therefore can finish it today? —— therefore 更常用于陈述和书面推理，这样放在疑问句里很不自然。✓ Therefore, can we finish it today? 但更自然是 So, can we finish it today?

✗ The evidence is weak therefore the claim is doubtful. —— 书面语里 therefore 前后通常要有清楚标点。✓ The evidence is weak; therefore, the claim is doubtful. 或 The evidence is weak. Therefore, the claim is doubtful.

contrasts.title: so + 结果句
contrasts.note: 结构上，so 常直接连在同一句里：cause, so result；therefore 更常作副词放句首或分号后。语气上，so 更口语，therefore 更正式。对比：It was late, so we left. / It was late. Therefore, we left.

contrasts.title: thus
contrasts.note: 结构上，thus 和 therefore 都能作结果副词，位置相近；语气上，thus 更书面、更凝练，某些学术文体里更常见。therefore 对学习者更直观。对比：The sample was contaminated; thus, the test failed. / Therefore, the test failed.

contrasts.title: that's why + 结果
contrasts.note: 结构上，that's why 后面常接完整结果句，前面通常先说原因；therefore 则是副词，不带 why。语气上，that's why 更口语、更解释性。对比：I missed the bus. That's why I was late. / I missed the bus. Therefore, I was late.

contrasts.title: because + 从句
contrasts.note: 结构上，because 用来引出原因；therefore 用来引出结果，方向相反。语气上，because 中性常用，therefore 更正式。对比：I stayed home because it rained. / It rained. Therefore, I stayed home.

contrasts.title: because of + 名词/动名词
contrasts.note: 结构上，because of 后面接名词或动名词；therefore 后面一般接完整句。语气上，because of 很常用，therefore 更像论证连接。对比：Because of the storm, classes were canceled. / There was a storm. Therefore, classes were canceled.

examples.sentence: It was raining hard. Therefore, we stayed inside.
examples.translation: 雨下得很大。因此，我们待在室内。
examples.note: 最安全基础型：前一句原因，第二句句首 therefore 引出结果。

examples.sentence: The roads were icy; therefore, school was canceled.
examples.translation: 道路结冰了；因此，学校停课了。
examples.note: 正式写作中也常放在分号后。

examples.sentence: The method is simple and inexpensive. It is therefore suitable for small schools.
examples.translation: 这种方法简单且便宜，因此适合小型学校。
examples.note: 句中位置，therefore 放在 be 动词前，语气更书面。

examples.sentence: He didn’t study, so he failed the test. He didn’t study. Therefore, he failed the test.
examples.translation: 他没学习，所以考试没通过。他没学习。因此，他考试没通过。
examples.note: 同一意思下，前句更口语，后句更正式。

examples.sentence: The data are incomplete. Therefore, we cannot draw a firm conclusion.
examples.translation: 数据不完整。因此，我们无法得出明确结论。
examples.note: 典型论证写法，适合报告和学术表达。

examples.sentence: My phone was out of battery. Therefore, I couldn’t call you.
examples.translation: 我的手机没电了。因此，我没法给你打电话。
examples.note: 日常也能用，但比 so 更正式。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
templates.template: [原因完整句]. Therefore, [结果完整句].
slotHints: 前面放原因、事实或前提句
后面放由前文推出的结果句
templates.exampleSentence: The shop was closed. Therefore, we went home.
templates.exampleTranslation: 商店关门了。因此，我们回家了。

templates.label: 句中正式模板
templates.template: [前文]. [主语] therefore [谓语其余部分].
slotHints: 前文先交代事实
therefore 常放在主语后、实义动词前；若后面是 be，常放在 be 前
templates.exampleSentence: The rule is unclear. Students therefore make many mistakes.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这条规则不清楚，因此学生会犯很多错误。

templates.label: 分号连接模板
slotHints: 前后都是完整句
适合正式书面语，把两句紧密连接
templates.exampleSentence: The evidence is limited; therefore, the claim remains uncertain.
templates.exampleTranslation: 证据有限；因此，这一说法仍不确定。

templates.label: 写作论证模板
templates.template: [事实/数据句]. Therefore, [判断/结论句].
slotHints: 前句写观察到的事实或数据
后句写结论、建议或判断
templates.exampleSentence: Sales fell by 20% last month. Therefore, we need a new strategy.
templates.exampleTranslation: 上个月销售额下降了20%。因此，我们需要一个新策略。

items.title: thus (result adverb)
items.coreExplanation: thus用来把前一句的情况与后一句的结果紧凑地连接起来，意思相当于“因此、从而”。它通常放在结果分句前，常见格式是“陈述句。Thus, 结果句”或“主语... and thus 谓语...”。thus后面一般接完整结果分句，写作中最常放句首或and后。
items.usageNote: thus语气偏正式，最自然的场景是说明文、报告、论文、分析和较正式邮件；日常聊天里通常不用，口语里更常说so。它不适合拿来单独回答，也不适合频繁连用很多次。若thus放在句首，后面通常加逗号；若放在and thus中间，一般不单独加句号。初学者最安全的做法是先写原因句，再写“Thus, + 完整结果句”。

slotSchema.label: 前一句情况/原因
slotSchema.hint: 放完整陈述句，说明背景、事实或前提。常见错误是只写短语而不是句子。例：The road was closed.

slotSchema.label: 结果句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整结果分句，要有主语和谓语。常见错误是thus后只接名词短语。例：Thus, we took a different route.

slotSchema.label: 句内连接位
slotSchema.hint: 可用and thus把thus放在句中，前后仍要能接成完整结构。常见错误是随便插在动词和宾语中间。例：The system failed and thus caused delays.

commonErrors: ✗ Thus the delay. → 错在thus后面只放了名词短语，不是完整结果句。✓ 改为：Thus, the meeting was delayed.
✗ Because it rained, thus we stayed home. → 错在because已经引出原因，再接thus显得重复累赘。✓ 改为：Because it rained, we stayed home. / It rained. Thus, we stayed home.
✗ We thus can finish early. → 不算绝对错误，但初学者这样放位置容易生硬，且常不如句首清楚。✓ 更稳妥：Thus, we can finish early.
✗ The data was incomplete thus we repeated the test. → 错在把两个完整分句直接逗号省掉或标点不足。✓ 改为：The data was incomplete. Thus, we repeated the test. / The data was incomplete; thus, we repeated the test.
✗ Thus, to reduce costs. → 错在thus后没有完整谓语结构，像残句。✓ 改为：Thus, we reduced costs.
✗ I was tired. Thus I didn’t go, and thus I slept, and thus... → 错在连续堆叠thus，书面上显得僵硬。✓ 改为只保留关键一次，其他地方换普通连接方式。

contrasts.title: thus vs therefore
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可放句首接结果句，但therefore更常见、更通用；thus更书面，也常见于and thus。场景上，报告和分析里二者都行，但日常正式写作里therefore更稳。对比：The sample was contaminated. Therefore, we discarded it. / The sample was contaminated. Thus, we discarded it.

contrasts.title: thus vs so
contrasts.note: 结构上so也接结果句，但so更口语，thus更正式。场景上，聊天、短信、随口解释更适合so；论文、报告更适合thus。对比：It was late, so we left. / It was late. Thus, we left. 后一句明显更书面。

contrasts.title: thus vs thereby
contrasts.note: 结构上thus通常连接整个结果分句；thereby通常放在动词后，表示“从而通过这一点”。场景上，thereby更像说明某动作带来的结果。对比：The company cut costs. Thus, profits rose. / The company cut costs, thereby increasing profits.

contrasts.title: thus不用来直接引出原因
contrasts.note: 结构上thus只表示结果，不像because/since/as那样直接引出原因从句。场景上，如果你想先说原因，用because等；如果你想接着推出结论，用thus。对比：Because demand fell, prices dropped. / Demand fell. Thus, prices dropped.

contrasts.title: 句首thus vs and thus
contrasts.note: 结构上句首thus常写成“Thus, + 完整句”；and thus则嵌在一个更长句子里。场景上，句首更清楚，适合初学者；and thus更像正式连写。对比：The file was corrupted. Thus, we restored a backup. / The file was corrupted and thus required restoration.

examples.sentence: The road was closed. Thus, we took a different route.
examples.translation: 这条路封了，因此我们走了另一条路线。
examples.note: 最安全基础型：前一句写事实，thus后写完整结果句。

examples.sentence: The device overheated and thus shut down automatically.
examples.translation: 设备过热，因此自动关机了。
examples.note: 句中位置示例：放在and thus后面，适合较紧凑的书面表达。

examples.sentence: The survey sample was too small; thus, the findings should be interpreted cautiously.
examples.translation: 调查样本太小；因此，这些结果应谨慎解读。
examples.note: 典型正式写作风格，常见于报告和论文。

examples.sentence: The evidence is limited. Therefore, we cannot draw a firm conclusion. The evidence is limited. Thus, we cannot draw a firm conclusion.
examples.translation: 证据有限。因此，我们无法得出明确结论。证据有限。因此，我们无法得出明确结论。
examples.note: 与therefore对比：意思接近，但thus更书面、更压缩。

examples.sentence: The store was already closed. Thus, I bought nothing.
examples.translation: 商店已经关门了，因此我什么也没买。
examples.note: 日常内容也能用，但整体语气仍偏书面。

examples.sentence: Several files were missing. Thus, the report could not be completed on time.
examples.translation: 有几个文件缺失，因此报告无法按时完成。
examples.note: 适合说明“前因导致后果”的办公场景。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
slotHints: 前面先写完整事实句；thus后也必须是完整结果句，不要只写名词或短语。
templates.exampleSentence: The internet was down. Thus, we could not submit the form.
templates.exampleTranslation: 网络断了，因此我们无法提交表格。

templates.label: 句中替代位置模板
templates.template: [主语] [原因动作], and thus [结果动作/结果分句].
slotHints: 用于同一句里紧凑表达结果；and thus后面要能自然接动作或完整分句。
templates.exampleSentence: The team missed the deadline and thus lost the contract.
templates.exampleTranslation: 团队错过了截止日期，因此失去了合同。

templates.label: 写作用模板
slotHints: 前半句常是研究发现、数据事实或观察；后半句常是结论、判断、建议。
templates.exampleSentence: The response rate was low; thus, the results may not be representative.
templates.exampleTranslation: 回复率很低；因此，结果可能不具代表性。

items.title: but (contrast connector)
items.coreExplanation: but 用来连接两个意思相反、相对或不符合预期的分句，表示“但是/可是”。最常见结构是“陈述句, but 陈述句”。but 前通常先说第一点，后面补充对比信息；它多放在句子中间，前面常有逗号。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和一般写作都很常用，适合聊天、说明情况、写邮件或短文中的直接对比。不太适合非常正式、需要强烈逻辑标记的学术写作，那时常用 however。but 一般不放在逗号后单独接短语凑句，后面最好接完整分句。初学者最安全的做法是先写完整句A，再写“, but + 完整句B”。

slotSchema.label: 第一分句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整陈述句，先说事实或观点。常见错误：只放一个词组。例：I like coffee

slotSchema.label: 第二分句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整陈述句，内容与前面形成转折。常见错误：后面不完整。例：but I don't drink it at night

slotSchema.label: 逗号
slotSchema.hint: 书写时常在 but 前加逗号连接两个分句。常见错误：直接连两个完整句。例：I tried, but I failed

commonErrors: Wrong: Although I was tired, but I finished the work.｜错因：although 和 but 都表示让步/转折，不能在同一层结构里重复连。｜改法：Although I was tired, I finished the work. / I was tired, but I finished the work.
Wrong: I like this but not buy it.｜错因：but 后面不是完整分句，主语缺失，初学者容易写成碎片。｜改法：I like this, but I won't buy it.
Wrong: He is rich, but very kind.｜错因：第二部分只有形容词短语，不是完整陈述句，书面基础练习里不稳妥。｜改法：He is rich, but he is very kind.
Wrong: But I was late.｜错因：单独一句以 but 开头在口语里可以，但基础写作里容易显得前文缺失。｜改法：I wanted to come earlier, but I was late.
Wrong: I studied hard but, I failed.｜错因：逗号位置错了；逗号通常放在 but 前，不放在 but 后。｜改法：I studied hard, but I failed.
Wrong: She is small but strong?｜错因：语调和标点不匹配；这是陈述对比，不应用问号。｜改法：She is small but strong.

contrasts.title: however (sentence adverb)
contrasts.note: 结构上，however 常放句首/句中并与前句分开；but 直接连接两个分句。语气上 however 更正式，but 更日常。对比：I was tired, but I went. / I was tired. However, I went.

contrasts.title: yet (contrast connector)
contrasts.note: 结构上，yet 和 but 都可连两个分句；但 yet 常带“尽管如此/仍然”的意味更强。语气上 yet 稍书面一些。对比：It was expensive, but I bought it. / It was expensive, yet I bought it.

contrasts.title: though + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，though 后面直接接从句，不能像 but 那样夹在两个完整主句中间做并列连接。语气上 though 更像先让步再转主句。对比：I liked it, but it was too long. / Though I liked it, it was too long.

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，although 引导从句，主句在后；but 连接并列分句。语气上 although 更适合先承认一面，再说另一面。对比：Although it was raining, we went out. / It was raining, but we went out.

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构上，despite 后接名词或动名词，不接完整句；but 后接完整分句。语气上 despite 更压缩、更书面。对比：Despite the rain, we went out. / It was raining, but we went out.

examples.sentence: I am tired, but I still want to go.
examples.translation: 我很累，但是我还是想去。
examples.note: 最安全的基础结构：完整句 + , but + 完整句。

examples.sentence: The room is small, but it is clean.
examples.translation: 这个房间很小，但是很干净。
examples.note: 第二分句重复主语 it，结构清楚，适合初学者。

examples.sentence: I wanted to call you, but my phone died.
examples.translation: 我本来想给你打电话，但是我的手机没电了。
examples.note: 常见于解释情况。

examples.sentence: The idea is simple, but it could be very useful.
examples.translation: 这个想法很简单，但它可能非常有用。
examples.note: 比 however 更口语、更直接。

examples.sentence: He is young, but he is very responsible.
examples.translation: 他很年轻，但是他很有责任感。
examples.note: 适合练习“表面对比 vs 实际评价”的用法。

examples.sentence: I don't eat meat, but I do eat fish.
examples.translation: 我不吃肉，但是我吃鱼。
examples.note: 这个例子适合体会直接对比；do 用来加强对比。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 先放完整陈述句A
but 后再放完整陈述句B，内容与A形成对比
templates.exampleSentence: I like this coat, but it is too expensive.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我喜欢这件外套，但是它太贵了。

templates.label: 说明情况模板
slotHints: action 放想做的动作原形
problem clause 放完整问题说明
templates.exampleSentence: I wanted to help, but I didn't have time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我本来想帮忙，但是我没有时间。

templates.label: 写作用对比模板
templates.template: [thing/person] is [point A], but it is [point B].
slotHints: 前面放人或事物
两个形容词或描述要形成明显对比
templates.exampleSentence: The test was difficult, but it was fair.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个考试很难，但是很公平。

items.title: yet (contrast connector)
items.coreExplanation: yet作连词时表示“然而、可是”，把前后两个完整意思连接起来，强调结果有点出乎意料。常见结构是“陈述句, yet 陈述句”，yet通常放在第二分句前，前面多用逗号，后面接完整主谓，不直接接名词。
items.usageNote: 语气比but更书面、更简洁，正式和中性写作里很常见，新闻、说明、评论、短文中都自然；日常口语也能用，但频率低于but。它适合“前面情况本来会让人期待另一结果”的场景，不适合只做普通并列或递进。通常写成“, yet ...”；句中别漏逗号。初学者最稳妥的做法：先写完整句子A，再加“, yet + 完整句子B”。

slotSchema.label: first statement
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整陈述句，先说背景或预期。常见错误：只放短语。例：The task was simple

slotSchema.label: contrasting statement
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整陈述句，表示出乎意料的对比结果。常见错误：yet后只接名词/短语。例：no one finished it on time

slotSchema.label: comma before yet
slotSchema.hint: 前一句后通常加逗号再写yet。常见错误：写成句号+yet却不重组句子。例：, yet

slotSchema.label: subject in second clause
slotSchema.hint: 第二分句通常要有自己的主语。常见错误：省掉主语，写成yet impossible。例：it was impossible

commonErrors: × The task was simple, yet impossible. 原因：yet后面不是完整分句，只是形容词。改：The task was simple, yet it was impossible to finish on time.
× Although he was tired, yet he kept working. 原因：although已经表示让步，不能再和连词yet直接重复搭配。改：Although he was tired, he kept working. / He was tired, yet he kept working.
× It was cheap yet. I didn't buy it. 原因：这里yet被误放到句尾，像副词“还”。改：It was cheap, yet I didn't buy it.
× She studied hard yet passed the exam. 原因：第二分句缺主语，书面标准结构不完整。改：She studied hard, yet she failed the exam. / ..., yet she still didn't pass.
× He is young, but yet very experienced. 原因：but和yet作为并列连词不要叠加使用。改：He is young, yet very experienced. / He is young, but very experienced.
× Yet he was nervous, he gave the talk. 原因：把yet放句首后仍用逗号连接两个完整句，结构不对。改：He was nervous, yet he gave the talk. / He was nervous. Yet he gave the talk.

contrasts.title: yet vs but
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可连接两个分句：A, yet B / A, but B。语气上but最普通、最口语；yet更强调“按理说不该这样却这样了”，更有出乎意料感。对比：It was expensive, but I bought it.（普通转折）/ It was expensive, yet I bought it.（有点意外）

contrasts.title: yet vs however
contrasts.note: 结构上yet是连词，常放在逗号后直接接分句；however更像句子副词，常写成“A. However, B.”或“A; however, B.”。语气上however更正式、更明显分段。对比：The plan was risky, yet it worked. / The plan was risky. However, it worked.

contrasts.title: yet vs although
contrasts.note: 结构上although放在从句前：Although A, B；yet放在两句之间：A, yet B。语气上although先交代让步背景，yet更像先说事实再突然转折。对比：Although he apologized, I was still upset. / He apologized, yet I was still upset.

contrasts.title: yet vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上even though也引导从句：Even though A, B；yet连接两个独立意思。语气上even though让步更强、更明确；yet更简洁。对比：Even though it was raining, we went out. / It was raining, yet we went out.

contrasts.title: yet vs despite
contrasts.note: 结构上despite后接名词或v-ing，不接完整分句；yet后通常接完整分句。语气上despite更压缩、更像书面短语。对比：Despite the rain, we went out. / It was raining, yet we went out.

examples.sentence: The box was small, yet it was very heavy.
examples.translation: 这个箱子很小，然而却很重。
examples.note: 最基础安全句型：前后都是完整分句。

examples.sentence: She is only nineteen, yet she runs her own company.
examples.translation: 她才十九岁，然而已经经营自己的公司了。
examples.note: 用年龄和结果形成“出乎意料”的对比。

examples.sentence: The method seems old-fashioned, yet it remains highly effective.
examples.translation: 这种方法看起来过时，然而仍然非常有效。
examples.note: 较常见于说明文、评论和正式写作。

examples.sentence: It was cheaper, but the quality was worse; the other one cost more, yet it lasted longer.
examples.translation: 这个更便宜，但质量更差；另一个更贵，然而却更耐用。
examples.note: 同句中对比but和yet：yet更突出“贵却更值”。

examples.sentence: He had very little time, yet he called his grandmother.
examples.translation: 他时间很少，然而还是给祖母打了电话。
examples.note: 日常场景中表达“尽管忙，还是做了”。

examples.sentence: The evidence was limited, yet the conclusion was stated with confidence.
examples.translation: 证据有限，然而结论却被很有把握地提出。
examples.note: 被动语态+yet，常见于学术或评论写作。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: 前句写事实或预期背景
后句写出乎意料的结果
templates.exampleSentence: The test was short, yet it was difficult.
templates.exampleTranslation: 考试很短，然而却很难。

templates.label: Alternate-position writing template
slotHints: 先用句号结束第一句
Yet句首首字母大写，后面接完整句
templates.exampleSentence: He had no experience. Yet he solved the problem quickly.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他没有经验。然而他很快就解决了问题。

templates.label: Writing-oriented evaluation template
slotHints: 前面写表面印象
后面写真实评价或结果
templates.exampleSentence: The idea seems simple, yet it is powerful.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个想法看起来简单，然而很有力量。

items.title: still (contrast adverb)
items.coreExplanation: Still放在一句完整话后面，表示“尽管前面如此，这一点仍然成立”。基础结构是“陈述句。Still, 陈述句.”，Still后通常接逗号，再接完整主句。它最常出现在第二句句首，用来承接前文、表达让步后的坚持、评价或结果。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性，也常见于带一点主观感受的口语或书面表达，适合说明“前面不理想，但后面依然是真的”。常用于安慰、反思、评价、做决定。不适合直接连接名词短语，也不要拿它代替although引导从句。通常放在第二句句首并写成“Still,”；初学者最稳妥的做法是先写第一句事实，再另起一句用Still, +完整句。

slotSchema.label: 前一句事实
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整陈述句，先交代不利情况或前提。常见错误是只写短语，不成句。例：It was late.

slotSchema.label: Still后面的主句
slotSchema.hint: 必须是完整句，写“仍然成立”的内容。常见错误是Still后只接名词或动词原形。例：Still, we went out.

slotSchema.label: 标点形式
slotSchema.hint: 最稳形式是句号+Still,+逗号。常见错误是漏掉逗号或把still全小写夹在错误位置。例：It was expensive. Still, I bought it.

commonErrors: × Still although it was raining, we went out.——错在把Still和although重复叠加，一个句子里功能重了。✓ Although it was raining, we went out. / It was raining. Still, we went out.
× Still the rain, we went out.——错在Still后不能直接接名词短语。✓ Despite the rain, we went out. / It was raining. Still, we went out.
× It was hard, still I finished it.——错在still作句首转折副词时通常要有逗号，书写不完整。✓ It was hard. Still, I finished it.
× I still, went to class.——错在把表示“仍然”的句内still和句首转折Still混用了，位置也错。✓ I still went to class. / I was tired. Still, I went to class.
× Still, because I was tired.——错在Still后面不能只接原因从句，缺少主句。✓ Still, I went because it was important. / I was tired. Still, I went.
× We failed. Still we are disappointed.——语法不算全错，但意思常不自然；Still通常引出与前句形成反差的“仍然成立”的内容，这里不够转折。✓ We failed. Still, we learned a lot.

contrasts.title: Still vs however
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可放句首接逗号；但however更中性、逻辑化，Still更强调“尽管如此，还是/仍然”，常带说话人态度。写正式分析常用however；安慰或反思常用Still。比较：It was risky. However, we proceeded. / It was risky. Still, we proceeded.

contrasts.title: Still vs yet
contrasts.note: 结构上yet常连接并列分句，也可作句首副词；Still更常见于“前一句。Still, 后一句”。语气上yet更简洁硬朗，Still更像在承认前情后继续坚持。比较：The room was small, yet it felt cozy. / The room was small. Still, it felt cozy.

contrasts.title: Still vs although + clause
contrasts.note: 结构区别最大：although必须带从句放在同一句里；Still通常放第二句句首，后面接完整主句。although更像提前让步，Still更像先说事实再补转折评价。比较：Although I was tired, I went. / I was tired. Still, I went.

contrasts.title: Still vs even though + clause
contrasts.note: even though也是从属连词，后接完整从句，语气比although更强；Still不是连词，不能直接接让步从句。场景上，even though强调“明明如此也……”；Still强调“说到这里，我还是要说……”。比较：Even though it was expensive, I bought it. / It was expensive. Still, I bought it.

contrasts.title: Contrast Still vs time still
contrasts.note: 结构上，时间义still常放在助动词后、实义动词前，如I still work there；转折义Still常放句首并后接逗号。语义上前者是“仍然、还”，后者是“尽管如此”。比较：I still like him. / He made mistakes. Still, I like him.

examples.sentence: It was raining. Still, we went for a walk.
examples.translation: 那时在下雨。尽管如此，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: 最基础安全用法：前句交代不利情况，后句写仍然发生的事。

examples.sentence: I was tired after work. I still cooked dinner.
examples.translation: 下班后我很累，但我还是做了晚饭。
examples.note: 这是句内still，表示“仍然”；不是本卡核心，但可帮助区分位置差异。

examples.sentence: The plan is not perfect. Still, it is the best option at this stage.
examples.translation: 这个计划并不完美。尽管如此，它在这个阶段仍是最好的选择。
examples.note: 较常见于说明、讨论和写作。

examples.sentence: It was expensive. Still, I bought it.
examples.translation: 它很贵。尽管如此，我还是买了。
examples.note: 适合和although/even though比较：这里用两句，语气更像说话人回顾决定。

examples.sentence: She didn't apologize. Still, I want to help her.
examples.translation: 她没有道歉。尽管如此，我还是想帮她。
examples.note: 常用于表达复杂情绪：前面有不满，后面仍保留善意。

examples.sentence: The test was difficult. Still, I think I did okay.
examples.translation: 考试很难。尽管如此，我觉得我考得还行。
examples.note: 口语中很自然，常用于自我反思或安慰。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: [前一句事实]. Still, [仍然成立的完整句].
slotHints: 先写不利情况或前提
再写“还是/仍然”的结果，必须是完整句
templates.exampleSentence: It was late. Still, we kept studying.
templates.exampleTranslation: 已经很晚了。尽管如此，我们还是继续学习。

templates.label: 常用决定表达模板
templates.template: [不理想情况]. Still, I [动词过去式/现在式] [其他内容].
slotHints: 第一句写问题，如价格高、太晚、很累
第二句常写自己的决定或态度
templates.exampleSentence: I was nervous. Still, I asked the question.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我很紧张。尽管如此，我还是提了那个问题。

templates.label: 写作用评价模板
templates.template: [局限/问题]. Still, [主语] is/are [评价].
slotHints: 第一句写不足之处
第二句给出总体评价，很适合作文和讨论
templates.exampleSentence: The method is slow. Still, it is reliable.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这种方法很慢。尽管如此，它很可靠。

items.title: though + clause
items.coreExplanation: though表示“虽然、尽管”，用来先承认一个事实，再说后面的主要结果或看法。它后面必须接完整从句，常见结构是Though + 从句, 主句；也可放句尾作补充评论，但基础用法最安全的是放句首并用逗号隔开。
items.usageNote: though语气比although更轻、更口语，常见于对话、邮件、日常说明，也可用于不太正式的写作。它适合表达“有这个事实，但结果/判断不同”的让步关系。不适合后面直接接名词；那要用despite / in spite of。句首时通常后面加逗号；句尾though前面也常有逗号。初学者先固定用“Though + 完整句子, 主句”最不容易错。

slotSchema.label: 让步从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整句子，要有主语和谓语。常错：只放名词，如 though the rain。应说 though it was raining。例：though he was tired

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放真正想强调的信息或结果。常错：主句不完整。应写完整意思。例：he kept working

slotSchema.label: 句尾though
slotSchema.hint: 可放在句尾表示“不过、可是”，多见口语。常错：和前面句首though重复使用。例：I like it. It’s expensive, though.

commonErrors: ✗ though the rain, we went out. → 错在though后不能直接接名词。✓ 改为 though it was raining, we went out. 或 despite the rain, we went out.
✗ though being tired, she finished it. → 错在基础用法里though后面应接完整从句，这里不自然。✓ 改为 though she was tired, she finished it. 或 despite being tired, she finished it.
✗ Though he was tired but he kept working. → 错在though已经表达转折，主句前不要再加but。✓ 改为 Though he was tired, he kept working.
✗ I went, though I was sick yesterday. → 句法不一定错，但信息重点混乱；初学者更自然的是先放让步从句。✓ 改为 Though I was sick yesterday, I went.
✗ Though he is young. → 错在只有从句，没有主句，意思没说完。✓ 改为 Though he is young, he is very reliable.
✗ Though, he was tired, he kept working. → 错在though后不要立刻加逗号把从句切断。✓ 改为 Though he was tired, he kept working.

contrasts.title: though vs although
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整从句；语气上although更正式、更书面，though更轻松、更常口语。比较：Although the plan is risky, it may work. / Though the plan is risky, it may work.

contrasts.title: though vs even though
contrasts.note: 结构上都接完整从句；语气上even though让步更强，强调“尽管事实很明显仍然如此”。比较：Though he was tired, he came. / Even though he was very tired, he came.

contrasts.title: though vs despite
contrasts.note: 结构不同：though + 从句，despite + 名词/动名词。场景上despite更常见于较紧凑的书面表达。比较：Though it was raining, we left. / Despite the rain, we left.

contrasts.title: though vs in spite of
contrasts.note: 结构不同：though后面要有主语+谓语；in spite of后接名词或v-ing。语气上in spite of稍长，常见于正式或说明文。比较：Though she was ill, she worked. / In spite of her illness, she worked.

contrasts.title: clause-initial though vs sentence-final though
contrasts.note: 结构上句首though引出从句；句尾though不再带从句，只作“不过”补充。语气上句尾更口语。比较：Though it’s small, it’s useful. / It’s small. It’s useful, though.

examples.sentence: Though it was raining, we went for a walk.
examples.translation: 虽然在下雨，我们还是去散步了。
examples.note: 最安全的基础结构：Though + 完整从句, 主句。

examples.sentence: We decided to go, though the weather looked bad.
examples.translation: 虽然天气看起来很糟，我们还是决定去。
examples.note: though从句也可放句尾，但初学者写句首更稳。

examples.sentence: Though the data is limited, the report offers a useful starting point.
examples.translation: 虽然数据有限，这份报告还是提供了一个有用的起点。
examples.note: 较书面，但仍比although稍轻。

examples.sentence: Though he is young, he is more reliable than some older staff.
examples.translation: 虽然他年轻，但他比一些年长员工更可靠。
examples.note: 适合做对比，注意不要再加but。

examples.sentence: I like this cafe. It’s a bit noisy, though.
examples.translation: 我喜欢这家咖啡馆，不过它有点吵。
examples.note: 句尾though是口语常用补充说法。

examples.sentence: Though I don’t agree with everything, I understand your point.
examples.translation: 虽然我不是每一点都同意，但我理解你的观点。
examples.note: 适合表达委婉不同意。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: {concession}放完整让步从句，如 I was tired
{main}放主要结果或判断，如 I finished the work
templates.exampleSentence: Though I was tired, I finished the work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然我累了，我还是完成了工作。

templates.label: 句尾替代模板
slotHints: {main}放完整句子；though放句尾表示“不过”
常用于口语补充，不再另加though从句
templates.exampleSentence: It’s expensive, though.
templates.exampleTranslation: 不过，它很贵。

templates.label: 对比评价模板
slotHints: {concession}放让步事实，如 he is young
{main}放比较内容，如 he is more careful
{comparison}放比较对象，如 others
templates.exampleSentence: Though he is young, he is more careful than others.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然他年轻，但他比别人更仔细。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
slotHints: {concession}可放客观限制，如 the sample is small
{main}写主要价值或结论，如 the results are still useful
templates.exampleSentence: Though the sample is small, the results are still useful.
templates.exampleTranslation: 虽然样本很小，结果仍然有用。

items.title: whereas + clause
items.coreExplanation: whereas用来把两个事实、情况或特征并列对照，强调“而、却、相比之下”。它后面必须接完整从句，最常放在两部分中间：A, whereas B。也可句首写Whereas B, A，但更常见于书面比较、报告和分析句。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式，常见于论文、报告、新闻、合同说明、数据分析等需要冷静对比的场景。口语里能用，但通常会显得较书面；日常聊天更常用while或but。它不适合单纯表示让步，也不接名词。写作时常在前一分句后加逗号，再接whereas从句；句首时，whereas从句后也通常加逗号。初学者先稳用“A..., whereas B...”模板最安全。

slotSchema.label: 前半句A
slotSchema.hint: 放一个完整句子，先说第一项事实或特点。常错：只放短语而不是完整主谓。例：Sales increased in June

slotSchema.label: whereas后从句B
slotSchema.hint: 必须是完整从句，常用来写相对情况。常错：whereas后只接名词。例：whereas costs remained stable

slotSchema.label: 可比对象
slotSchema.hint: A和B最好围绕同一主题或可比较对象。常错：两句内容毫无对应关系。例：City residents prefer buses, whereas rural residents rely on cars

commonErrors: We stayed home, whereas the rain was heavy.｜错因：这里不是对照两个可比较事实，而是在说原因。｜改法：用because/since；或改成对照句：We stayed home, whereas they went out.
The north saw growth, whereas a decline in the south.｜错因：whereas后面只有名词短语，不是完整从句。｜改法：补出主语和谓语：The north saw growth, whereas the south saw a decline.
Whereas he is friendly but strict.｜错因：只有whereas从句，没有与之对照的主句。｜改法：补上另一部分：She is patient, whereas he is friendly but strict.
I like tea, whereas although my brother likes coffee.｜错因：重复连词，结构混乱。｜改法：二选一：I like tea, whereas my brother likes coffee.
He works in sales whereas in London.｜错因：whereas后缺少完整陈述，信息残缺。｜改法：补完整：He works in sales, whereas she works in London.
Whereas, the company cut costs, profits fell.｜错因：whereas后面不单独加逗号，且句首结构应是“Whereas从句, 主句”。｜改法：Whereas the company cut costs, profits fell less than expected.

contrasts.title: while + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接完整从句；但while既可表“同时”也可表“对比”， whereas几乎只表正式对照。语气上while更中性、更常见于口语。对比：He likes coffee, while she prefers tea.／更正式：He likes coffee, whereas she prefers tea.

contrasts.title: however
contrasts.note: whereas是连词，直接连接两个从句；however是副词，常分开两个独立句子，前后常有句号或分号。语气上however更像“然而”的转折，whereas更像并列比较。对比：A is cheap, whereas B is durable.／A is cheap. However, B is more durable.

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: although表示“虽然”，重点是让步；whereas表示“而、相比之下”，重点是对照。结构上两者都接从句，但逻辑不同。对比：Although it was expensive, I bought it.／This one is expensive, whereas that one is affordable.

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: despite后接名词或动名词，不接完整从句；whereas后必须接完整从句。语气上despite强调“尽管”，不是并列比较。对比：Despite the rain, we left.／We stayed, whereas they left.

contrasts.title: but
contrasts.note: but通常连接更直接、更口语的转折；whereas更正式，且更适合把两个对应项目平行摆出来。结构上but可连接词、短语或句子，whereas最好连接两个完整可比从句。对比：I’m tired, but I’ll go.／The adults agreed, whereas the children objected.

examples.sentence: Tom likes working alone, whereas Sarah enjoys teamwork.
examples.translation: 汤姆喜欢独自工作，而萨拉喜欢团队合作。
examples.note: 最基础的对照：两个主语，两种偏好。

examples.sentence: Whereas the first plan saves money, the second plan saves time.
examples.translation: 相比第一个方案省钱，第二个方案更省时间。
examples.note: 句首位置也可以；whereas从句后加逗号。

examples.sentence: The urban population grew by 8%, whereas the rural population declined slightly.
examples.translation: 城市人口增长了8%，而农村人口则略有下降。
examples.note: 典型书面分析句，适合数据和报告。

examples.sentence: Although both phones are reliable, the older model is cheaper, whereas the newer one is faster.
examples.translation: 虽然两款手机都很可靠，但旧款更便宜，而新款更快。
examples.note: 和although同句出现时，whereas负责具体对照，不表示让步。

examples.sentence: My sister studies in Beijing, whereas I work in Shanghai.
examples.translation: 我姐姐在北京读书，而我在上海工作。
examples.note: 日常也能用，但语气比but更书面。

examples.sentence: The agreement applies to full-time staff, whereas part-time staff are covered by a separate policy.
examples.translation: 该协议适用于全职员工，而兼职员工则受另一项政策约束。
examples.note: 正式说明、规则文本中很常见。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: A和B都写完整句子，内容要能直接对照；不要在whereas后只放名词
templates.exampleSentence: The north recovered quickly, whereas the south faced delays.
templates.exampleTranslation: 北部恢复很快，而南部遇到了延误。

templates.label: 句首替换模板
slotHints: 先写对照背景B，再写主信息A；whereas从句后记得加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: Whereas online sales increased, store sales fell.
templates.exampleTranslation: 相比线上销售上升，门店销售下降了。

templates.label: 写作分析模板
slotHints: 适合图表、数据、研究结果；X和Y最好是两组可比较对象
templates.exampleSentence: Group A shows steady improvement, whereas Group B shows large fluctuations.
templates.exampleTranslation: A组表现出稳定提升，而B组则波动很大。

templates.label: 人物特征对照模板
slotHints: 形容词前后尽量平行，如 quiet/outgoing, cheap/durable
templates.exampleSentence: This room is bright, whereas that one is quieter.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个房间更明亮，而那个更安静。

items.title: no matter what + clause
items.pattern: no matter what + clause, main clause
items.coreExplanation: no matter what用来表示“无论发生什么/无论是什么情况，后面的结果都不变”。它后面通常接完整从句，常见形式是no matter what + 主语 + 动词，然后接主句。这个让步从句多放句首，后面常用逗号；也可放句末加强语气。
items.usageNote: 语气较强但很常用，口语和书面语都自然，常见于承诺、决心、安慰、规则说明，如表达“无论怎样我都会……”。不适合只列一个具体条件的场景；那时更像even if。句首最常见，后面通常加逗号；句末也可以，但别漏主句。初学者最安全的做法是先用“no matter what + happens/you say/they do, 主句”。

slotSchema.label: no matter what从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整意思，常用“what + 主语 + 动词”，如what happens / what you say / what they do。常见错误是只放名词：no matter what the weather；应改为no matter what the weather is like，或直接用whatever the weather。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放“不变的结果/做法”，常用will/can + 动词，如I will help you。常见错误是主句意思太弱或不完整。例：No matter what happens, I will stay here.

commonErrors: Wrong: No matter what the weather, we will go. 错因：这个结构通常要接完整从句，不是直接接裸名词。改法：No matter what the weather is like, we will go. 或 Whatever the weather, we will go.
Wrong: I will help you no matter what happens, and. 错因：主句后面结构断掉，句子不完整。改法：I will help you no matter what happens. 或 No matter what happens, I will help you.
Wrong: No matter what will happen, I’ll stay calm. 错因：时间从句里通常不用will表示将来。改法：No matter what happens, I’ll stay calm.
Wrong: No matter what, if you need me, call me. 错因：no matter what后面缺少清晰对应内容，句子关系松散。改法：No matter what happens, call me if you need me.
Wrong: Even if what happens, I will go. 错因：把even if和what结构混在一起了。改法：Even if it rains, I will go. / No matter what happens, I will go.
Wrong: No matter what you say, but I won’t change my mind. 错因：英语里让步结构后主句前不再加but。改法：No matter what you say, I won’t change my mind.

contrasts.title: even if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，even if后接一个具体假设条件；no matter what强调所有可能情况都不影响结果。语气上，even if较针对单一情况，no matter what更强。对比：Even if it rains, we’ll go.（就算下雨）/ No matter what happens, we’ll go.（无论发生什么）

contrasts.title: whatever-clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，whatever既可引导名词性结构，也可引导让步意义；no matter what更明确是“无论什么”。语气上，whatever在书面和固定搭配里更灵活。对比：Whatever happens, stay calm. = No matter what happens, stay calm.

contrasts.title: although + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上，although接已知事实让步；no matter what接不确定或全部可能情况。语气上，although较陈述，no matter what较强调“不受影响”。对比：Although he is tired, he keeps working. / No matter what happens, he keeps working.

contrasts.title: despite + noun / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构上，despite后接名词或动名词，不接完整从句；no matter what后通常接完整从句。语气上，despite更简洁书面。对比：Despite the rain, we left. / No matter what the weather is like, we’ll leave.

examples.sentence: No matter what happens, I will help you.
examples.translation: 无论发生什么，我都会帮助你。
examples.note: 最安全、最常用的核心句型。

examples.sentence: I’ll support you no matter what happens.
examples.translation: 无论发生什么，我都会支持你。
examples.note: 从句放句末也自然，语气稍更口语。

examples.sentence: No matter what problems arise, the team must remain calm.
examples.translation: 无论出现什么问题，团队都必须保持冷静。
examples.note: 较书面，arise常见于正式表达。

examples.sentence: Even if it rains, we’ll play indoors, but no matter what happens, the event will continue.
examples.translation: 即使下雨，我们也会改到室内进行；无论发生什么，活动都会继续。
examples.note: 同句对比even if与no matter what的差别。

examples.sentence: No matter what you say, I’m not going back.
examples.translation: 不管你说什么，我都不会回去。
examples.note: 常用于表达坚决态度。

examples.sentence: Call me no matter what time it is.
examples.translation: 不管几点，都给我打电话。
examples.note: 这里what后接时间从句，表示“无论是什么时间”。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: 主句放不变的结果，常用I will/can + 动词，如I will stay with you
templates.exampleSentence: No matter what happens, I will stay with you.
templates.exampleTranslation: 无论发生什么，我都会陪着你。

templates.label: 句末位置模板
slotHints: 前面先说主要信息，再用no matter what happens补充“无论如何”
templates.exampleSentence: We’ll keep trying no matter what happens.
templates.exampleTranslation: 无论发生什么，我们都会继续努力。

templates.label: 可替换主语模板
slotHints: 中间放主语+动词，如you say / they do；主句写你的决定或不变结果
templates.exampleSentence: No matter what they do, we won’t change the plan.
templates.exampleTranslation: 不管他们做什么，我们都不会改变计划。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
slotHints: arise前常放problems, difficulties, issues等复数名词，主句用must/should更正式
templates.exampleSentence: No matter what difficulties arise, we must finish the work.
templates.exampleTranslation: 无论出现什么困难，我们都必须完成这项工作。

items.title: provided that + clause
items.coreExplanation: provided that + 从句 表示“只要／条件是”，强调前面主句成立必须满足这个条件，常见于规则、协议、通知和较正式说明。最常放在主句后，用逗号隔开：main clause, provided that + 完整从句；也可放句首。
items.usageNote: 语气偏正式，比 if 更像“附带条件”或“前提”。适合合同、学校规定、邮件、公告、较正式口语；日常闲聊里常改用 if 或 as long as。provided that 后必须接完整从句，不接名词。放句中常在前面加逗号；放句首后也常用逗号。初学者先安全套用“主句, provided that + 主语 + 动词 ...”。

slotSchema.label: main clause
slotSchema.hint: 写结果或允许发生的事。常见错误：这里只写半句，缺主语或谓语。例：You may leave early

slotSchema.label: subject in condition
slotSchema.hint: 写条件从句的主语。常见错误：省略主语，直接写动词。例：you

slotSchema.label: condition clause
slotSchema.hint: 必须是完整从句：主语+动词+其他内容。常见错误：把 provided that 后面写成名词短语。例：you finish the report today

commonErrors: provided that your ID card → 错在 that 后面不能只接名词短语；要接完整从句。改为：provided that you show your ID card
You can join provided that to pay the fee. → 错在 provided that 后不能接不定式。改为：You can join, provided that you pay the fee.
Provided that you will finish on time, we can proceed. → 在条件从句里通常不用 will 表将来条件。改为：Provided that you finish on time, we can proceed.
We can refund you provided that, the product is unused. → 错在逗号位置；provided that 前可加逗号，后面不要把连接拆开。改为：We can refund you, provided that the product is unused.
You can borrow it, provided that finish it today. → 错在条件从句缺主语。改为：You can borrow it, provided that you finish it today.
Provided that + clause, so we can start. → 错在条件从句后又接 so，造成重复连接。改为：Provided that everyone is ready, we can start.

contrasts.title: vs if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上二者都接完整从句，但 if 更中性、最常用；provided that 更正式，更像“前提是”。规则条款里更自然：We will approve it, provided that all documents are complete. 日常更常说：We’ll approve it if all the documents are complete.

contrasts.title: vs as long as + clause
contrasts.note: 结构相近，都可表示“只要”；但 as long as 口语更常见、更自然，provided that 更正式、更书面。比较：You can stay as long as you’re quiet. / You may remain, provided that you keep quiet.

contrasts.title: vs only if + clause
contrasts.note: only if 更强调“只有在这个条件下才行”，限制更强；provided that 表示附带前提，语气稍柔和。比较：I’ll go only if Tom goes. / I’ll go, provided that Tom goes too.

contrasts.title: vs unless + clause
contrasts.note: unless 表“除非不……否则……”，是否定条件；provided that 是肯定条件。结构都接完整从句，但意思方向不同。比较：We won’t start unless he arrives. / We’ll start, provided that he arrives on time.

examples.sentence: You may leave early, provided that you finish your work.
examples.translation: 只要你完成工作，你就可以早点离开。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：主句在前，条件在后。

examples.sentence: Provided that you have a valid ticket, you can enter the hall.
examples.translation: 只要你有有效门票，就可以进入大厅。
examples.note: 条件放句首也自然，但语气更正式。

examples.sentence: The refund will be issued, provided that the item is returned within seven days.
examples.translation: 如果商品在七天内退回，将予以退款。
examples.note: 很常见于书面通知、条款和客服说明。

examples.sentence: We can extend the deadline if you have a good reason, but only provided that you tell us in advance.
examples.translation: 如果你有充分理由，我们可以延长期限，但前提是你要提前通知我们。
examples.note: 这里突出“前提条件”，比单纯 if 更强。

examples.sentence: You can borrow my car, provided that you bring it back by nine.
examples.translation: 你可以借我的车，只要你九点前还回来。
examples.note: 虽然偏正式，但在较认真的日常约定中也能用。

examples.sentence: Admission is free, provided that visitors register online first.
examples.translation: 访客只要先在线登记即可免费入场。
examples.note: 公告体、说明体很自然。

templates.label: safest beginner template
slotHints: 先写结果或许可，再写完整条件从句
provided that 后一定要有主语和动词
templates.exampleSentence: You can use this room, provided that you keep it clean.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你可以使用这个房间，只要你保持整洁。

templates.label: alternate-position template
slotHints: 想先说前提时用句首位置
句首条件后通常加逗号
templates.exampleSentence: Provided that you arrive before six, we can meet today.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要你六点前到，我们今天就能见面。

templates.label: writing-oriented template
slotHints: 适合规定、通知、条款
主句常用 will be + 过去分词
templates.exampleSentence: Your application will be accepted, provided that all documents are complete.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只要所有文件齐全，你的申请将被受理。

items.title: only if + clause
items.coreExplanation: only if 表示“只有在这个条件下才……”，强调结果受这一条件严格限制。基本结构是主句 + only if + 从句，也可把 only if 从句放句首，此时主句通常要部分倒装。它常出现在规则、要求、正式说明中。
items.usageNote: 语气比普通 if 更强，更像“仅在……情况下”。常用于制度、通知、申请条件、正式承诺，也可用于较郑重的口语提醒。不适合表达一般、宽松或不确定的条件；那时用 if 更自然。放句中时通常不加逗号：You can go only if...；放句首时后面常加逗号，且主句要倒装：Only if you finish, can you go. 初学者最安全的写法是先用“主句 + only if + 从句”。

slotSchema.label: main clause
slotSchema.hint: 写结果或许可，常用 can/will/may 等。常见错误是把限制意思写弱成普通陈述。例：You can leave

slotSchema.label: only if
slotSchema.hint: 固定写法 only if。常见错误是误换成 if only；if only 表示“要是……就好了”。例：only if

slotSchema.label: condition clause
slotSchema.hint: 写真实条件，通常用一般现在时表示将来条件。常见错误是在条件从句里乱用 will。例：you finish your work

commonErrors: You can go only if you will finish early.｜条件从句里一般不用 will 表示普通将来条件。｜改为：You can go only if you finish early.
Only if you finish your work, you can leave.｜句首 Only if 从句后，主句通常要部分倒装。｜改为：Only if you finish your work can you leave.
If only you finish your work, you can leave.｜if only 不是“只有如果”，而是“要是……就好了”。｜改为：You can leave only if you finish your work.
You can only if finish your work leave.｜only if 应该连在一起引出条件从句，位置不能拆散。｜改为：You can leave only if you finish your work.
Only if he studies hard, he will pass.｜句首 only if 后主句要倒装，而且更自然写成 will he pass。｜改为：Only if he studies hard will he pass.
I’ll help you only if maybe I have time.｜only if 后应接完整、明确的从句；maybe 放这里使条件松散别扭。｜改为：I’ll help you only if I have time.

contrasts.title: only if vs if
contrasts.note: 结构上，if 只是普通条件；only if 是“唯一条件/严格限制”。语气上，if 中性，only if 更强、更像规则。对比：If you call, I’ll answer.（普通条件）/ I’ll answer only if you call.（只有你打来我才接）

contrasts.title: only if vs unless
contrasts.note: 结构上，unless = if...not，表示“除非”；only if 表示“只有在……才”。语义方向不同：unless 强调“否则不”，only if 强调“唯一条件”。对比：I won’t go unless he goes. / I’ll go only if he goes.

contrasts.title: only if vs provided that
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接从句，但 provided that 更正式，常见于合同、书面条款；only if 更强调限制性。对比：You may borrow it provided that you return it tomorrow. / You may borrow it only if you return it tomorrow.

contrasts.title: only if vs only when
contrasts.note: 结构上，only if 强调条件是否满足；only when 更强调时间点或时机。语境上，谈规则用 only if 更自然，谈“到……时候才”用 only when。对比：You can enter only if you have ID. / You will understand only when you try it.

contrasts.title: only if vs if only
contrasts.note: 词序不同，意思完全不同。only if = 只有在……才；if only = 要是……就好了，表示愿望或遗憾。对比：I’ll go only if she goes. / If only she were here!

examples.sentence: You can leave only if you finish your homework.
examples.translation: 只有完成作业，你才可以离开。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：主句 + only if + 从句。

examples.sentence: Only if you have a ticket can you enter the hall.
examples.translation: 只有持票，你才能进入大厅。
examples.note: only if 从句前置时，主句用部分倒装 can you enter。

examples.sentence: The scholarship will be awarded only if all documents are submitted by Friday.
examples.translation: 只有在周五前提交全部材料，奖学金才会发放。
examples.note: 书面、通知风格很自然。

examples.sentence: If you study hard, you may pass; you will succeed only if you keep working consistently.
examples.translation: 如果你努力学习，你可能会通过；但只有持续努力，你才会成功。
examples.note: 前半句是普通 if，后半句是更强的唯一条件，对比明显。

examples.sentence: I’ll lend you my bike only if you bring it back before six.
examples.translation: 只有你在六点前还回来，我才把自行车借给你。
examples.note: 日常口语也能用，但语气比 if 更硬。

examples.sentence: Only if the data are accurate will the report be reliable.
examples.translation: 只有数据准确，报告才会可靠。
examples.note: 较正式、写作感强。

templates.label: Beginner-safe template
slotHints: [main] 写结果、许可或承诺，如 You can go / I will help you
[condition] 写真实条件，常用一般现在时，如 you finish your work
templates.exampleSentence: You can use my laptop only if you ask first.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只有你先征得同意，才可以用我的笔记本。

templates.label: Fronted condition template
slotHints: [condition] 写前置条件从句，如 you have your ID
主句要倒装，把 can/will/is 等放到主语前
templates.exampleSentence: Only if you have your ID can you enter.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只有你带了身份证件，才能进入。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
slotHints: [result] 可写制度、申请、计划等，如 The request / The application
[condition] 常写书面条件，如 all forms are completed correctly
templates.exampleSentence: The application will be accepted only if all sections are completed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 只有所有部分都填写完整，申请才会被接受。

items.title: as soon as + clause
items.pattern: as soon as + clause, main clause
items.coreExplanation: as soon as + 从句 表示“某事一发生，另一事立刻发生”。as soon as 后面必须接完整从句，通常用主语+动词；主句也要完整。时间从句可放句首，也可放主句后。说将来时，as soon as 从句通常不用 will，而主句常用 will。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合说计划、承诺、说明步骤、日常安排。它强调“紧接着、几乎没有间隔”，如果只是一般时间关系，不一定要用它。句首用时后面常加逗号；句尾用时通常不加。初学者最安全的做法是先写“As soon as + 主语 + 现在时, 主语 + will + 动词”。
sceneTags: 做完一件事立刻下一步
日常回家/到达后马上行动

slotSchema.label: as soon as 后面的时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整从句：主语+动词，如 as soon as I get home。常见错误：只放名词或 will。不要说 as soon as my arrival / as soon as I will get home。

slotSchema.label: 主句
slotSchema.hint: 放紧接着发生的动作，如 I’ll call you。常见错误：主句不完整。要有明确主语和谓语。例：As soon as she arrives, we’ll start.

commonErrors: as soon as I will get home, I will call you.｜错因：时间从句里一般不用 will。｜改法：As soon as I get home, I will call you.
as soon as arriving home, I’ll cook.｜错因：as soon as 后通常接完整从句，不直接接 v-ing。｜改法：As soon as I arrive home, I’ll cook. / After arriving home, I’ll cook.
I’ll call you as soon as.｜错因：as soon as 后面缺少完整从句。｜改法：I’ll call you as soon as I finish work.
As soon as I finished dinner, I will take a shower.｜错因：时态搭配不自然，前后时间基准不一致。｜改法：As soon as I finish dinner, I’ll take a shower. / As soon as I finished dinner, I took a shower.
As soon as the train, we left.｜错因：as soon as 后不能只接名词短语。｜改法：As soon as the train arrived, we left.
We will start the meeting, as soon as everyone arrives.｜错因：逗号把主句和句尾从句硬断开，不自然。｜改法：We will start the meeting as soon as everyone arrives. / As soon as everyone arrives, we will start the meeting.

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接完整从句；但 when 只表示“当……时”，不一定强调立刻，语气更宽。as soon as 强调几乎无间隔。对比：When I get home, I usually rest.（一般情况）/ As soon as I get home, I check my email.（马上做）

contrasts.title: once + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上也接完整从句。once 更像“ 一旦…… / 等……之后就”，常带条件或流程感，书面感稍强；as soon as 更直接强调时间上的立刻。对比：Once you finish, send it to me. / As soon as you finish, send it to me.

contrasts.title: after + v-ing / noun
contrasts.note: 结构差别最大：after 后面可接 v-ing 或名词，也可接从句；as soon as 这里安全用法是接从句。after 只表示“之后”，不强调马上。对比：After dinner, we went out.（饭后，未必立刻）/ As soon as dinner ended, we went out.（一结束就去）

contrasts.title: while + clause (time)
contrasts.note: while 接从句时强调两个动作同时进行或一段时间内发生；as soon as 表示前后紧接。场景不同，不能互换。对比：While I was cooking, he cleaned.（同时）/ As soon as I finished cooking, he ate.（先后紧接）

examples.sentence: As soon as I get home, I’ll call you.
examples.translation: 我一到家就给你打电话。
examples.note: 最安全的初学者句型：时间从句用现在时，主句用 will。

examples.sentence: I’ll text you as soon as the meeting ends.
examples.translation: 会议一结束，我就给你发短信。
examples.note: 从句放句尾也很常见，通常不加逗号。

examples.sentence: As soon as payment is received, your order will be processed.
examples.translation: 一收到付款，您的订单就会被处理。
examples.note: 较书面、常见于通知和商务邮件。

examples.sentence: When I get to the office, I drink coffee; as soon as I get to the office, I check my messages.
examples.translation: 我到办公室时会喝咖啡；我一到办公室就会查看消息。
examples.note: 对比 when 与 as soon as：后者更强调立刻。

examples.sentence: As soon as she finishes dinner, she washes the dishes.
examples.translation: 她一吃完晚饭就洗碗。
examples.note: 也可用于习惯性动作，不只讲未来。

examples.sentence: Please close the window as soon as it starts raining.
examples.translation: 一开始下雨，请马上把窗户关上。
examples.note: 适合说明指令或提醒。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
templates.template: As soon as [subject] [present verb], [subject] will [base verb].
slotHints: 前半句写先发生的事，用一般现在时，不用 will；后半句写紧接着发生的事。
templates.exampleSentence: As soon as I finish my homework, I will watch TV.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我一做完作业就看电视。

templates.label: 句尾位置模板
templates.template: [Subject] will [base verb] as soon as [subject] [present verb].
slotHints: 适合把重点放在主句动作上；句尾从句前通常不要加逗号。
templates.exampleSentence: We will leave as soon as the rain stops.
templates.exampleTranslation: 雨一停我们就走。

templates.label: 书面表达模板
templates.template: As soon as [event] is [past participle], [result] will [base verb/passive].
slotHints: 常用于通知、流程、邮件，如 payment is received, form is approved。
templates.exampleSentence: As soon as your application is approved, you will receive an email.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你的申请一获批准，你就会收到一封邮件。

items.title: by the time + clause
items.pattern: by the time + clause, main clause
items.coreExplanation: by the time 表示“到……的时候为止”，重点是截止到某个时间点之前，主句动作已经完成、发生或状态已经成立。后面必须接完整从句；整个 by the time 从句常放句首，也可放句末，主句里常配完成时。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，常用于计划、截止时间、结果预测和叙述先后顺序。它自然出现在“到那时已经……”的场景，不适合只想说“当……时”的普通同时发生。句首时后面通常加逗号；句末一般不用。初学者最稳妥的做法：先写 by the time + 一般现在时/一般过去时从句，再让主句用 will have done 或 had done。
sceneTags: 做计划时说明截止结果

slotSchema.label: 时间从句
slotSchema.hint: 这里放完整主谓结构，表示一个时间点，如 she arrives / we got there。常见错误是只放名词时间，如 × by the time tomorrow；要改成 by the time it is tomorrow morning 或直接用 by tomorrow。例：by the time he arrives

slotSchema.label: 主句结果
slotSchema.hint: 这里放到那个时间点之前已经完成或成立的内容，初学者优先用 will have + 过去分词 或 had + 过去分词。常见错误是用普通将来时却没有“已完成”感。例：we will have finished the report

slotSchema.label: 位置
slotSchema.hint: 可放句首：By the time..., 主句；也可放句末：主句 by the time...。常见错误是句首不用逗号导致难读。例：By the time you wake up, I’ll have left.

commonErrors: × By the time tomorrow, I will finish it.｜错因：by the time 后面应接从句，不直接接时间名词。｜改为：By tomorrow, I will finish it. / By the time it is tomorrow evening, I will have finished it.
× By the time he arrives, I leave.｜错因：主句没有表达“到那时已经离开”的完成感。｜改为：By the time he arrives, I will have left.
× By the time we got there, the movie starts.｜错因：过去参照点下，主句时态不一致；应表示“那时已经开始了”。｜改为：By the time we got there, the movie had started.
× I will have finished by the time when you arrive.｜错因：by the time 后面不用 when，重复了。｜改为：I will have finished by the time you arrive.
× By the time I was a student, I lived in Beijing.｜错因：这里不是“截止到某时已完成”，而是身份时间背景，by the time 不自然。｜改为：When I was a student, I lived in Beijing.
× By the time she arrives, I will finish dinner.｜错因：意思更像“到她到时我才完成”，英语里更自然说已经完成。｜改为：By the time she arrives, I will have finished dinner.

contrasts.title: when + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都接从句，但 when 只表示“当……时”，不自带“到那时已经”含义；语气更中性、范围更广。比较：When he arrives, we’ll eat.＝他到时我们开吃；By the time he arrives, we’ll have eaten.＝他到时我们已经吃完了。

contrasts.title: before + clause
contrasts.note: before 强调“在……之前”这个先后关系，未必说明另一动作到那时已完成；by the time 强调截止点和结果状态。比较：Finish it before he arrives.＝在他到前完成；By the time he arrives, you’ll have finished it.＝他到时你已经完成了。

contrasts.title: until + clause
contrasts.note: until 表示动作或状态持续到某时才停止/改变；by the time 表示到某时之前已完成。场景和逻辑不同。比较：I waited until he arrived.＝我一直等到他来；By the time he arrived, I had left.＝他来时我已经走了。

contrasts.title: after + clause
contrasts.note: after 说明“在……之后”再发生，关注先后触发；by the time 关注截止时点前的完成状态。比较：After she arrived, we started.＝她到了以后我们才开始；By the time she arrived, we had started.＝她到时我们已经开始了。

contrasts.title: by + 时间名词
contrasts.note: by 后面可直接接具体时间名词，表示“最晚到……为止”；by the time 后面通常接从句。语气上 by + 时间名词更简洁。比较：By Friday, I’ll be done.；By the time Friday comes, I’ll be done. 后者更像叙述句。

examples.sentence: By the time you arrive, I will have finished dinner.
examples.translation: 你到的时候，我就已经吃完晚饭了。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的将来完成用法。

examples.sentence: I had already left by the time he called me.
examples.translation: 他给我打电话的时候，我已经离开了。
examples.note: 同一结构放句末也很自然。

examples.sentence: By the time the meeting begins, we will have prepared all the documents.
examples.translation: 到会议开始时，我们将已经准备好所有文件。
examples.note: 适合工作和计划场景。

examples.sentence: When she gets home, I’ll cook dinner; by the time she gets home, I’ll have cooked dinner.
examples.translation: 她到家时，我会做晚饭；她到家时，我就已经把晚饭做好了。
examples.note: 对比 when 和 by the time 的差别。

examples.sentence: By the time we got to the station, the train had left.
examples.translation: 我们到车站的时候，火车已经开走了。
examples.note: 过去时间点前已完成，用过去完成时。

examples.sentence: By the time this report is published, the data will have changed again.
examples.translation: 到这份报告发表的时候，数据将又已经变化了。
examples.note: 较偏书面语、写作常见。

templates.label: 最稳妥初学者模板
templates.template: By the time + 从句（一般现在时）, + 主句（will have + 过去分词）.
slotHints: 从句写一个将来的时间点事件，如 you arrive / she gets home；主句写到那时已经完成的动作，如 will have finished / will have left。
templates.exampleSentence: By the time she gets home, I will have cleaned the kitchen.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她到家的时候，我就已经把厨房打扫好了。

templates.label: 句末位置模板
templates.template: 主句（had + 过去分词） + by the time + 从句（一般过去时）.
slotHints: 用于讲过去经历；主句写先发生且已完成的动作，从句写后到达的过去时间点。
templates.exampleSentence: We had found our seats by the time the movie started.
templates.exampleTranslation: 电影开始的时候，我们已经找到座位了。

templates.label: 书面计划模板
templates.template: By the time + 从句, + 主语 + will have + 过去分词 + 其他信息.
slotHints: 适合项目、报告、截止安排；可加 all, already, fully 等词强化“已完成”。
templates.exampleSentence: By the time the semester ends, the team will have completed the final project.
templates.exampleTranslation: 到学期结束时，团队将已经完成期末项目。

templates.label: 状态成立模板
templates.template: By the time + 从句, + 主语 + will have been + 形容词/名词.
slotHints: 不只可用于动作完成，也可表示状态到那时已经成立，如 will have been ready / will have been a teacher for ten years。
templates.exampleSentence: By the time you read this, I will have been ready for hours.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你读到这个时，我早就准备好了。

items.title: whether or not + clause
items.coreExplanation: whether or not + 从句 用来明确表示“两种情况都纳入考虑”：要么在问“是否……”，要么表示“无论是否……都一样”。后面必须接完整从句，常放在动词后作宾语，也常放句首作主语或在介词后，语气比单独 whether 更完整明确。
items.usageNote: 这是中性到偏正式的结构，常见于决定、说明条件无关、通知、邮件、学术或书面表达，也能用于较认真口语。日常随意对话里常可用 whether 或 if 替代，但在介词后、句首主语位置、或想强调“两种情况都算”时，whether or not 更自然。它通常整体连写在从句前，不要把 or not 随便丢到句末；初学者先用“动词+whether or not+完整句子”最稳。

slotSchema.label: 前面的动词/固定搭配
slotSchema.hint: 常用 know, decide, ask, discuss, depend on, be unclear 等。常见错误是随便接任何动词；像 say 后通常不用。例：We need to decide ...

slotSchema.label: 从句主语
slotSchema.hint: 放人、物或 it/there 都可以。常见错误是只写名词不成完整句。例：whether or not the plan ...

slotSchema.label: 从句谓语部分
slotSchema.hint: 这里要补成完整陈述句，如 is ready, will come, we should leave。常见错误是只放动词原形或短语。例：whether or not she agrees

commonErrors: wrong: We will go whether or not?｜错因：whether or not 不能单独当完整问句尾巴。｜改法：后面接完整从句：We will go whether or not he comes.
wrong: I don't know whether or not to go?｜错因：此卡型是 whether or not + 从句，不是 to-infinitive 结构；而且陈述句不该用问号。｜改法：I don't know whether or not I should go.
wrong: It depends of whether or not he is ready.｜错因：固定搭配是 depend on，不是 depend of。｜改法：It depends on whether or not he is ready.
wrong: Whether or not he will come is unclear to me?｜错因：这是陈述句，不用问号。｜改法：Whether or not he will come is unclear to me.
wrong: I asked if or not she was busy.｜错因：不能说 if or not；要么用 if/whether，要么用 whether or not。｜改法：I asked whether or not she was busy.
wrong: We discussed whether or not about the plan would change.｜错因：whether or not 后不能先塞 about；应直接接完整从句。｜改法：We discussed whether or not the plan would change.

contrasts.title: whether + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上 whether 可单独接从句，whether or not 更明确地把“是/否”两面都说出来。语气上前者更简洁，后者更强调两种可能都在考虑。对比：I don't know whether he will come. / I don't know whether or not he will come.

contrasts.title: if + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上 if 也可表“是否”，但不能自然用于很多正式位置，尤其介词后通常用 whether。语气上 if 更口语、是否感较弱。对比：It depends on whether he comes. 不说 depends on if he comes.

contrasts.title: whether + clause + or not
contrasts.note: 结构上 or not 也可放在从句后：whether he comes or not；而 whether or not he comes 把选择提前，更紧凑。语气上两者都对，前者稍更展开。对比：I don't know whether or not he agrees. / I don't know whether he agrees or not.

contrasts.title: regardless of whether
contrasts.note: 结构上 regardless of whether 后常接从句，前面多了介词短语；whether or not 本身只负责引出“是否”从句。语气上 regardless of whether 更正式，强调“无论是否都不影响”。对比：We'll start whether or not he comes. / We'll start regardless of whether he comes.

contrasts.title: no matter what + clause
contrasts.note: 结构上 no matter what 后接内容/事情，不是单纯“是否”；whether or not 只处理 yes/no 两种可能。语气上 no matter what 更强调“任何情况”。对比：We'll help you whether or not you agree. / We'll help you no matter what happens.

examples.sentence: I don't know whether or not she is at home.
examples.translation: 我不知道她是否在家。
examples.note: 最安全的基础用法：动词后接 whether or not 从句。

examples.sentence: Whether or not he will join us is still unclear.
examples.translation: 他是否会加入我们仍然不清楚。
examples.note: 放句首作主语，更偏书面。

examples.sentence: The decision should be made based on the facts, whether or not they are convenient.
examples.translation: 决定应当基于事实，不管这些事实是否方便接受。
examples.note: 写作风格例句，表示结果不取决于“是否”。

examples.sentence: It depends on whether or not the train arrives on time.
examples.translation: 这取决于火车是否准点到达。
examples.note: 介词 on 后通常用 whether，不用 if。

examples.sentence: We will go ahead whether or not Tom agrees.
examples.translation: 不管汤姆是否同意，我们都会继续进行。
examples.note: 这里不是单纯提问，而是表示“无论是否都一样”。

examples.sentence: Please let me know whether or not you can come by Friday.
examples.translation: 请在周五前告诉我你能不能来。
examples.note: 邮件和通知里很常见，礼貌且明确。

templates.label: 最稳妥入门模板
templates.template: I don't know whether or not + [subject] + [predicate].
slotHints: [subject] 放人或事物，如 he / the plan
[predicate] 用完整陈述，如 is ready / will come
templates.exampleSentence: I don't know whether or not the meeting is today.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我不知道会议是不是今天。

templates.label: 句首主语模板
slotHints: [subject] 放人或事物
[comment] 常用 unclear, important, true
templates.exampleSentence: Whether or not she agrees is not important.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她是否同意并不重要。

templates.label: 介词后模板
templates.template: It depends on whether or not + [subject] + [predicate].
slotHints: 固定搭配 depends on
从句要完整，不要只写名词
templates.exampleSentence: It depends on whether or not they have enough time.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这取决于他们是否有足够的时间。

templates.label: 写作表达模板
slotHints: 前面先写主句
后面补充“无论是否都不影响”的条件
templates.exampleSentence: We must act now, whether or not the data is complete.
templates.exampleTranslation: 无论数据是否完整，我们现在都必须行动。

items.title: so + adjective / adverb + that + clause
items.coreExplanation: 这个结构用来表示“程度非常……，结果就……”。基本形式是so+形容词/副词+that+从句。so后面放描述程度的词，that后面必须是完整句子。它通常放在主句中间，先说程度，再接结果；口语里that有时可省，但初学者先保留最安全。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和书面语都很常见，适合描述结果、讲故事、解释某事为什么发生。它不适合直接表达“目的”；想说“为了……”不要用这个结构。that后最好紧跟结果从句，不要隔太多成分；写作中保留that更清楚。初学者先用“主语+be动词+so+形容词+that+完整句子”最稳。

slotSchema.label: 形容词或副词
slotSchema.hint: 放表示程度的形容词或副词，如 tired, fast, loudly。常见错误：放名词，如 so a noise。名词要改用 such。例：so tired that...

slotSchema.label: 结果从句
slotSchema.hint: 必须是完整句子，要有主语和谓语，如 that I fell asleep。常见错误：只放短语，如 that asleep。例：that we missed the bus.

slotSchema.label: 主语和前半句
slotSchema.hint: 常见形式是主语+be动词，或主语+实义动词。常见错误：漏掉系动词，如 He so tired。例：She was so happy that...

commonErrors: wrong: He was so a kind person that everyone liked him.｜错因：so后面不能直接接“a+名词”。｜改法：用such a kind person that，或改成so kind that everyone liked him.
wrong: The bag was so heavy to carry.｜错因：that后面应是从句，不是to do。｜改法：The bag was so heavy that I couldn't carry it. 或 The bag was too heavy to carry.
wrong: She so quickly ran that I couldn't see her.｜错因：so+副词通常应紧贴副词，位置不要拆开得太怪。｜改法：She ran so quickly that I couldn't see her.
wrong: I was so tired that to sleep early.｜错因：that后面不是完整句子。｜改法：I was so tired that I went to bed early.
wrong: So the movie was boring that we left.｜错因：普通语序里so不放句首修饰整个主句。｜改法：The movie was so boring that we left.（倒装句型另学）
wrong: He studied hard so that he passed the exam.｜错因：这里容易混成目的结构so that；本句想表达结果，更自然用so...that...｜改法：He studied so hard that he passed the exam.

contrasts.title: such + noun + that
contrasts.note: 结构区别：so后接形容词/副词，such后接名词短语。语气都可中性，但such更适合突出“人/事物”本身。对比：so kind that everyone smiled / such a kind person that everyone smiled.

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：too...to后接不定式；so...that后接完整从句。语义区别：too...to常带“太……而不能……”，结果偏否定；so...that只表示程度导致结果。对比：too tired to walk / so tired that I couldn't walk.

contrasts.title: adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：enough放在形容词后，后面接to do；so...that接从句。语气区别：enough强调“达到足够程度”，so...that更强调强烈结果。对比：old enough to drive / so old that he retired.

contrasts.title: so that + clause
contrasts.note: 结构区别：so...that中间必须有形容词或副词；so that前面是完整主句。功能区别：so...that说结果，so that常说目的。对比：He was so careful that he found the error. / He checked again so that he could find the error.

contrasts.title: because
contrasts.note: 结构区别：because直接引原因从句，不表示程度词；so...that先给高程度，再给结果。场景区别：because回答“为什么”，so...that强调“有多……以至于……”。对比：We stayed home because it rained. / It rained so hard that we stayed home.

examples.sentence: I was so tired that I fell asleep on the sofa.
examples.translation: 我太累了，以至于在沙发上睡着了。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：be动词+so+形容词+that从句。

examples.sentence: She ran so fast that nobody could catch her.
examples.translation: 她跑得太快了，以至于没人追得上她。
examples.note: 这里so修饰副词fast，不是形容词。

examples.sentence: The road was so narrow that two cars could not pass at the same time.
examples.translation: 这条路太窄了，两辆车不能同时通过。
examples.note: 较书面、适合说明情况。

examples.sentence: It was so cold that we stayed inside, not just because of the wind.
examples.translation: 天气冷得我们待在室内，不只是因为有风。
examples.note: 用于和because作意义对比：强调程度导致结果。

examples.sentence: He spoke so quietly that I couldn't hear him.
examples.translation: 他说话太轻了，我听不见。
examples.note: 常见日常口语场景。

examples.sentence: The movie was so funny that we laughed the whole time.
examples.translation: 这部电影太好笑了，我们全程都在笑。
examples.note: 自然的故事叙述句。

templates.label: 初学者最稳模板
templates.template: [主语] + be + so + [形容词] + that + [结果从句]
slotHints: 主语常用I/he/the movie等
形容词用tired, happy, cold等
结果从句必须完整，如 I went home
templates.exampleSentence: I was so hungry that I ate two sandwiches.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我太饿了，所以吃了两个三明治。

templates.label: 副词版模板
templates.template: [主语] + [动词] + so + [副词] + that + [结果从句]
slotHints: 动词常用run, speak, drive等
副词用fast, slowly, quietly等
结果从句写出实际后果
templates.exampleSentence: She spoke so softly that we had to ask her to repeat it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她说得太轻了，我们只好请她重复一遍。

templates.label: 写作常用模板
templates.template: [事物/情况] + be + so + [形容词] + that + [结果从句]
slotHints: 主语可用The problem, The road, The news等
形容词适合客观描述，如 serious, narrow, surprising
写作中that最好保留
templates.exampleSentence: The problem was so serious that the meeting lasted three hours.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个问题太严重了，会议开了三个小时。

items.title: such + noun + that + clause
items.coreExplanation: such + 名词短语 + that 从句 用来表示“如此……以至于……”，重点落在名词短语的程度、性质或数量上，而不只是在形容词上。常见结构是 such a/an + 形容词 + 单数可数名词 + that...，也可用 such + 复数/不可数名词 + that...，通常放在主句中，that 从句紧跟后面说明结果。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和写作都自然，尤其适合描述、强调结果、讲故事或书面说明。适合在你想突出“这是怎样一种人/事/情况”时使用。不适合只想强调形容词本身时，那时常用 so + 形容词 + that。that 后面接完整句子，不要接短语；such 后面先放名词短语。初学者最安全写法是 such a/an + 形容词 + 单数名词 + that + 结果句。

slotSchema.label: 名词短语
slotSchema.hint: 放名词中心的内容，可带冠词、形容词、数量，如 a kind teacher, heavy rain。常错：只放形容词，如 such tired。应改为 such a tired worker。例：such a noisy room

slotSchema.label: 结果从句
slotSchema.hint: 放完整主谓句，说明结果。常错：that 后只接短语，如 that very late。应改为 that we were very late。例：that everyone laughed

slotSchema.label: 名词前修饰语
slotSchema.hint: 可放形容词、数量词或 kind/sort 等，帮助强调名词特点。常错：冠词位置错，如 such an interesting book。例：such a difficult question

commonErrors: wrong: such tired that I slept early；错因：such 后面不能直接接形容词来带 that 从句；应改：so tired that I slept early，或 such fatigue that...
wrong: such a good weather that we went out；错因：weather 是不可数名词，不能用 a；应改：such good weather that we went out
wrong: such interesting book that I bought it；错因：单数可数名词 book 前缺冠词；应改：such an interesting book that I bought it
wrong: it was such cold that we stayed home；错因：cold 这里表示“寒冷”时前面要有名词，如 weather；应改：it was such cold weather that we stayed home
wrong: such a big that everyone noticed it；错因：such 后必须有名词，不可只留 big；应改：such a big mistake that everyone noticed it
wrong: such people are kind that I trust them；错因：that 从句前的名词短语需要形成“如此……以至于……”的整体意义；应改：they are such kind people that I trust them

contrasts.title: so + adjective/adverb + that
contrasts.note: 结构上，so 直接修饰形容词或副词；such 修饰名词短语。语气都可中性强调，但 so 更适合突出“多么怎么样”，such 更适合突出“怎样一种事物”。对比：so tired that he slept / such a tiring day that he slept early。

contrasts.title: too + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构上，too...to 后接不定式，不接 that 从句；such...that 后接完整结果句。too 常带“太……而不能……”的限制意味，such...that 只是陈述结果。对比：too tired to walk / such fatigue that he could not walk。

contrasts.title: adjective + enough to...
contrasts.note: 结构上，enough 放在形容词后，后面接 to do；such 用名词短语加 that 从句。enough 常较直接、日常；such 更有强调和描写感。对比：old enough to drive / such a young driver that everyone worried。

contrasts.title: so that 表目的
contrasts.note: 结构上，such...that 表结果；so that 既可表结果，也常表目的。场景上，写原因结果时用 such...that；写“为了……”时不用 such。对比：It was such a loud noise that we woke up / I spoke slowly so that everyone could understand。

contrasts.title: what a / such a
contrasts.note: 结构上，what a 常用于感叹句，such a 可放在陈述句中接 that 从句。语气上，what a 更感叹、更口语；such a 更适合继续说明结果。对比：What a mess! / It was such a mess that we left immediately。

examples.sentence: It was such a hot day that we stayed inside.
examples.translation: 天气如此炎热，我们只好待在室内。
examples.note: 最安全的基础结构：such a + 形容词 + 单数名词 + that 从句。

examples.sentence: They are such kind people that everyone likes them.
examples.translation: 他们是如此善良的人，大家都喜欢他们。
examples.note: such 可修饰复数名词 people，不用冠词。

examples.sentence: We had such heavy rain that the game was canceled.
examples.translation: 雨下得这么大，比赛被取消了。
examples.note: 不可数名词 rain 前不用 a。

examples.sentence: It was, in fact, such a simple idea that nobody noticed its value at first.
examples.translation: 事实上，这是一个如此简单的想法，以至于一开始没人注意到它的价值。
examples.note: 写作中可在主句插入副词短语，但 that 从句仍紧跟 such 名词短语后的主句部分。

examples.sentence: She made such a strange comment that the room went quiet.
examples.translation: 她说了一句非常奇怪的话，房间一下子安静了。
examples.note: 突出“怎样一种评论”，比 so strange 更偏重名词 comment。

examples.sentence: It was such good coffee that I ordered another cup.
examples.translation: 咖啡太好了，我又点了一杯。
examples.note: good coffee 是不可数名词短语。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
slotHints: [modifier] 放常见形容词，如 nice, hot, difficult
[noun] 放单数可数名词，如 day, book, problem
[resultClause] 放完整结果句，如 we stayed home
templates.exampleSentence: It was such a difficult test that many students gave up.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这次考试太难了，许多学生放弃了。

templates.label: 复数或不可数名词模板
slotHints: [modifier] 可放 many, heavy, good 等
[nounPhrase] 放复数名词或不可数名词，如 problems, rain, luck
[resultClause] 写因此发生的结果
templates.exampleSentence: We had such heavy traffic that we missed the train.
templates.exampleTranslation: 路上太堵了，我们错过了火车。

templates.label: 换主语模板
slotHints: [Subject] 可放人、事、地点
[modifier]+[noun] 一起构成名词短语
[resultClause] 用过去式或现在式保持时态一致
templates.exampleSentence: She is such a careful teacher that her students trust her.
templates.exampleTranslation: 她是一位非常认真的老师，所以学生们信任她。

templates.label: 写作型模板
slotHints: [nounPhrase] 可用抽象或不可数名词，如 confusion, pressure, noise
[resultClause] 说明造成的客观结果
templates.exampleSentence: There was such confusion that nobody knew what to do.
templates.exampleTranslation: 当时一片混乱，没人知道该怎么办。

items.title: comparative + than
items.coreExplanation: comparative + than 用来直接比较两者，表示“A比B更……”。基本结构是 A + be/verb + 比较级 + than + B。比较级前常有 much, a little, even 等程度词。than 后面多放被比较的人、事、时间或地点，通常紧跟在比较级后，不要随意隔开太远。
items.usageNote: 这是中性、非常常用的比较句型，适合日常对话、考试写作、观点表达和简单说明。最自然的场景是明确拿两个对象作对比。不适合只有一个对象、没有比较基准的句子；那时直接用形容词或副词更自然。than 后面一般不加逗号，通常贴着比较级出现。初学者最安全的做法是先用“A is 比较级 than B”或“A 动词 + 副词比较级 than B”。
sceneTags: 描述价格速度大小差异

slotSchema.label: 被说明的对象A
slotSchema.hint: 放主要对象，如 a car, Tom, this book。常见错误是把 A 和 B 顺序说反，导致意思相反。例：Tom is taller than Jack.

slotSchema.label: 比较级部分
slotSchema.hint: 放形容词比较级或副词比较级，如 taller, more expensive, faster。常见错误是用原级或最高级。例：more useful / runs faster

slotSchema.label: 比较对象B
slotSchema.hint: 放被拿来比较的人或事，如 me, the old one, last year。常见错误是漏掉 than 或 than 后只放形容词。例：than me / than the old plan

commonErrors: wrong pattern: He is tall than me.｜原因：tall 是原级，than 前要用比较级。｜改法：He is taller than me.
wrong pattern: This book is more better than that one.｜原因：better 已经是比较级，不能再加 more。｜改法：This book is better than that one.
wrong pattern: She more carefully than I do.｜原因：缺少动词，副词比较级不能单独作谓语。｜改法：She works more carefully than I do.
wrong pattern: My phone is cheaper from yours.｜原因：英语比较通常用 than，不用 from。｜改法：My phone is cheaper than yours.
wrong pattern: He is taller than.｜原因：than 后缺少比较对象，信息不完整。｜改法：He is taller than his brother.
wrong pattern: Of the three, Tom is taller than.｜原因：三者中选最高通常要用最高级，不是空着的 than 结构。｜改法：Of the three, Tom is the tallest.

contrasts.title: as...as...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：as + 原级 + as；comparative + than 用比较级。语气/场景区别：as...as...强调“和……一样”，than 强调“更……”。对比：He is as tall as me. / He is taller than me.

contrasts.title: the more..., the more...
contrasts.note: 结构区别：the + 比较级, the + 比较级 是两个分句；comparative + than 是直接比较两者。场景区别：前者说“越……越……”，后者说“A比B更……”。对比：The more you read, the faster you learn. / You read faster than me.

contrasts.title: superlative
contrasts.note: 结构区别：最高级常用 the + -est / the most；than 结构用比较级。场景区别：最高级用于三者或以上中“最……”，than 用于两者直接比较。对比：Tom is taller than Jack. / Tom is the tallest in the class.

contrasts.title: -er vs more
contrasts.note: 结构区别：短形容词常加 -er，较长形容词常用 more + 形容词。语体区别不大，但错误搭配会很不自然。对比：taller than me 正确；more tall than me 不自然。more expensive than this 正确。

contrasts.title: than vs compared with
contrasts.note: 结构区别：than 必须接比较级；compared with 后面常接完整描述。场景区别：than 更直接、更口语；compared with 稍偏书面、分析性。对比：This room is bigger than that one. / Compared with that room, this one is bigger.

examples.sentence: My bag is heavier than yours.
examples.translation: 我的包比你的重。
examples.note: 最安全的基础模板：A + be + 比较级 + than + B。

examples.sentence: She runs faster than her brother.
examples.translation: 她跑得比她哥哥快。
examples.note: 动词后用副词比较级。

examples.sentence: This year, sales are higher than last year.
examples.translation: 今年的销售额比去年高。
examples.note: 时间状语放句首也很自然，than 仍紧跟比较级后。

examples.sentence: This sofa is more comfortable than that chair.
examples.translation: 这张沙发比那把椅子更舒服。
examples.note: 长一点的形容词常用 more + 形容词。

examples.sentence: Compared with the old plan, this one is cheaper than I expected.
examples.translation: 和旧方案相比，这个比我预想的更便宜。
examples.note: 句中仍是 comparative + than；这里的 than 比较的是“超出预期”的程度，容易和 compared with 混淆。

examples.sentence: The train is much faster than the bus.
examples.translation: 火车比公交快得多。
examples.note: much 放在比较级前，表示程度更明显。

templates.label: 最安全初学者模板
slotHints: item1 放人或物；comp 用 taller/better/more useful 这类比较级；item2 放比较对象
templates.exampleSentence: My room is smaller than hers.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我的房间比她的小。

templates.label: 动作用法模板
slotHints: 这里的 comp 常是副词比较级，如 faster, more carefully；不要漏掉动词
templates.exampleSentence: He drives more carefully than his father.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他开车比他父亲更小心。

templates.label: 句首加场景信息模板
slotHints: 先放时间地点等信息，再说比较；than 仍要跟在比较级后面
templates.exampleSentence: In summer, this lake is busier than that beach.
templates.exampleTranslation: 夏天这个湖比那个海滩更热闹。

templates.label: 写作用数据模板
templates.template: [item1] is [comp] than [item2] in terms of [fixed point].
slotHints: 适合说明价格、效率、质量等；comp 常用 higher/lower/better/worse
templates.exampleSentence: This model is better than the old one in terms of battery life.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这个型号在续航方面比旧款更好。

items.title: would rather + base
items.coreExplanation: would rather 用来表达“宁愿、更想做某事”的个人选择，后面直接接动词原形，不加 to。要比较两个动作时，用 would rather A than B，than 后也接动词原形。它通常放在主语后面作谓语核心，如 I’d rather stay home.
items.usageNote: 这是口语和中性书面语里都很常见的表达，语气自然，适合聊天、提出选择、礼貌说明偏好。它最常用于说“我/我们宁愿……”，也可问别人偏好。一般不用来陈述很正式、客观的数据偏好。注意 would 常缩写成 ’d，否定是 would rather not + 动词原形；than 短语放在后面，不要在 rather 后立刻加 than。初学者先稳用 I’d rather + 动词原形。

slotSchema.label: Subject
slotSchema.hint: 放人称主语或名词短语，如 I, we, she。常见错误：漏掉主语直接说 would rather stay。正确：I’d rather stay home.

slotSchema.label: Preferred action
slotSchema.hint: 放动词原形或“动词原形+宾语”，不能加 to，也不要用 -ing。错误：would rather to go / would rather going。正确：would rather go now.

slotSchema.label: Compared action
slotSchema.hint: 用 than 引出另一种较不想要的动作，than 后也用动词原形。常见错误：than to drive。正确：I’d rather walk than drive.

commonErrors: I would rather to go now.｜错在 rather 后不能接 to do。｜改为：I would rather go now.
She would rather going by train.｜错在 rather 后不能接 -ing。｜改为：She would rather go by train.
We would rather stay home than to go out.｜错在 than 后也要用动词原形。｜改为：We would rather stay home than go out.
Rather I would stay here.｜错在位置不自然；would rather 通常放在主语后。｜改为：I would rather stay here.
I rather would call him.｜错在词序；固定说法是 would rather，不是 rather would。｜改为：I would rather call him.
I would rather not to wait.｜错在否定形式里 not 后仍用动词原形。｜改为：I would rather not wait.

contrasts.title: prefer A to B
contrasts.note: 结构上 prefer 常接名词或动名词：prefer tea to coffee / prefer walking to driving；would rather 接动词原形。语气上 prefer 稍更平稳，也常用于一般习惯；would rather 更像眼前选择。对比：I’d rather walk today. / I prefer walking to driving.

contrasts.title: rather than
contrasts.note: 结构上 rather than 本身多连接两个并列成分，未必前面有 would；would rather 是完整谓语结构。语气上 rather than 更像“而不是”，书面感稍强。对比：I chose tea rather than coffee. / I’d rather drink tea than coffee.

contrasts.title: would like to
contrasts.note: 结构上 would like to 后接 to do；would rather 后接动词原形。意思上 would like to 是“想要”，不一定有比较；would rather 是“宁愿，更偏向”。对比：I’d like to rest. / I’d rather rest than work late.

contrasts.title: had better
contrasts.note: 结构上 had better 也接动词原形，但表示“最好”，带建议或警告；would rather 表示个人偏好。场景不同：前者谈合适做法，后者谈自己想做什么。对比：You’d better leave now. / I’d rather leave now.

contrasts.title: want to
contrasts.note: 结构上 want to 接 to do；would rather 接原形。语气上 want to 更直接，说“想做”；would rather 更柔和，也常带比较选择。对比：I want to go home. / I’d rather go home than stay here.

examples.sentence: I’d rather stay home.
examples.translation: 我宁愿待在家里。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：主语 + would rather + 动词原形。

examples.sentence: I’d rather walk than drive.
examples.translation: 我宁愿走路，也不愿开车。
examples.note: 有比较时用 than + 动词原形。

examples.sentence: We would rather not discuss it now.
examples.translation: 我们现在宁愿不讨论这件事。
examples.note: 否定形式是 would rather not + 动词原形。

examples.sentence: Rather than wait for a taxi, I’d rather take the subway.
examples.translation: 与其等出租车，我更愿意坐地铁。
examples.note: 同句中可出现 rather than 和 would rather，但作用不同。

examples.sentence: In this situation, most residents would rather keep the old trees than cut them down.
examples.translation: 在这种情况下，大多数居民宁愿保留老树，也不愿把它们砍掉。
examples.note: 中性书面语里也自然，适合说明偏好选择。

examples.sentence: Would you rather sit inside or outside?
examples.translation: 你更愿意坐里面还是外面？
examples.note: 常用于礼貌询问别人偏好。

templates.label: Safest beginner template
slotHints: 先只表达一个偏好，不必急着加 than 比较；动词不要加 to。
templates.exampleSentence: I’d rather leave now.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我宁愿现在离开。

templates.label: Comparison template
templates.template: I’d rather + 动词原形 + than + 动词原形.
slotHints: 两个动作都用原形；than 后不要加 to，也不要重复主语。
