templates.exampleSentence: I’d rather cook than eat out.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我宁愿做饭，也不愿在外面吃。

templates.label: Negative template
templates.template: I’d rather not + 动词原形.
slotHints: 表示“宁愿不……”；not 放在 rather 后、动词前。
templates.exampleSentence: I’d rather not talk about it.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我宁愿不谈这件事。

templates.label: Writing-oriented template
templates.template: Subject + would rather + 动词原形 + than + 动词原形.
slotHints: 主语可换成较正式的名词短语，如 students, residents, the company。
templates.exampleSentence: The company would rather reduce costs than raise prices.
templates.exampleTranslation: 这家公司宁愿降低成本，也不愿提高价格。

items.title: there is no point in + v-ing
items.pattern: there is no point in + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: there is no point in + V-ing 表示“做某事没有意义/没有用”。in 后面通常接动名词，不能直接接动词原形。它常放在句首作整体判断，也可接 doing sth 或 someone’s doing sth，后面常补充原因或结果。
items.usageNote: 语气多为中性，常用于给建议、讨论办法、争论某事是否值得继续做，口语和书面语都自然。它强调“做了也无帮助或无结果”，不适合只是表示“没必要”或礼貌劝阻的场景。通常写作 There is no point in ...，句首大写；后面若再说明原因，常接 because/if/when 从句或独立分句。初学者最安全的做法是直接用 there is no point in + 动名词短语。
sceneTags: 考试或工作中的策略建议

slotSchema.label: 无意义的动作
slotSchema.hint: 这里放动名词短语，如 arguing, waiting longer, trying again。常见错误是放动词原形：× in argue。要改成：in arguing。例：There is no point in waiting.

slotSchema.label: 动作对象/内容
slotSchema.hint: 可放在动名词后作宾语，如 discussing it, blaming him。常见错误是只写名词不配动名词：× in the problem。应说：in discussing the problem。例：There is no point in blaming him.

slotSchema.label: 原因或结果说明
slotSchema.hint: 可在后面补充 why，如 when nothing can change, because it’s over。常见错误是把从句直接放到 in 后：× in we can't win。应先用动名词，再补原因。例：There is no point in trying again if we can't win.

commonErrors: × There is no point in argue with him.｜错因：in 后不能接动词原形。｜改法：用动名词。√ There is no point in arguing with him.
× There is no point to argue with him.｜错因：这个固定搭配用 in，不用 to。｜改法：√ There is no point in arguing with him.
× It is no point in arguing.｜错因：主语框架错了；这里用 there is，不用 it is。｜改法：√ There is no point in arguing.
× There is no point in to wait longer.｜错因：in 后不能接 to do。｜改法：√ There is no point in waiting longer.
× There is not point in trying.｜错因：固定说法是 no point，不说 not point。｜改法：√ There is no point in trying.
× There is no point in we discuss this now.｜错因：in 后不能直接接完整句子。｜改法：√ There is no point in discussing this now. 或 √ There is no point in discussing this now because we're out of time.

contrasts.title: there is no need to + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 结构上：no point in + V-ing；no need to + 动词原形。语气上，no point 强调“做了也没用”，no need 强调“没必要做”。对比：There is no point in calling him now.（打了也没帮助）/ There is no need to call him now.（现在不必打）

contrasts.title: it is useless to + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 结构上：it is useless to do 与 there is no point in doing 都可表示“没用”。语气上，useless 更直接、更重；no point 更像理性判断。对比：It's useless to complain. / There's no point in complaining.

contrasts.title: it is a waste of time to / doing
contrasts.note: 结构上：waste of time 强调“浪费时间”，no point 强调“没有意义/帮助”。场景上，waste of time 更口语、带评价；no point 更常见于建议和分析。对比：It's a waste of time to argue. / There's no point in arguing.

contrasts.title: it's + adjective + to...
contrasts.note: 结构上：it's + 形容词 + to do 用来评价动作性质；there is no point in doing 专指“无意义”。场景上，前者范围很广，后者更聚焦结果无效。对比：It's hard to explain. / There's no point in explaining.

contrasts.title: there is / there are
contrasts.note: 结构上都用 there is 开头，但 there is no point in 是固定功能框架，不是在介绍“有某物”。不要类推成一般存在句。对比：There is a book on the desk. / There is no point in waiting.

examples.sentence: There is no point in arguing with him.
examples.translation: 和他争论没有意义。
examples.note: 最基础、最安全的核心句型。

examples.sentence: If they have already made the decision, there is no point in complaining.
examples.translation: 如果他们已经做了决定，抱怨也没有意义。
examples.note: 前面可先放条件状语，主句仍用该结构。

examples.sentence: There is no point in attempting to conceal the truth.
examples.translation: 试图掩盖真相是没有意义的。
examples.note: attempting 较书面，适合写作。

examples.sentence: There is no point in calling a taxi now; there is no need to rush.
examples.translation: 现在叫出租车没有意义；也没必要着急。
examples.note: 和 no need to 对比：前半句说没用，后半句说没必要。

examples.sentence: There was no point in waiting any longer, so we left.
examples.translation: 再等下去也没有意义，所以我们走了。
examples.note: 可变过去时，表示当时的判断。

examples.sentence: There is no point in your apologizing if you keep making the same mistake.
examples.translation: 如果你还一直犯同样的错，你道歉也没有意义。
examples.note: 可用 someone’s doing 强调动作执行者。

templates.label: 初学者最安全模板
slotHints: [action] 放动名词短语，如 waiting, arguing about it, trying again；不要用原形或 to do
templates.exampleSentence: There is no point in worrying.
templates.exampleTranslation: 担心也没有意义。

templates.label: 带条件的模板
templates.template: If [condition], there is no point in [action].
slotHints: [condition] 放一般情况或前提；[action] 仍用动名词，如 discussing it further
templates.exampleSentence: If nobody is listening, there is no point in explaining it again.
templates.exampleTranslation: 如果没人听，再解释一遍也没有意义。

templates.label: 写作用模板
templates.template: There is no point in [action], since [reason].
slotHints: [action] 放较正式的动名词短语，如 attempting to change the result；[reason] 说明为什么没用
templates.exampleSentence: There is no point in attempting to change the result, since the deadline has passed.
templates.exampleTranslation: 截止日期已过，试图改变结果已经没有意义。

templates.label: 强调执行者模板
slotHints: [person] 常用名词或所有格；[action] 用动名词，如 your apologizing, Tom's waiting
templates.exampleSentence: There is no point in your saying sorry now.
templates.exampleTranslation: 你现在道歉也没有意义。

items.title: can't help + v-ing
items.coreExplanation: can't help + 动词-ing 表示“忍不住、情不自禁地做某事”，强调反应自然发生、很难控制。后面通常接动作类动词的 -ing 形式，不接动词原形。它常放在主语后作谓语，如 I can't help laughing. 也常和 when/if 等时间原因内容连用。
items.usageNote: 语气中性，口语和一般书面语都很常见，适合表达笑、哭、担心、想起后产生的自然反应，也可用于叙述个人感受。它不适合表示“不会做”“没办法做”或“帮助不了某人”，那不是这个结构。can't help 后面直接接 v-ing，不要在中间随便加 to；句中多放在主语后。初学者最安全的写法是 I can't help + v-ing + when...
sceneTags: 看到某物后的自然反应

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 放人或事物，最常见是 I/we/he/she。常见错误是漏主语。例：I can't help laughing.

slotSchema.label: 忍不住发生的动作
slotSchema.hint: 放表示反应的动词-ing，如 laughing, smiling, worrying。常见错误是用原形 laugh。例：can't help smiling.

slotSchema.label: 触发情境
slotSchema.hint: 可放 when/if/because 引导的内容，说明为什么会这样。常见错误是把完整句子硬接在后面不连接。例：when I see old photos.

commonErrors: I can't help laugh.｜错在 help 后这里要接 v-ing，不接动词原形。｜改成 I can't help laughing.
I can't help to laugh.｜错在这个固定结构不用 to-infinitive。｜改成 I can't help laughing.
I can't help him laughing.｜错在这里会变成“我无法帮助他笑”，不是“我忍不住笑”。｜如果想说自己忍不住笑，用 I can't help laughing.
I can't help but laughing.｜错在把两个结构混用了；can't help + v-ing 或 can't help but + 动词原形 选一个。｜改成 I can't help laughing. / I can't help but laugh.
I can't help when I see him.｜错在缺少真正的反应动作，when 从句不能直接充当主要补足语。｜改成 I can't help smiling when I see him.
I can't help being late.｜常被误用来表达“我没办法不迟到”；但这个结构更像“忍不住……”，语义不自然。｜想说能力/情况问题，用 I can't avoid being late. 或 I can't help it if I'm late. 视语境改写。

contrasts.title: have trouble + v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构上两者都可接 v-ing，但 can't help + v-ing 是“情不自禁”，have trouble + v-ing 是“做起来有困难”。语气和场景不同：前者讲自然反应，后者讲能力问题。对比：I can't help laughing.（忍不住笑）/ I have trouble understanding him.（听懂他有困难）

contrasts.title: be used to + v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构都接 v-ing，但 be used to 表示“习惯于”，不是一时控制不住的反应。场景上前者多谈情绪反应，后者多谈长期习惯。对比：I'm used to getting up early.（习惯早起）/ I can't help yawning.（忍不住打哈欠）

contrasts.title: can't help but + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 两者意思接近，都可表示“忍不住”；结构不同：can't help + v-ing，更基础更稳妥；can't help but + 动词原形，稍偏书面或强调。初学者先优先用 v-ing。对比：I can't help smiling. / I can't help but smile.

contrasts.title: can't help it
contrasts.note: can't help it 是固定说法，表示“没办法、不是我能控制的”，后面通常不直接接动作；can't help + v-ing 才表示忍不住做某动作。场景上前者常作独立回应，后者常接具体行为。对比：Sorry, I can't help it. / I can't help laughing.

contrasts.title: help + 宾语 + 动词
contrasts.note: 普通 help 表示“帮助某人做某事”，和 can't help + v-ing 完全不是一个意思。结构上 help 后可接人和动词，can't help 后直接接 v-ing。最容易在写作时混淆。对比：I helped him carry the box. / I can't help crying.

examples.sentence: I can't help laughing.
examples.translation: 我忍不住笑。
examples.note: 最核心、最安全的基础句。

examples.sentence: When I see that photo, I can't help smiling.
examples.translation: 当我看到那张照片时，我就忍不住微笑。
examples.note: 展示常见位置：前面先放时间从句。

examples.sentence: She couldn't help crying after reading the letter.
examples.translation: 她看完那封信后忍不住哭了。
examples.note: 过去式 couldn't help 很常用。

examples.sentence: I have trouble remembering names, but I can't help forgetting his joke and laughing again.
examples.translation: 我记名字有困难，但一想到他的笑话还是会忍不住又笑出来。
examples.note: 与 have trouble 对比：前半是能力问题，后半是自然反应。

examples.sentence: He can't help worrying about his daughter.
examples.translation: 他忍不住为女儿担心。
examples.note: 可接心理活动类动词，但要是自然冒出的情绪。

examples.sentence: In such situations, people can't help feeling nervous.
examples.translation: 在这种情况下，人们往往会不由得感到紧张。
examples.note: 较偏说明性书面表达。

templates.label: 最安全基础模板
slotHints: 把 V-ing 换成 laughing/smiling/crying/worrying 等自然反应动作
templates.exampleSentence: I can't help smiling.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我忍不住微笑。

templates.label: 带触发情境模板
templates.template: I can't help + V-ing + when + 从句.
slotHints: V-ing 放反应动作；when 从句放触发它的情境
templates.exampleSentence: I can't help laughing when he tells that story.
templates.exampleTranslation: 他讲那个故事时，我就忍不住笑。

templates.label: 前置情境模板
templates.template: When + 从句, + 主语 + can't help + V-ing.
slotHints: 前面先写时间/条件情境，后面写谁忍不住做什么
templates.exampleSentence: When I hear this song, I can't help singing along.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我一听到这首歌，就忍不住跟着唱。

templates.label: 写作说明模板
templates.template: In/After + 情境, + 主语 + can't help + V-ing.
slotHints: 适合较书面的叙述；情境可用 in such situations / after hearing the news
templates.exampleSentence: After hearing the news, she couldn't help crying.
templates.exampleTranslation: 听到这个消息后，她忍不住哭了。

items.title: be used to + noun / v-ing
items.coreExplanation: be used to + 名词或动名词表示“已经习惯、觉得不陌生”。to 在这里是介词，所以后面不能接动词原形，要接名词、代词或 v-ing。常放在主语后作谓语：I am used to..., She isn't used to..., Are you used to...?
items.usageNote: 语气中性，最适合描述生活习惯、工作节奏、环境变化后的适应状态，如早起、通勤、吃辣、一个人生活。它强调“现在已经习惯”，不适合单纯说过去常常做某事，那要用 used to do。句中通常紧跟在 be 动词后；后面若接动作，必须写 v-ing，不要写原形。初学者最稳妥的写法是：主语 + be + used to + 名词/动名词。
sceneTags: 比较以前不习惯现在习惯了

slotSchema.label: 主语
slotSchema.hint: 放人或事物，如 I, she, my body。常见错误是漏掉 be 动词。例：I am used to... / She is used to...

slotSchema.label: be动词
slotSchema.hint: 按时态和主语变化：am/is/are/was/were。常见错误是一直用 be 原形。例：He is used to... / They are used to...

slotSchema.label: 习惯的对象或活动
slotSchema.hint: 可放名词、代词或动名词，不可放动词原形。常见错误：used to wake up；应为 used to waking up。例：used to the weather / used to driving at night

commonErrors: I am used to wake up early.｜错因：to 后面误接动词原形。｜改法：to 是介词，改成动名词：I am used to waking up early.
She used to working late.｜错因：少了 be 动词，而且 mixed with used to do。｜改法：现在已习惯要说 She is used to working late.
Are you used to live alone?｜错因：to 后面不能接 live 原形。｜改法：Are you used to living alone?
I get used to the cold now.｜错因：get used to 强调“逐渐适应”，和 now 连用想表达“已经习惯”不自然。｜改法：说状态用 I am used to the cold now.
He is used to do everything by himself.｜错因：把 be used to 与 be used to do 混了。｜改法：表示习惯：He is used to doing everything by himself.
I am very used to.｜错因：后面缺少具体对象，意思不完整。｜改法：补出名词或动名词，如 I am very used to this schedule.

contrasts.title: used to + 动词原形
contrasts.note: 结构不同：used to do 表示“过去常常，现在不了”；be used to + 名词/v-ing 表示“现在已经习惯”。语境上前者讲过去习惯，后者讲当前状态。对比：I used to drive to work.（以前常开车）/ I am used to driving to work.（现在已习惯开车上班）

contrasts.title: get used to + 名词 / v-ing
contrasts.note: 结构接近，但 get used to 强调“变得习惯、适应的过程”，be used to 强调“已经处于习惯状态”。语气上前者更像变化，后者更像结果。对比：I am getting used to the noise. / I am used to the noise.

contrasts.title: be used to + 动词原形（被用来）
contrasts.note: 表面相似但结构和意思完全不同。be used to do 里的 to 是不定式，表示“被用来做某事”；be used to doing 里的 to 是介词，表示“习惯于”。对比：This knife is used to cut bread. / I am used to cutting bread with this knife.

contrasts.title: can't help + v-ing
contrasts.note: can't help doing 表示“忍不住做”，不是“习惯做”。结构上没有 be，也没有介词 to。场景上前者讲情绪控制不住，后者讲长期适应。对比：I can't help checking my phone. / I am used to checking my phone.

examples.sentence: I am used to getting up early.
examples.translation: 我已经习惯早起了。
examples.note: 最安全的基础句型：be used to + 动名词。

examples.sentence: At first I hated the city, but now I am used to the noise.
examples.translation: 起初我讨厌这座城市，但现在我已经习惯这里的噪音了。
examples.note: 接名词也很常见，不一定要接动名词。

examples.sentence: Are you used to working with this software?
examples.translation: 你习惯使用这个软件工作了吗？
examples.note: 疑问句里 be 动词提前，后面仍然接 v-ing。

examples.sentence: Students are not used to writing formal reports in the first week.
examples.translation: 学生们在第一周通常还不习惯写正式报告。
examples.note: 较书面一点，适合学习或工作场景。

examples.sentence: I used to take the bus, but now I'm used to driving.
examples.translation: 我以前常坐公交，但现在我已经习惯开车了。
examples.note: 和 used to do 对比，最能看出两个结构的区别。

examples.sentence: She isn't used to spicy food yet.
examples.translation: 她还不习惯吃辣。
examples.note: 日常口语里常和 yet 连用，表示还没完全适应。

templates.label: 新手最稳模板
slotHints: 主语放人或事物
be 用 am/is/are 等
target 放名词或动名词，不能放动词原形
templates.exampleSentence: I am used to studying at night.
templates.exampleTranslation: 我已经习惯晚上学习了。

templates.label: 疑问句模板
slotHints: 把 be 动词提前
subject 常用 you/he/they
target 仍然用名词或动名词
templates.exampleSentence: Are you used to living alone?
templates.exampleTranslation: 你习惯一个人生活了吗？

templates.label: 书面表达模板
slotHints: 适合表达“还不习惯”
not 放在 be 后
yet 常放句末
templates.exampleSentence: Many new employees are not used to the schedule yet.
templates.exampleTranslation: 许多新员工还不习惯这个作息安排。
